1Kings4:8 And these are their names: The son of Hur
1Kings4:9 The son of Dekar
1Kings4:10 The son of Hesed
1Kings4:11 The son of Abinadab
1Kings4:12 Baana the son of Ahilud; to him pertained Taanach and Megiddo
1Kings4:13 The son of Geber
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab the son of Iddo had Mahanaim:
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz was in Naphtali; he also took Basmath the daughter of Solomon to wife:
1Kings4:16 Baanah the son of Hushai was in Asher and in Aloth:
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah
1Kings4:18 Shimei the son of Elah
1Kings4:19 Geber the son of Uri was in the country of Gilead
1Kings4:20 Judah and Israel were many
1Kings4:21 And Solomon reigned over all kingdoms from the river unto the land of the Philistines
1Kings4:22 And Solomon's provision for one day was thirty measures of fine flour
1Kings4:23 Ten fat oxen
1Kings4:24 For he had dominion over all the region on this side the river
1Kings4:25 And Judah and Israel dwelt safely
1Kings4:26 And Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots
1Kings4:27 And those officers provided victual for king Solomon
1Kings4:28 Barley also and straw for the horses and dromedaries brought they unto the place where the officers were
1Kings4:29 And God gave Solomon wisdom and understanding exceeding much
1Kings4:30 And Solomon's wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the children of the east country
1Kings4:31 For he was wiser than all men; than Ethan the Ezrahite
1Kings4:32 And he spake three thousand proverbs: and his songs were a thousand and five.
1Kings4:33 And he spake of trees
1Kings4:34 And there came of all people to hear the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings5:1 And Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants unto Solomon; for he had heard that they had anointed him king in the room of his father: for Hiram was ever a lover of David.
1Kings5:2 And Solomon sent to Hiram
1Kings5:3 Thou knowest how that David my father could not build an house unto the name of the LORD his God for the wars which were about him on every side
1Kings5:4 But now the LORD my God hath given me rest on every side
1Kings5:5 And
1Kings5:6 Now therefore command thou that they hew me cedar trees out of Lebanon; and my servants shall be with thy servants: and unto thee will I give hire for thy servants according to all that thou shalt appoint: for thou knowest that there is not among us any that can skill to hew timber like unto the Sidonians.
1Kings5:7 And it came to pass
1Kings5:8 And Hiram sent to Solomon
1Kings5:9 My servants shall bring them down from Lebanon unto the sea: and I will convey them by sea in floats unto the place that thou shalt appoint me
1Kings5:10 So Hiram gave Solomon cedar trees and fir trees according to all his desire.
1Kings5:11 And Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand measures of wheat for food to his household
1Kings5:12 And the LORD gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 And king Solomon raised a levy out of all Israel; and the levy was thirty thousand men.
1Kings5:14 And he sent them to Lebanon
1Kings5:15 And Solomon had threescore and ten thousand that bare burdens
1Kings5:16 Beside the chief of Solomon's officers which were over the work
1Kings5:17 And the king commanded
1Kings5:18 And Solomon's builders and Hiram's builders did hew them
1Kings6:1 And it came to pass in the four hundred and eightieth year after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 And the house which king Solomon built for the LORD
1Kings6:3 And the porch before the temple of the house
1Kings6:4 And for the house he made windows of narrow lights.
1Kings6:5 And against the wall of the house he built chambers round about
1Kings6:6 The nethermost chamber was five cubits broad
1Kings6:7 And the house
1Kings6:8 The door for the middle chamber was in the right side of the house: and they went up with winding stairs into the middle chamber
1Kings6:9 So he built the house
1Kings6:10 And then he built chambers against all the house
1Kings6:11 And the word of the LORD came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 Concerning this house which thou art in building
1Kings6:13 And I will dwell among the children of Israel
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house
1Kings6:15 And he built the walls of the house within with boards of cedar
1Kings6:16 And he built twenty cubits on the sides of the house
1Kings6:17 And the house
1Kings6:18 And the cedar of the house within was carved with knops and open flowers: all was cedar; there was no stone seen.
1Kings6:19 And the oracle he prepared in the house within
1Kings6:20 And the oracle in the forepart was twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:21 So Solomon overlaid the house within with pure gold: and he made a partition by the chains of gold before the oracle; and he overlaid it with gold.
1Kings6:22 And the whole house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:23 And within the oracle he made two cherubim of olive tree
1Kings6:24 And five cubits was the one wing of the cherub
1Kings6:25 And the other cherub was ten cubits: both the cherubim were of one measure and one size.
1Kings6:26 The height of the one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 And he set the cherubim within the inner house: and they stretched forth the wings of the cherubim
1Kings6:28 And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 And he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved figures of cherubim and palm trees and open flowers
1Kings6:30 And the floors of the house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:31 And for the entering of the oracle he made doors of olive tree: the lintel and side posts were a fifth part of the wall.
1Kings6:32 The two doors also were of olive tree; and he carved upon them carvings of cherubim and palm trees and open flowers
1Kings6:33 So also made he for the door of the temple posts of olive tree
1Kings6:34 And the two doors were of fir tree: the two leaves of the one door were folding
1Kings6:35 And he carved thereon cherubim and palm trees and open flowers: and covered them with gold fitted upon the carved work.
1Kings6:36 And he built the inner court with three rows of hewed stone
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year was the foundation of the house of the LORD laid
1Kings6:38 And in the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 But Solomon was building his own house thirteen years
1Kings7:2 He built also the house of the forest of Lebanon; the length thereof was an hundred cubits
1Kings7:3 And it was covered with cedar above upon the beams
1Kings7:4 And there were windows in three rows
1Kings7:5 And all the doors and posts were square
1Kings7:6 And he made a porch of pillars; the length thereof was fifty cubits
1Kings7:7 Then he made a porch for the throne where he might judge
1Kings7:8 And his house where he dwelt had another court within the porch
1Kings7:9 All these were of costly stones
1Kings7:10 And the foundation was of costly stones
1Kings7:11 And above were costly stones
1Kings7:12 And the great court round about was with three rows of hewed stones
1Kings7:13 And king Solomon sent and fetched Hiram out of Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was a widow's son of the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 For he cast two pillars of brass
1Kings7:16 And he made two chapiters of molten brass
1Kings7:17 And nets of checker work
1Kings7:18 And he made the pillars
1Kings7:19 And the chapiters that were upon the top of the pillars were of lily work in the porch
1Kings7:20 And the chapiters upon the two pillars had pomegranates also above
1Kings7:21 And he set up the pillars in the porch of the temple: and he set up the right pillar
1Kings7:22 And upon the top of the pillars was lily work: so was the work of the pillars finished.
1Kings7:23 And he made a molten sea
1Kings7:24 And under the brim of it round about there were knops compassing it
1Kings7:25 It stood upon twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 And it was an hand breadth thick
1Kings7:27 And he made ten bases of brass; four cubits was the length of one base
1Kings7:28 And the work of the bases was on this manner: they had borders
1Kings7:29 And on the borders that were between the ledges were lions
1Kings7:30 And every base had four brazen wheels
1Kings7:31 And the mouth of it within the chapiter and above was a cubit: but the mouth thereof was round after the work of the base
1Kings7:32 And under the borders were four wheels; and the axletrees of the wheels were joined to the base: and the height of a wheel was a cubit and half a cubit.
1Kings7:33 And the work of the wheels was like the work of a chariot wheel: their axletrees
1Kings7:34 And there were four undersetters to the four corners of one base: and the undersetters were of the very base itself.
1Kings7:35 And in the top of the base was there a round compass of half a cubit high: and on the top of the base the ledges thereof and the borders thereof were of the same.
1Kings7:36 For on the plates of the ledges thereof
1Kings7:37 After this manner he made the ten bases: all of them had one casting
1Kings7:38 Then made he ten lavers of brass: one laver contained forty baths: and every laver was four cubits: and upon every one of the ten bases one laver.
1Kings7:39 And he put five bases on the right side of the house
1Kings7:40 And Hiram made the lavers
1Kings7:41 The two pillars
1Kings7:42 And four hundred pomegranates for the two networks
1Kings7:43 And the ten bases
1Kings7:44 And one sea
1Kings7:45 And the pots
1Kings7:46 In the plain of Jordan did the king cast them
1Kings7:47 And Solomon left all the vessels unweighed
1Kings7:48 And Solomon made all the vessels that pertained unto the house of the LORD: the altar of gold
1Kings7:49 And the candlesticks of pure gold
1Kings7:50 And the bowls
1Kings7:51 So was ended all the work that king Solomon made for the house of the LORD. And Solomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated; even the silver
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel
1Kings8:2 And all the men of Israel assembled themselves unto king Solomon at the feast in the month Ethanim
1Kings8:3 And all the elders of Israel came
1Kings8:4 And they brought up the ark of the LORD
1Kings8:5 And king Solomon
1Kings8:6 And the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of the LORD unto his place
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread forth their two wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 And they drew out the staves
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark save the two tables of stone
1Kings8:10 And it came to pass
1Kings8:11 So that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud: for the glory of the LORD had filled the house of the LORD.
1Kings8:12 Then spake Solomon
1Kings8:13 I have surely built thee an house to dwell in
1Kings8:14 And the king turned his face about
1Kings8:15 And he said
1Kings8:16 Since the day that I brought forth my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 And it was in the heart of David my father to build an house for the name of the LORD God of Israel.
1Kings8:18 And the LORD said unto David my father
1Kings8:19 Nevertheless thou shalt not build the house; but thy son that shall come forth out of thy loins
1Kings8:20 And the LORD hath performed his word that he spake
1Kings8:21 And I have set there a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 And Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in the presence of all the congregation of Israel
1Kings8:23 And he said
1Kings8:24 Who hast kept with thy servant David my father that thou promisedst him: thou spakest also with thy mouth
1Kings8:25 Therefore now
1Kings8:26 And now
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed dwell on the earth? behold
1Kings8:28 Yet have thou respect unto the prayer of thy servant
1Kings8:29 That thine eyes may be open toward this house night and day
1Kings8:30 And hearken thou to the supplication of thy servant
1Kings8:31 If any man trespass against his neighbor
1Kings8:32 Then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:33 When thy people Israel be smitten down before the enemy
1Kings8:34 Then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:35 When heaven is shut up
1Kings8:36 Then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:37 If there be in the land famine
1Kings8:38 What prayer and supplication soever be made by any man
1Kings8:39 Then hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place
1Kings8:40 That they may fear thee all the days that they live in the land which thou gavest unto our fathers.
1Kings8:41 Moreover concerning a stranger
1Kings8:42 (For they shall hear of thy great name
1Kings8:43 Hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place
1Kings8:44 If thy people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 Then hear thou in heaven their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:46 If they sin against thee
1Kings8:47 Yet if they shall bethink themselves in the land whither they were carried captives
1Kings8:48 And so return unto thee with all their heart
1Kings8:49 Then hear thou their prayer and their supplication in heaven thy dwelling place
1Kings8:50 And forgive thy people that have sinned against thee
1Kings8:51 For they be thy people
1Kings8:52 That thine eyes may be open unto the supplication of thy servant
1Kings8:53 For thou didst separate them from among all the people of the earth
1Kings8:54 And it was so
1Kings8:55 And he stood
1Kings8:56 Blessed be the LORD
1Kings8:57 The LORD our God be with us
1Kings8:58 That he may incline our hearts unto him
1Kings8:59 And let these my words
1Kings8:60 That all the people of the earth may know that the LORD is God
1Kings8:61 Let your heart therefore be perfect with the LORD our God
1Kings8:62 And the king
1Kings8:63 And Solomon offered a sacrifice of peace offerings
1Kings8:64 The same day did the king hallow the middle of the court that was before the house of the LORD: for there he offered burnt offerings
1Kings8:65 And at that time Solomon held a feast
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away: and they blessed the king
1Kings9:1 And it came to pass
1Kings9:2 That the LORD appeared to Solomon the second time
1Kings9:3 And the LORD said unto him
1Kings9:4 And if thou wilt walk before me
1Kings9:5 Then I will establish the throne of thy kingdom upon Israel for ever
1Kings9:6 But if ye shall at all turn from following me
1Kings9:7 Then will I cut off Israel out of the land which I have given them; and this house
1Kings9:8 And at this house
1Kings9:9 And they shall answer
1Kings9:10 And it came to pass at the end of twenty years
1Kings9:11 (Now Hiram the king of Tyre had furnished Solomon with cedar trees and fir trees
1Kings9:12 And Hiram came out from Tyre to see the cities which Solomon had given him; and they pleased him not.
1Kings9:13 And he said
1Kings9:14 And Hiram sent to the king sixscore talents of gold.
1Kings9:15 And this is the reason of the levy which king Solomon raised; for to build the house of the LORD
1Kings9:16 For Pharaoh king of Egypt had gone up
1Kings9:17 And Solomon built Gezer
1Kings9:18 And Baalath
1Kings9:19 And all the cities of store that Solomon had
1Kings9:20 And all the people that were left of the Amorites
1Kings9:21 Their children that were left after them in the land
1Kings9:22 But of the children of Israel did Solomon make no bondmen: but they were men of war
1Kings9:23 These were the chief of the officers that were over Solomon's work
1Kings9:24 But Pharaoh's daughter came up out of the city of David unto her house which Solomon had built for her: then did he build Millo.
1Kings9:25 And three times in a year did Solomon offer burnt offerings and peace offerings upon the altar which he built unto the LORD
1Kings9:26 And king Solomon made a navy of ships in Eziongeber
1Kings9:27 And Hiram sent in the navy his servants
1Kings9:28 And they came to Ophir
1Kings10:1 And when the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon concerning the name of the LORD
1Kings10:2 And she came to Jerusalem with a very great train
1Kings10:3 And Solomon told her all her questions: there was not any thing hid from the king
1Kings10:4 And when the queen of Sheba had seen all Solomon's wisdom
1Kings10:5 And the meat of his table
1Kings10:6 And she said to the king
1Kings10:7 Howbeit I believed not the words
1Kings10:8 Happy are thy men
1Kings10:9 Blessed be the LORD thy God
1Kings10:10 And she gave the king an hundred and twenty talents of gold
1Kings10:11 And the navy also of Hiram
1Kings10:12 And the king made of the almug trees pillars for the house of the LORD
1Kings10:13 And king Solomon gave unto the queen of Sheba all her desire
1Kings10:14 Now the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred threescore and six talents of gold
1Kings10:15 Beside that he had of the merchantmen
1Kings10:16 And king Solomon made two hundred targets of beaten gold: six hundred shekels of gold went to one target.
1Kings10:17 And he made three hundred shields of beaten gold; three pound of gold went to one shield: and the king put them in the house of the forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 Moreover the king made a great throne of ivory
1Kings10:19 The throne had six steps
1Kings10:20 And twelve lions stood there on the one side and on the other upon the six steps: there was not the like made in any kingdom.
1Kings10:21 And all king Solomon's drinking vessels were of gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had at sea a navy of Tharshish with the navy of Hiram: once in three years came the navy of Tharshish
1Kings10:23 So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom.
1Kings10:24 And all the earth sought to Solomon
1Kings10:25 And they brought every man his present
1Kings10:26 And Solomon gathered together chariots and horsemen: and he had a thousand and four hundred chariots
1Kings10:27 And the king made silver to be in Jerusalem as stones
1Kings10:28 And Solomon had horses brought out of Egypt
1Kings10:29 And a chariot came up and went out of Egypt for six hundred shekels of silver
1Kings11:1 But king Solomon loved many strange women
1Kings11:2 Of the nations concerning which the LORD said unto the children of Israel
1Kings11:3 And he had seven hundred wives
1Kings11:4 For it came to pass
1Kings11:5 For Solomon went after Ashtoreth the goddess of the Zidonians
1Kings11:6 And Solomon did evil in the sight of the LORD
1Kings11:7 Then did Solomon build an high place for Chemosh
1Kings11:8 And likewise did he for all his strange wives
1Kings11:9 And the LORD was angry with Solomon
1Kings11:10 And had commanded him concerning this thing
1Kings11:11 Wherefore the LORD said unto Solomon
1Kings11:12 Notwithstanding in thy days I will not do it for David thy father's sake: but I will rend it out of the hand of thy son.
1Kings11:13 Howbeit I will not rend away all the kingdom; but will give one tribe to thy son for David my servant's sake
1Kings11:14 And the LORD stirred up an adversary unto Solomon
1Kings11:15 For it came to pass
1Kings11:16 (For six months did Joab remain there with all Israel
1Kings11:17 That Hadad fled
1Kings11:18 And they arose out of Midian
1Kings11:19 And Hadad found great favor in the sight of Pharaoh
1Kings11:20 And the sister of Tahpenes bare him Genubath his son
1Kings11:21 And when Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his fathers
1Kings11:22 Then Pharaoh said unto him
1Kings11:23 And God stirred him up another adversary
1Kings11:24 And he gathered men unto him
1Kings11:25 And he was an adversary to Israel all the days of Solomon
1Kings11:26 And Jeroboam the son of Nebat
1Kings11:27 And this was the cause that he lifted up his hand against the king: Solomon built Millo
1Kings11:28 And the man Jeroboam was a mighty man of valor: and Solomon seeing the young man that he was industrious
1Kings11:29 And it came to pass at that time when Jeroboam went out of Jerusalem
1Kings11:30 And Ahijah caught the new garment that was on him
1Kings11:31 And he said to Jeroboam
1Kings11:32 (But he shall have one tribe for my servant David's sake
1Kings11:33 Because that they have forsaken me
1Kings11:34 Howbeit I will not take the whole kingdom out of his hand: but I will make him prince all the days of his life for David my servant's sake
1Kings11:35 But I will take the kingdom out of his son's hand
1Kings11:36 And unto his son will I give one tribe
1Kings11:37 And I will take thee
1Kings11:38 And it shall be
1Kings11:39 And I will for this afflict the seed of David
1Kings11:40 Solomon sought therefore to kill Jeroboam. And Jeroboam arose
1Kings11:41 And the rest of the acts of Solomon
1Kings11:42 And the time that Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel was forty years.
1Kings11:43 And Solomon slept with his fathers
1Kings12:1 And Rehoboam went to Shechem: for all Israel were come to Shechem to make him king.
1Kings12:2 And it came to pass
1Kings12:3 That they sent and called him. And Jeroboam and all the congregation of Israel came
1Kings12:4 Thy father made our yoke grievous: now therefore make thou the grievous service of thy father
1Kings12:5 And he said unto them
1Kings12:6 And king Rehoboam consulted with the old men
1Kings12:7 And they spake unto him
1Kings12:8 But he forsook the counsel of the old men
1Kings12:9 And he said unto them
1Kings12:10 And the young men that were grown up with him spake unto him
1Kings12:11 And now whereas my father did lade you with a heavy yoke
1Kings12:12 So Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam the third day
1Kings12:13 And the king answered the people roughly
1Kings12:14 And spake to them after the counsel of the young men
1Kings12:15 Wherefore the king hearkened not unto the people; for the cause was from the LORD
1Kings12:16 So when all Israel saw that the king hearkened not unto them
1Kings12:17 But as for the children of Israel which dwelt in the cities of Judah
1Kings12:18 Then king Rehoboam sent Adoram
1Kings12:19 So Israel rebelled against the house of David unto this day.
1Kings12:20 And it came to pass
1Kings12:21 And when Rehoboam was come to Jerusalem
1Kings12:22 But the word of God came unto Shemaiah the man of God
1Kings12:23 Speak unto Rehoboam
1Kings12:24 Thus saith the LORD
1Kings12:25 Then Jeroboam built Shechem in mount Ephraim
1Kings12:26 And Jeroboam said in his heart
1Kings12:27 If this people go up to do sacrifice in the house of the LORD at Jerusalem
1Kings12:28 Whereupon the king took counsel
1Kings12:29 And he set the one in Bethel
1Kings12:30 And this thing became a sin: for the people went to worship before the one
1Kings12:31 And he made an house of high places
1Kings12:32 And Jeroboam ordained a feast in the eighth month
1Kings12:33 So he offered upon the altar which he had made in Bethel the fifteenth day of the eighth month
1Kings13:1 And
1Kings13:2 And he cried against the altar in the word of the LORD
1Kings13:3 And he gave a sign the same day
1Kings13:4 And it came to pass
1Kings13:5 The altar also was rent
1Kings13:6 And the king answered and said unto the man of God
1Kings13:7 And the king said unto the man of God
1Kings13:8 And the man of God said unto the king
1Kings13:9 For so was it charged me by the word of the LORD
1Kings13:10 So he went another way
1Kings13:11 Now there dwelt an old prophet in Bethel; and his sons came and told him all the works that the man of God had done that day in Bethel: the words which he had spoken unto the king
1Kings13:12 And their father said unto them
1Kings13:13 And he said unto his sons
1Kings13:14 And went after the man of God
1Kings13:15 Then he said unto him
1Kings13:16 And he said
1Kings13:17 For it was said to me by the word of the LORD
1Kings13:18 He said unto him
1Kings13:19 So he went back with him
1Kings13:20 And it came to pass
1Kings13:21 And he cried unto the man of God that came from Judah
1Kings13:22 But camest back
1Kings13:23 And it came to pass
1Kings13:24 And when he was gone
1Kings13:25 And
1Kings13:26 And when the prophet that brought him back from the way heard thereof
1Kings13:27 And he spake to his sons
1Kings13:28 And he went and found his carcass cast in the way
1Kings13:29 And the prophet took up the carcass of the man of God
1Kings13:30 And he laid his carcass in his own grave; and they mourned over him
1Kings13:31 And it came to pass
1Kings13:32 For the saying which he cried by the word of the LORD against the altar in Bethel
1Kings13:33 After this thing Jeroboam returned not from his evil way
1Kings13:34 And this thing became sin unto the house of Jeroboam
1Kings14:1 At that time Abijah the son of Jeroboam fell sick.
1Kings14:2 And Jeroboam said to his wife
1Kings14:3 And take with thee ten loaves
1Kings14:4 And Jeroboam's wife did so
1Kings14:5 And the LORD said unto Ahijah
1Kings14:6 And it was so
1Kings14:7 Go
1Kings14:8 And rent the kingdom away from the house of David
1Kings14:9 But hast done evil above all that were before thee: for thou hast gone and made thee other gods
1Kings14:10 Therefore
1Kings14:11 Him that dieth of Jeroboam in the city shall the dogs eat; and him that dieth in the field shall the fowls of the air eat: for the LORD hath spoken it.
1Kings14:12 Arise thou therefore
1Kings14:13 And all Israel shall mourn for him
1Kings14:14 Moreover the LORD shall raise him up a king over Israel
1Kings14:15 For the LORD shall smite Israel
1Kings14:16 And he shall give Israel up because of the sins of Jeroboam
1Kings14:17 And Jeroboam's wife arose
1Kings14:18 And they buried him; and all Israel mourned for him
1Kings14:19 And the rest of the acts of Jeroboam
1Kings14:20 And the days which Jeroboam reigned were two and twenty years: and he slept with his fathers
1Kings14:21 And Rehoboam the son of Solomon reigned in Judah. Rehoboam was forty and one years old when he began to reign
1Kings14:22 And Judah did evil in the sight of the LORD
1Kings14:23 For they also built them high places
1Kings14:24 And there were also sodomites in the land: and they did according to all the abominations of the nations which the LORD cast out before the children of Israel.
1Kings14:25 And it came to pass in the fifth year of king Rehoboam
1Kings14:26 And he took away the treasures of the house of the LORD
1Kings14:27 And king Rehoboam made in their stead brazen shields
1Kings14:28 And it was so
1Kings14:29 Now the rest of the acts of Rehoboam
1Kings14:30 And there was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all their days.
1Kings14:31 And Rehoboam slept with his fathers
1Kings15:1 Now in the eighteenth year of king Jeroboam the son of Nebat reigned Abijam over Judah.
1Kings15:2 Three years reigned he in Jerusalem. and his mother's name was Maachah
1Kings15:3 And he walked in all the sins of his father
1Kings15:4 Nevertheless for David's sake did the LORD his God give him a lamp in Jerusalem
1Kings15:5 Because David did that which was right in the eyes of the LORD
1Kings15:6 And there was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all the days of his life.
1Kings15:7 Now the rest of the acts of Abijam
1Kings15:8 And Abijam slept with his fathers; and they buried him in the city of David: and Asa his son reigned in his stead.
1Kings15:9 And in the twentieth year of Jeroboam king of Israel reigned Asa over Judah.
1Kings15:10 And forty and one years reigned he in Jerusalem. And his mother's name was Maachah
1Kings15:11 And Asa did that which was right in the eyes of the LORD
1Kings15:12 And he took away the sodomites out of the land

1Kings15:21 And it came to pass

2Kings19:13 Where is the king of Hamath
2Kings19:14 And Hezekiah received the letter of the hand of the messengers
2Kings19:15 And Hezekiah prayed before the LORD
2Kings19:16 LORD
2Kings19:17 Of a truth
2Kings19:18 And have cast their gods into the fire: for they were no gods
2Kings19:19 Now therefore
2Kings19:20 Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah
2Kings19:21 This is the word that the LORD hath spoken concerning him; The virgin the daughter of Zion hath despised thee
2Kings19:22 Whom hast thou reproached and blasphemed? and against whom hast thou exalted thy voice
2Kings19:23 By thy messengers thou hast reproached the LORD
2Kings19:24 I have digged and drunk strange waters
2Kings19:25 Hast thou not heard long ago how I have done it
2Kings19:26 Therefore their inhabitants were of small power
2Kings19:27 But I know thy abode
2Kings19:28 Because thy rage against me and thy tumult is come up into mine ears
2Kings19:29 And this shall be a sign unto thee
2Kings19:30 And the remnant that is escaped of the house of Judah shall yet again take root downward
2Kings19:31 For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant
2Kings19:32 Therefore thus saith the LORD concerning the king of Assyria

2Kings19:34 For I will defend this city
2Kings19:35 And it came to pass that night

2Kings19:37 And it came to pass
2Kings20:1 In those days was Hezekiah sick unto death. And the prophet Isaiah the son of Amoz came to him

2Kings20:4 And it came to pass
2Kings20:5 Turn again
2Kings20:6 And I will add unto thy days fifteen years; and I will deliver thee and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria; and I will defend this city for mine own sake

2Kings21:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign
2Kings21:2 And he did that which was evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings21:3 For he built up again the high places which Hezekiah his father had destroyed; and he reared up altars for Baal
2Kings21:4 And he built altars in the house of the LORD
2Kings21:5 And he built altars for all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of the LORD.
2Kings21:6 And he made his son pass through the fire
2Kings21:7 And he set a graven image of the grove that he had made in the house
2Kings21:8 Neither will I make the feet of Israel move any more out of the land which I gave their fathers; only if they will observe to do according to all that I have commanded them
2Kings21:9 But they hearkened not: and Manasseh seduced them to do more evil than did the nations whom the LORD destroyed before the children of Israel.
2Kings21:10 And the LORD spake by his servants the prophets
2Kings21:11 Because Manasseh king of Judah hath done these abominations
2Kings21:12 Therefore thus saith the LORD God of Israel
2Kings21:13 And I will stretch over Jerusalem the line of Samaria
2Kings21:14 And I will forsake the remnant of mine inheritance
2Kings21:15 Because they have done that which was evil in my sight
2Kings21:16 Moreover Manasseh shed innocent blood very much
2Kings21:17 Now the rest of the acts of Manasseh
2Kings21:18 And Manasseh slept with his fathers
2Kings21:19 Amon was twenty and two years old when he began to reign

2Kings21:23 And the servants of Amon conspired against him
2Kings21:24 And the people of the land slew all them that had conspired against king Amon; and the people of the land made Josiah his son king in his stead.
2Kings21:25 Now the rest of the acts of Amon which he did
2Kings21:26 And he was buried in his sepulchre in the garden of Uzza: and Josiah his son reigned in his stead.

2Chronicles30:23 And the whole assembly took counsel to keep other seven days: and they kept other seven days with gladness.

Nehemiah9:1 Now in the twenty and fourth day of this month the children of Israel were assembled with fasting

Nehemiah9:6 Thou
Nehemiah9:7 Thou art the LORD the God
Nehemiah9:8 And foundest his heart faithful before thee
Nehemiah9:9 And didst see the affliction of our fathers in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 And shewedst signs and wonders upon Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 And thou didst divide the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 Moreover thou leddest them in the day by a cloudy pillar; and in the night by a pillar of fire
Nehemiah9:13 Thou camest down also upon mount Sinai
Nehemiah9:14 And madest known unto them thy holy sabbath
Nehemiah9:15 And gavest them bread from heaven for their hunger
Nehemiah9:16 But they and our fathers dealt proudly
Nehemiah9:17 And refused to obey
Nehemiah9:18 Yea
Nehemiah9:19 Yet thou in thy manifold mercies forsookest them not in the wilderness: the pillar of the cloud departed not from them by day
Nehemiah9:20 Thou gavest also thy good spirit to instruct them
Nehemiah9:21 Yea
Nehemiah9:22 Moreover thou gavest them kingdoms and nations
Nehemiah9:23 Their children also multipliedst thou as the stars of heaven
Nehemiah9:24 So the children went in and possessed the land
Nehemiah9:25 And they took strong cities
Nehemiah9:26 Nevertheless they were disobedient
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore thou deliveredst them into the hand of their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 But after they had rest
Nehemiah9:29 And testifiedst against them
Nehemiah9:30 Yet many years didst thou forbear them
Nehemiah9:31 Nevertheless for thy great mercies' sake thou didst not utterly consume them
Nehemiah9:32 Now therefore
Nehemiah9:33 Howbeit thou art just in all that is brought upon us; for thou hast done right
Nehemiah9:34 Neither have our kings
Nehemiah9:35 For they have not served thee in their kingdom

Nehemiah9:38 And because of all this we make a sure covenant

Esther1:2 That in those days

Esther1:11 To bring Vashti the queen before the king with the crown royal

Esther1:20 And when the king's decree which he shall make shall be published throughout all his empire
Esther1:21 And the saying pleased the king and the princes; and the king did according to the word of Memucan:

Job1:19 And

Job2:12 And when they lifted up their eyes afar off

Job3:1 After this opened Job his mouth
Job3:2 And Job spake
Job3:3 Let the day perish wherein I was born
Job3:4 Let that day be darkness; let not God regard it from above
Job3:5 Let darkness and the shadow of death stain it; let a cloud dwell upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.
Job3:6 As for that night
Job3:7 Lo
Job3:8 Let them curse it that curse the day
Job3:9 Let the stars of the twilight thereof be dark; let it look for light
Job3:10 Because it shut not up the doors of my mother's womb
Job3:11 Why died I not from the womb? why did I not give up the ghost when I came out of the belly?
Job3:12 Why did the knees prevent me? or why the breasts that I should suck?
Job3:13 For now should I have lain still and been quiet
Job3:14 With kings and counsellors of the earth
Job3:15 Or with princes that had gold
Job3:16 Or as an hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants which never saw light.
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the weary be at rest.
Job3:18 There the prisoners rest together; they hear not the voice of the oppressor.
Job3:19 The small and great are there; and the servant is free from his master.
Job3:20 Wherefore is light given to him that is in misery
Job3:21 Which long for death
Job3:22 Which rejoice exceedingly
Job3:23 Why is light given to a man whose way is hid
Job3:24 For my sighing cometh before I eat
Job3:25 For the thing which I greatly feared is come upon me
Job3:26 I was not in safety
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said
Job4:2 If we assay to commune with thee
Job4:3 Behold
Job4:4 Thy words have upholden him that was falling
Job4:5 But now it is come upon thee
Job4:6 Is not this thy fear
Job4:7 Remember
Job4:8 Even as I have seen
Job4:9 By the blast of God they perish
Job4:10 The roaring of the lion
Job4:11 The old lion perisheth for lack of prey
Job4:12 Now a thing was secretly brought to me
Job4:13 In thoughts from the visions of the night
Job4:14 Fear came upon me
Job4:15 Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up:
Job4:16 It stood still
Job4:17 Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?
Job4:18 Behold
Job4:19 How much less in them that dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding it.
Job4:21 Doth not their excellency which is in them go away? they die
Job5:1 Call now
Job5:2 For wrath killeth the foolish man
Job5:3 I have seen the foolish taking root: but suddenly I cursed his habitation.
Job5:4 His children are far from safety
Job5:5 Whose harvest the hungry eateth up
Job5:6 Although affliction cometh not forth of the dust
Job5:7 Yet man is born unto trouble
Job5:8 I would seek unto God
Job5:9 Which doeth great things and unsearchable; marvellous things without number:
Job5:10 Who giveth rain upon the earth
Job5:11 To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.
Job5:12 He disappointeth the devices of the crafty
Job5:13 He taketh the wise in their own craftiness: and the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in the day time
Job5:15 But he saveth the poor from the sword
Job5:16 So the poor hath hope
Job5:17 Behold
Job5:18 For he maketh sore
Job5:19 He shall deliver thee in six troubles: yea
Job5:20 In famine he shall redeem thee from death: and in war from the power of the sword.
Job5:21 Thou shalt be hid from the scourge of the tongue: neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.
Job5:22 At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh: neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.
Job5:23 For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field: and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.
Job5:24 And thou shalt know that thy tabernacle shall be in peace; and thou shalt visit thy habitation
Job5:25 Thou shalt know also that thy seed shall be great
Job5:26 Thou shalt come to thy grave in a full age
Job5:27 Lo this
Job6:1 But Job answered and said
Job6:2 Oh that my grief were throughly weighed
Job6:3 For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea: therefore my words are swallowed up.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are within me
Job6:5 Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or loweth the ox over his fodder?
Job6:6 Can that which is unsavoury be eaten without salt? or is there any taste in the white of an egg?
Job6:7 The things that my soul refused to touch are as my sorrowful meat.
Job6:8 Oh that I might have my request; and that God would grant me the thing that I long for!
Job6:9 Even that it would please God to destroy me; that he would let loose his hand
Job6:10 Then should I yet have comfort; yea
Job6:11 What is my strength
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones? or is my flesh of brass?
Job6:13 Is not my help in me? and is wisdom driven quite from me?
Job6:14 To him that is afflicted pity should be shewed from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.
Job6:15 My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook
Job6:16 Which are blackish by reason of the ice
Job6:17 What time they wax warm
Job6:18 The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing
Job6:19 The troops of Tema looked
Job6:20 They were confounded because they had hoped; they came thither
Job6:21 For now ye are nothing; ye see my casting down
Job6:22 Did I say
Job6:23 Or
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How forcible are right words! but what doth your arguing reprove?
Job6:26 Do ye imagine to reprove words
Job6:27 Yea
Job6:28 Now therefore be content
Job6:29 Return
Job6:30 Is there iniquity in my tongue? cannot my taste discern perverse things?
Job7:1 Is there not an appointed time to man upon earth? are not his days also like the days of an hireling?
Job7:2 As a servant earnestly desireth the shadow
Job7:3 So am I made to possess months of vanity
Job7:4 When I lie down
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle
Job7:7 O remember that my life is wind: mine eye shall no more see good.
Job7:8 The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no more: thine eyes are upon me
Job7:9 As the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away: so he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no more.
Job7:10 He shall return no more to his house
Job7:11 Therefore I will not refrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I a sea
Job7:13 When I say
Job7:14 Then thou scarest me with dreams
Job7:15 So that my soul chooseth strangling
Job7:16 I loathe it; I would not live alway: let me alone; for my days are vanity.
Job7:17 What is man
Job7:18 And that thou shouldest visit him every morning
Job7:19 How long wilt thou not depart from me
Job7:20 I have sinned; what shall I do unto thee
Job7:21 And why dost thou not pardon my transgression
Job8:1 Then answered Bildad the Shuhite
Job8:2 How long wilt thou speak these things? and how long shall the words of thy mouth be like a strong wind?
Job8:3 Doth God pervert judgment? or doth the Almighty pervert justice?
Job8:4 If thy children have sinned against him
Job8:5 If thou wouldest seek unto God betimes
Job8:6 If thou wert pure and upright; surely now he would awake for thee
Job8:7 Though thy beginning was small
Job8:8 For enquire
Job8:9 (For we are but of yesterday
Job8:10 Shall not they teach thee
Job8:11 Can the rush grow up without mire? can the flag grow without water?
Job8:12 Whilst it is yet in his greenness
Job8:13 So are the paths of all that forget God; and the hypocrite's hope shall perish:
Job8:14 Whose hope shall be cut off
Job8:15 He shall lean upon his house
Job8:16 He is green before the sun
Job8:17 His roots are wrapped about the heap
Job8:18 If he destroy him from his place
Job8:19 Behold
Job8:20 Behold
Job8:21 Till he fill thy mouth with laughing
Job8:22 They that hate thee shall be clothed with shame; and the dwelling place of the wicked shall come to nought.
Job9:1 Then Job answered and said
Job9:2 I know it is so of a truth: but how should man be just with God?
Job9:3 If he will contend with him
Job9:4 He is wise in heart
Job9:5 Which removeth the mountains
Job9:6 Which shaketh the earth out of her place
Job9:7 Which commandeth the sun
Job9:8 Which alone spreadeth out the heavens
Job9:9 Which maketh Arcturus
Job9:10 Which doeth great things past finding out; yea
Job9:11 Lo
Job9:12 Behold
Job9:13 If God will not withdraw his anger
Job9:14 How much less shall I answer him
Job9:15 Whom
Job9:16 If I had called
Job9:17 For he breaketh me with a tempest
Job9:18 He will not suffer me to take my breath
Job9:19 If I speak of strength
Job9:20 If I justify myself
Job9:21 Though I were perfect
Job9:22 This is one thing
Job9:23 If the scourge slay suddenly
Job9:24 The earth is given into the hand of the wicked: he covereth the faces of the judges thereof; if not
Job9:25 Now my days are swifter than a post: they flee away
Job9:26 They are passed away as the swift ships: as the eagle that hasteth to the prey.
Job9:27 If I say
Job9:28 I am afraid of all my sorrows
Job9:29 If I be wicked
Job9:30 If I wash myself with snow water
Job9:31 Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch
Job9:32 For he is not a man
Job9:33 Neither is there any daysman betwixt us
Job9:34 Let him take his rod away from me
Job9:35 Then would I speak
Job10:1 My soul is weary of my life; I will leave my complaint upon myself; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say unto God
Job10:3 Is it good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress
Job10:4 Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?
Job10:5 Are thy days as the days of man? are thy years as man's days
Job10:6 That thou enquirest after mine iniquity
Job10:7 Thou knowest that I am not wicked; and there is none that can deliver out of thine hand.
Job10:8 Thine hands have made me and fashioned me together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.
Job10:9 Remember
Job10:10 Hast thou not poured me out as milk
Job10:11 Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 Thou hast granted me life and favour
Job10:13 And these things hast thou hid in thine heart: I know that this is with thee.
Job10:14 If I sin
Job10:15 If I be wicked
Job10:16 For it increaseth. Thou huntest me as a fierce lion: and again thou shewest thyself marvellous upon me.
Job10:17 Thou renewest thy witnesses against me
Job10:18 Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? Oh that I had given up the ghost
Job10:19 I should have been as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.
Job10:20 Are not my days few? cease then
Job10:21 Before I go whence I shall not return
Job10:22 A land of darkness
Job11:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite
Job11:2 Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?
Job11:3 Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest
Job11:4 For thou hast said
Job11:5 But oh that God would speak
Job11:6 And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom
Job11:7 Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?
Job11:8 It is as high as heaven; what canst thou do? deeper than hell; what canst thou know?
Job11:9 The measure thereof is longer than the earth
Job11:10 If he cut off
Job11:11 For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?
Job11:12 For vain men would be wise
Job11:13 If thou prepare thine heart
Job11:14 If iniquity be in thine hand
Job11:15 For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea
Job11:16 Because thou shalt forget thy misery
Job11:17 And thine age shall be clearer than the noonday: thou shalt shine forth
Job11:18 And thou shalt be secure
Job11:19 Also thou shalt lie down
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked shall fail
Job12:1 And Job answered and said
Job12:2 No doubt but ye are the people
Job12:3 But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: yea
Job12:4 I am as one mocked of his neighbour
Job12:5 He that is ready to slip with his feet is as a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.
Job12:6 The tabernacles of robbers prosper
Job12:7 But ask now the beasts
Job12:8 Or speak to the earth
Job12:9 Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the LORD hath wrought this?
Job12:10 In whose hand is the soul of every living thing
Job12:11 Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat?
Job12:12 With the ancient is wisdom; and in length of days understanding.
Job12:13 With him is wisdom and strength
Job12:14 Behold
Job12:15 Behold
Job12:16 With him is strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver are his.
Job12:17 He leadeth counsellors away spoiled
Job12:18 He looseth the bond of kings
Job12:19 He leadeth princes away spoiled
Job12:20 He removeth away the speech of the trusty
Job12:21 He poureth contempt upon princes
Job12:22 He discovereth deep things out of darkness
Job12:23 He increaseth the nations
Job12:24 He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth
Job12:25 They grope in the dark without light
Job13:1 Lo
Job13:2 What ye know
Job13:3 Surely I would speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 But ye are forgers of lies
Job13:5 O that ye would altogether hold your peace! and it should be your wisdom.
Job13:6 Hear now my reasoning
Job13:7 Will ye speak wickedly for God? and talk deceitfully for him?
Job13:8 Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?
Job13:9 Is it good that he should search you out? or as one man mocketh another
Job13:10 He will surely reprove you
Job13:11 Shall not his excellency make you afraid? and his dread fall upon you?
Job13:12 Your remembrances are like unto ashes
Job13:13 Hold your peace
Job13:14 Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth
Job13:15 Though he slay me
Job13:16 He also shall be my salvation: for an hypocrite shall not come before him.
Job13:17 Hear diligently my speech
Job13:18 Behold now
Job13:19 Who is he that will plead with me? for now
Job13:20 Only do not two things unto me: then will I not hide myself from thee.
Job13:21 Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.
Job13:22 Then call thou
Job13:23 How many are mine iniquities and sins? make me to know my transgression and my sin.

Job13:25 Wilt thou break a leaf driven to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?
Job13:26 For thou writest bitter things against me
Job13:27 Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks

Job14:1 Man that is born of a woman is of few days and full of trouble.
Job14:2 He cometh forth like a flower
Job14:3 And doth thou open thine eyes upon such an one
Job14:4 Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one.
Job14:5 Seeing his days are determined
Job14:6 Turn from him
Job14:7 For there is hope of a tree
Job14:8 Though the root thereof wax old in the earth
Job14:9 Yet through the scent of water it will bud
Job14:10 But man dieth
Job14:11 As the waters fail from the sea
Job14:12 So man lieth down
Job14:13 O that thou wouldest hide me in the grave
Job14:14 If a man die
Job14:15 Thou shalt call
Job14:16 For now thou numberest my steps: dost thou not watch over my sin?
Job14:17 My transgression is sealed up in a bag
Job14:18 And surely the mountains falling cometh to nought
Job14:19 The waters wear the stones: thou washest away the things which grow out of the dust of the earth; and thou destroyest the hope of man.
Job14:20 Thou prevailest for ever against him
Job14:21 His sons come to honour
Job14:22 But his flesh upon him shall have pain
Job15:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite
Job15:2 Should a wise man utter vain knowledge
Job15:3 Should he reason with unprofitable talk? or with speeches wherewith he can do no good?
Job15:4 Yea
Job15:5 For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity
Job15:6 Thine own mouth condemneth thee
Job15:7 Art thou the first man that was born? or wast thou made before the hills?
Job15:8 Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?
Job15:9 What knowest thou
Job15:10 With us are both the grayheaded and very aged men
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God small with thee? is there any secret thing with thee?
Job15:12 Why doth thine heart carry thee away? and what do thy eyes wink at
Job15:13 That thou turnest thy spirit against God
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 Behold
Job15:16 How much more abominable and filthy is man
Job15:17 I will shew thee
Job15:18 Which wise men have told from their fathers
Job15:19 Unto whom alone the earth was given
Job15:20 The wicked man travaileth with pain all his days
Job15:21 A dreadful sound is in his ears: in prosperity the destroyer shall come upon him.
Job15:22 He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness
Job15:23 He wandereth abroad for bread
Job15:24 Trouble and anguish shall make him afraid; they shall prevail against him
Job15:25 For he stretcheth out his hand against God
Job15:26 He runneth upon him
Job15:27 Because he covereth his face with his fatness
Job15:28 And he dwelleth in desolate cities
Job15:29 He shall not be rich
Job15:30 He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches
Job15:31 Let not him that is deceived trust in vanity: for vanity shall be his recompence.
Job15:32 It shall be accomplished before his time
Job15:33 He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine
Job15:34 For the congregation of hypocrites shall be desolate
Job15:35 They conceive mischief
Job16:1 Then Job answered and said
Job16:2 I have heard many such things: miserable comforters are ye all.
Job16:3 Shall vain words have an end? or what emboldeneth thee that thou answerest?
Job16:4 I also could speak as ye do: if your soul were in my soul's stead
Job16:5 But I would strengthen you with my mouth
Job16:6 Though I speak
Job16:7 But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate all my company.
Job16:8 And thou hast filled me with wrinkles
Job16:9 He teareth me in his wrath
Job16:10 They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.
Job16:11 God hath delivered me to the ungodly
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 His archers compass me round about
Job16:14 He breaketh me with breach upon breach
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin
Job16:16 My face is foul with weeping
Job16:17 Not for any injustice in mine hands: also my prayer is pure.
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 Also now
Job16:20 My friends scorn me: but mine eye poureth out tears unto God.
Job16:21 O that one might plead for a man with God

Job17:2 Are there not mockers with me? and doth not mine eye continue in their provocation?
Job17:3 Lay down now
Job17:4 For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt them.
Job17:5 He that speaketh flattery to his friends
Job17:6 He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret.
Job17:7 Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow
Job17:8 Upright men shall be astonied at this
Job17:9 The righteous also shall hold on his way

Job17:12 They change the night into day: the light is short because of darkness.

Job17:16 They shall go down to the bars of the pit

Job18:5 Yea
Job18:6 The light shall be dark in his tabernacle
Job18:7 The steps of his strength shall be straitened
Job18:8 For he is cast into a net by his own feet
Job18:9 The gin shall take him by the heel
Job18:10 The snare is laid for him in the ground
Job18:11 Terrors shall make him afraid on every side
Job18:12 His strength shall be hungerbitten
Job18:13 It shall devour the strength of his skin: even the firstborn of death shall devour his strength.

Job18:15 It shall dwell in his tabernacle
Job18:16 His roots shall be dried up beneath

Job18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked
Job19:1 Then Job answered and said
Job19:2 How long will ye vex my soul
Job19:3 These ten times have ye reproached me: ye are not ashamed that ye make yourselves strange to me.
Job19:4 And be it indeed that I have erred
Job19:5 If indeed ye will magnify yourselves against me
Job19:6 Know now that God hath overthrown me
Job19:7 Behold
Job19:8 He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass
Job19:9 He hath stripped me of my glory
Job19:10 He hath destroyed me on every side
Job19:11 He hath also kindled his wrath against me
Job19:12 His troops come together
Job19:13 He hath put my brethren far from me
Job19:14 My kinsfolk have failed
Job19:15 They that dwell in mine house
Job19:16 I called my servant
Job19:17 My breath is strange to my wife
Job19:18 Yea
Job19:19 All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.
Job19:20 My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh
Job19:21 Have pity upon me
Job19:22 Why do ye persecute me as God
Job19:23 Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book!

Job19:25 For I know that my redeemer liveth
Job19:26 And though after my skin worms destroy this body
Job19:27 Whom I shall see for myself
Job19:28 But ye should say
Job19:29 Be ye afraid of the sword: for wrath bringeth the punishments of the sword
Job20:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite
Job20:2 Therefore do my thoughts cause me to answer
Job20:3 I have heard the check of my reproach
Job20:4 Knowest thou not this of old
Job20:5 That the triumphing of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Though his excellency mount up to the heavens
Job20:7 Yet he shall perish for ever like his own dung: they which have seen him shall say
Job20:8 He shall fly away as a dream
Job20:9 The eye also which saw him shall see him no more; neither shall his place any more behold him.
Job20:10 His children shall seek to please the poor
Job20:11 His bones are full of the sin of his youth
Job20:12 Though wickedness be sweet in his mouth
Job20:13 Though he spare it
Job20:14 Yet his meat in his bowels is turned
Job20:15 He hath swallowed down riches
Job20:16 He shall suck the poison of asps: the viper's tongue shall slay him.
Job20:17 He shall not see the rivers
Job20:18 That which he laboured for shall he restore
Job20:19 Because he hath oppressed and hath forsaken the poor; because he hath violently taken away an house which he builded not;
Job20:20 Surely he shall not feel quietness in his belly
Job20:21 There shall none of his meat be left; therefore shall no man look for his goods.
Job20:22 In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits: every hand of the wicked shall come upon him.
Job20:23 When he is about to fill his belly
Job20:24 He shall flee from the iron weapon
Job20:25 It is drawn
Job20:26 All darkness shall be hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.
Job20:27 The heaven shall reveal his iniquity; and the earth shall rise up against him.
Job20:28 The increase of his house shall depart
Job20:29 This is the portion of a wicked man from God

Job21:3 Suffer me that I may speak; and after that I have spoken
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Mark me

Job21:7 Wherefore do the wicked live
Job21:8 Their seed is established in their sight with them
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 Their bull gendereth
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like a flock
Job21:12 They take the timbrel and harp
Job21:13 They spend their days in wealth
Job21:14 Therefore they say unto God
Job21:15 What is the Almighty

Job21:18 They are as stubble before the wind
Job21:19 God layeth up his iniquity for his children: he rewardeth him
Job21:20 His eyes shall see his destruction

Job21:23 One dieth in his full strength
Job21:24 His breasts are full of milk
Job21:25 And another dieth in the bitterness of his soul

Job21:28 For ye say

Job21:32 Yet shall he be brought to the grave
Job21:33 The clods of the valley shall be sweet unto him
Job21:34 How then comfort ye me in vain
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said
Job22:2 Can a man be profitable unto God
Job22:3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty
Job22:4 Will he reprove thee for fear of thee? will he enter with thee into judgment?
Job22:5 Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities infinite?
Job22:6 For thou hast taken a pledge from thy brother for nought
Job22:7 Thou hast not given water to the weary to drink
Job22:8 But as for the mighty man
Job22:9 Thou hast sent widows away empty
Job22:10 Therefore snares are round about thee
Job22:11 Or darkness
Job22:12 Is not God in the height of heaven? and behold the height of the stars
Job22:13 And thou sayest
Job22:14 Thick clouds are a covering to him
Job22:15 Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?
Job22:16 Which were cut down out of time
Job22:17 Which said unto God

Job22:19 The righteous see it
Job22:20 Whereas our substance is not cut down
Job22:21 Acquaint now thyself with him
Job22:22 Receive
Job22:23 If thou return to the Almighty
Job22:24 Then shalt thou lay up gold as dust
