Job22:26 For then shalt thou have thy delight in the Almighty
Job22:27 Thou shalt make thy prayer unto him
Job22:28 Thou shalt also decree a thing
Job22:29 When men are cast down
Job22:30 He shall deliver the island of the innocent: and it is delivered by the pureness of thine hands.
Job23:1 Then Job answered and said
Job23:2 Even to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh that I knew where I might find him! that I might come even to his seat!
Job23:4 I would order my cause before him
Job23:5 I would know the words which he would answer me
Job23:6 Will he plead against me with his great power? No; but he would put strength in me.
Job23:7 There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.
Job23:8 Behold
Job23:9 On the left hand
Job23:10 But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me
Job23:11 My foot hath held his steps
Job23:12 Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.
Job23:13 But he is in one mind
Job23:14 For he performeth the thing that is appointed for me: and many such things are with him.
Job23:15 Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider
Job23:16 For God maketh my heart soft
Job23:17 Because I was not cut off before the darkness
Job24:1 Why
Job24:2 Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks
Job24:3 They drive away the ass of the fatherless
Job24:4 They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together.
Job24:5 Behold
Job24:6 They reap every one his corn in the field: and they gather the vintage of the wicked.
Job24:7 They cause the naked to lodge without clothing
Job24:8 They are wet with the showers of the mountains
Job24:9 They pluck the fatherless from the breast
Job24:10 They cause him to go naked without clothing
Job24:11 Which make oil within their walls
Job24:12 Men groan from out of the city
Job24:13 They are of those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof
Job24:14 The murderer rising with the light killeth the poor and needy
Job24:15 The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they dig through houses
Job24:17 For the morning is to them even as the shadow of death: if one know them
Job24:18 He is swift as the waters; their portion is cursed in the earth: he beholdeth not the way of the vineyards.
Job24:19 Drought and heat consume the snow waters: so doth the grave those which have sinned.
Job24:20 The womb shall forget him; the worm shall feed sweetly on him; he shall be no more remembered; and wickedness shall be broken as a tree.
Job24:21 He evil entreateth the barren that beareth not: and doeth not good to the widow.
Job24:22 He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth up
Job24:23 Though it be given him to be in safety
Job24:24 They are exalted for a little while
Job24:25 And if it be not so now
Job25:1 Then answered Bildad the Shuhite
Job25:2 Dominion and fear are with him
Job25:3 Is there any number of his armies? and upon whom doth not his light arise?
Job25:4 How then can man be justified with God? or how can he be clean that is born of a woman?
Job25:5 Behold even to the moon
Job25:6 How much less man
Job26:1 But Job answered and said
Job26:2 How hast thou helped him that is without power? how savest thou the arm that hath no strength?
Job26:3 How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?
Job26:4 To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
Job26:5 Dead things are formed from under the waters
Job26:6 Hell is naked before him
Job26:7 He stretcheth out the north over the empty place
Job26:8 He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.
Job26:9 He holdeth back the face of his throne
Job26:10 He hath compassed the waters with bounds
Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.
Job26:12 He divideth the sea with his power
Job26:13 By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.
Job26:14 Lo
Job27:1 Moreover Job continued his parable
Job27:2 As God liveth
Job27:3 All the while my breath is in me
Job27:4 My lips shall not speak wickedness
Job27:5 God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.
Job27:6 My righteousness I hold fast
Job27:7 Let mine enemy be as the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the hypocrite
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?
Job27:10 Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?
Job27:11 I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 This is the portion of a wicked man with God
Job27:14 If his children be multiplied
Job27:15 Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.
Job27:16 Though he heap up silver as the dust
Job27:17 He may prepare it
Job27:18 He buildeth his house as a moth
Job27:19 The rich man shall lie down
Job27:20 Terrors take hold on him as waters
Job27:21 The east wind carrieth him away
Job27:22 For God shall cast upon him
Job27:23 Men shall clap their hands at him
Job28:1 Surely there is a vein for the silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken out of the earth
Job28:3 He setteth an end to darkness
Job28:4 The flood breaketh out from the inhabitant; even the waters forgotten of the foot: they are dried up
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 The stones of it are the place of sapphires: and it hath dust of gold.
Job28:7 There is a path which no fowl knoweth
Job28:8 The lion's whelps have not trodden it
Job28:9 He putteth forth his hand upon the rock; he overturneth the mountains by the roots.
Job28:10 He cutteth out rivers among the rocks; and his eye seeth every precious thing.
Job28:11 He bindeth the floods from overflowing; and the thing that is hid bringeth he forth to light.
Job28:12 But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man knoweth not the price thereof; neither is it found in the land of the living.
Job28:14 The depth saith
Job28:15 It cannot be gotten for gold
Job28:16 It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 The gold and the crystal cannot equal it: and the exchange of it shall not be for jewels of fine gold.
Job28:18 No mention shall be made of coral
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it
Job28:20 Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living
Job28:22 Destruction and death say
Job28:23 God understandeth the way thereof
Job28:24 For he looketh to the ends of the earth
Job28:25 To make the weight for the winds; and he weigheth the waters by measure.
Job28:26 When he made a decree for the rain
Job28:27 Then did he see it
Job28:28 And unto man he said
Job29:1 Moreover Job continued his parable
Job29:2 Oh that I were as in months past
Job29:3 When his candle shined upon my head
Job29:4 As I was in the days of my youth
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me
Job29:6 When I washed my steps with butter
Job29:7 When I went out to the gate through the city
Job29:8 The young men saw me
Job29:9 The princes refrained talking
Job29:10 The nobles held their peace
Job29:11 When the ear heard me
Job29:12 Because I delivered the poor that cried
Job29:13 The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me: and I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.
Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind
Job29:16 I was a father to the poor: and the cause which I knew not I searched out.
Job29:17 And I brake the jaws of the wicked
Job29:18 Then I said
Job29:19 My root was spread out by the waters
Job29:20 My glory was fresh in me
Job29:21 Unto me men gave ear
Job29:22 After my words they spake not again; and my speech dropped upon them.
Job29:23 And they waited for me as for the rain; and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.
Job29:24 If I laughed on them
Job29:25 I chose out their way
Job30:1 But now they that are younger than I have me in derision
Job30:2 Yea
Job30:3 For want and famine they were solitary; fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste.
Job30:4 Who cut up mallows by the bushes
Job30:5 They were driven forth from among men
Job30:6 To dwell in the cliffs of the valleys
Job30:7 Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.
Job30:8 They were children of fools
Job30:9 And now am I their song
Job30:10 They abhor me
Job30:11 Because he hath loosed my cord
Job30:12 Upon my right hand rise the youth; they push away my feet
Job30:13 They mar my path
Job30:14 They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.
Job30:16 And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.
Job30:17 My bones are pierced in me in the night season: and my sinews take no rest.
Job30:18 By the great force of my disease is my garment changed: it bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.
Job30:19 He hath cast me into the mire
Job30:20 I cry unto thee
Job30:21 Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.
Job30:22 Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to ride upon it
Job30:23 For I know that thou wilt bring me to death
Job30:24 Howbeit he will not stretch out his hand to the grave
Job30:25 Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was not my soul grieved for the poor?
Job30:26 When I looked for good
Job30:27 My bowels boiled
Job30:28 I went mourning without the sun: I stood up
Job30:29 I am a brother to dragons
Job30:30 My skin is black upon me
Job30:31 My harp also is turned to mourning
Job31:1 I made a covenant with mine eyes; why then should I think upon a maid?
Job31:2 For what portion of God is there from above? and what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?
Job31:3 Is not destruction to the wicked? and a strange punishment to the workers of iniquity?
Job31:4 Doth not he see my ways
Job31:5 If I have walked with vanity
Job31:6 Let me be weighed in an even balance that God may know mine integrity.
Job31:7 If my step hath turned out of the way
Job31:8 Then let me sow
Job31:9 If mine heart have been deceived by a woman
Job31:10 Then let my wife grind unto another
Job31:11 For this is an heinous crime; yea
Job31:12 For it is a fire that consumeth to destruction
Job31:13 If I did despise the cause of my manservant or of my maidservant
Job31:14 What then shall I do when God riseth up? and when he visiteth
Job31:15 Did not he that made me in the womb make him? and did not one fashion us in the womb?
Job31:16 If I have withheld the poor from their desire
Job31:17 Or have eaten my morsel myself alone
Job31:18 (For from my youth he was brought up with me
Job31:19 If I have seen any perish for want of clothing
Job31:20 If his loins have not blessed me
Job31:21 If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless
Job31:22 Then let mine arm fall from my shoulder blade
Job31:23 For destruction from God was a terror to me
Job31:24 If I have made gold my hope
Job31:25 If I rejoice because my wealth was great
Job31:26 If I beheld the sun when it shined
Job31:27 And my heart hath been secretly enticed
Job31:28 This also were an iniquity to be punished by the judge: for I should have denied the God that is above.
Job31:29 If I rejoice at the destruction of him that hated me
Job31:30 Neither have I suffered my mouth to sin by wishing a curse to his soul.
Job31:31 If the men of my tabernacle said not
Job31:32 The stranger did not lodge in the street: but I opened my doors to the traveller.
Job31:33 If I covered my transgressions as Adam
Job31:34 Did I fear a great multitude
Job31:35 Oh that one would hear me! behold
Job31:36 Surely I would take it upon my shoulder
Job31:37 I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I go near unto him.
Job31:38 If my land cry against me
Job31:39 If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money
Job31:40 Let thistles grow instead of wheat
Job32:1 So these three men ceased to answer Job
Job32:2 Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite
Job32:3 Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled
Job32:4 Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken
Job32:5 When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men
Job32:6 And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said
Job32:7 I said
Job32:8 But there is a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.
Job32:9 Great men are not always wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.
Job32:10 Therefore I said
Job32:11 Behold
Job32:12 Yea
Job32:13 Lest ye should say
Job32:14 Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.
Job32:15 They were amazed
Job32:16 When I had waited
Job32:17 I said
Job32:18 For I am full of matter
Job32:19 Behold
Job32:20 I will speak
Job32:21 Let me not
Job32:22 For I know not to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away.
Job33:1 Wherefore
Job33:2 Behold
Job33:3 My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.
Job33:4 The spirit of God hath made me
Job33:5 If thou canst answer me
Job33:6 Behold
Job33:7 Behold
Job33:8 Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing
Job33:9 I am clean without transgression
Job33:10 Behold
Job33:11 He putteth my feet in the stocks
Job33:12 Behold
Job33:13 Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.
Job33:14 For God speaketh once
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 Then he openeth the ears of men
Job33:17 That he may withdraw man from his purpose
Job33:18 He keepeth back his soul from the pit
Job33:19 He is chastened also with pain upon his bed
Job33:20 So that his life abhorreth bread
Job33:21 His flesh is consumed away
Job33:22 Yea
Job33:23 If there be a messenger with him
Job33:24 Then he is gracious unto him
Job33:25 His flesh shall be fresher than a child's: he shall return to the days of his youth:
Job33:26 He shall pray unto God
Job33:27 He looketh upon men
Job33:28 He will deliver his soul from going into the pit
Job33:29 Lo
Job33:30 To bring back his soul from the pit
Job33:31 Mark well
Job33:32 If thou hast anything to say
Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Furthermore Elihu answered and said
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 For the ear trieth words
Job34:4 Let us choose to us judgment: let us know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 For Job hath said
Job34:6 Should I lie against my right? my wound is incurable without transgression.
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 Which goeth in company with the workers of iniquity
Job34:9 For he hath said
Job34:10 Therefore hearken unto me ye men of understanding: far be it from God
Job34:11 For the work of a man shall he render unto him
Job34:12 Yea
Job34:13 Who hath given him a charge over the earth? or who hath disposed the whole world?
Job34:14 If he set his heart upon man
Job34:15 All flesh shall perish together
Job34:16 If now thou hast understanding
Job34:17 Shall even he that hateth right govern? and wilt thou condemn him that is most just?
Job34:18 Is it fit to say to a king
Job34:19 How much less to him that accepteth not the persons of princes
Job34:20 In a moment shall they die
Job34:21 For his eyes are upon the ways of man
Job34:22 There is no darkness
Job34:23 For he will not lay upon man more than right; that he should enter into judgment with God.
Job34:24 He shall break in pieces mighty men without number
Job34:25 Therefore he knoweth their works
Job34:26 He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others;
Job34:27 Because they turned back from him
Job34:28 So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him
Job34:29 When he giveth quietness
Job34:30 That the hypocrite reign not
Job34:31 Surely it is meet to be said unto God
Job34:32 That which I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity
Job34:33 Should it be according to thy mind? he will recompense it
Job34:34 Let men of understanding tell me
Job34:35 Job hath spoken without knowledge
Job34:36 My desire is that Job may be tried unto the end because of his answers for wicked men.
Job34:37 For he addeth rebellion unto his sin
Job35:1 Elihu spake moreover
Job35:2 Thinkest thou this to be right
Job35:3 For thou saidst
Job35:4 I will answer thee
Job35:5 Look unto the heavens
Job35:6 If thou sinnest
Job35:7 If thou be righteous
Job35:8 Thy wickedness may hurt a man as thou art; and thy righteousness may profit the son of man.
Job35:9 By reason of the multitude of oppressions they make the oppressed to cry: they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty.
Job35:10 But none saith
Job35:11 Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth
Job35:12 There they cry
Job35:13 Surely God will not hear vanity
Job35:14 Although thou sayest thou shalt not see him
Job35:15 But now
Job35:16 Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.
Job36:1 Elihu also proceeded
Job36:2 Suffer me a little
Job36:3 I will fetch my knowledge from afar
Job36:4 For truly my words shall not be false: he that is perfect in knowledge is with thee.
Job36:5 Behold
Job36:6 He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.
Job36:7 He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous: but with kings are they on the throne; yea
Job36:8 And if they be bound in fetters
Job36:9 Then he sheweth them their work
Job36:10 He openeth also their ear to discipline
Job36:11 If they obey and serve him
Job36:12 But if they obey not
Job36:13 But the hypocrites in heart heap up wrath: they cry not when he bindeth them.
Job36:14 They die in youth
Job36:15 He delivereth the poor in his affliction
Job36:16 Even so would he have removed thee out of the strait into a broad place
Job36:17 But thou hast fulfilled the judgment of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold on thee.
Job36:18 Because there is wrath
Job36:19 Will he esteem thy riches? no
Job36:20 Desire not the night
Job36:21 Take heed
Job36:22 Behold
Job36:23 Who hath enjoined him his way? or who can say
Job36:24 Remember that thou magnify his work
Job36:25 Every man may see it; man may behold it afar off.
Job36:26 Behold
Job36:27 For he maketh small the drops of water: they pour down rain according to the vapour thereof:
Job36:28 Which the clouds do drop and distil upon man abundantly.
Job36:29 Also can any understand the spreadings of the clouds
Job36:30 Behold
Job36:31 For by them judgeth he the people; he giveth meat in abundance.
Job36:32 With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.
Job36:33 The noise thereof sheweth concerning it
Job37:1 At this also my heart trembleth
Job37:2 Hear attentively the noise of his voice
Job37:3 He directeth it under the whole heaven
Job37:4 After it a voice roareth: he thundereth with the voice of his excellency; and he will not stay them when his voice is heard.
Job37:5 God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he
Job37:6 For he saith to the snow
Job37:7 He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.
Job37:8 Then the beasts go into dens
Job37:9 Out of the south cometh the whirlwind: and cold out of the north.
Job37:10 By the breath of God frost is given: and the breadth of the waters is straitened.
Job37:11 Also by watering he wearieth the thick cloud: he scattereth his bright cloud:
Job37:12 And it is turned round about by his counsels: that they may do whatsoever he commandeth them upon the face of the world in the earth.
Job37:13 He causeth it to come
Job37:14 Hearken unto this
Job37:15 Dost thou know when God disposed them
Job37:16 Dost thou know the balancings of the clouds
Job37:17 How thy garments are warm
Job37:18 Hast thou with him spread out the sky
Job37:19 Teach us what we shall say unto him; for we cannot order our speech by reason of darkness.
Job37:20 Shall it be told him that I speak? if a man speak
Job37:21 And now men see not the bright light which is in the clouds: but the wind passeth
Job37:22 Fair weather cometh out of the north: with God is terrible majesty.
Job37:23 Touching the Almighty
Job37:24 Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any that are wise of heart.
Job38:1 Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind
Job38:2 Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Gird up now thy loins like a man; for I will demand of thee
Job38:4 Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? declare
Job38:5 Who hath laid the measures thereof
Job38:6 Whereupon are the foundations thereof fastened? or who laid the corner stone thereof;
Job38:7 When the morning stars sang together
Job38:8 Or who shut up the sea with doors
Job38:9 When I made the cloud the garment thereof
Job38:10 And brake up for it my decreed place
Job38:11 And said
Job38:12 Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days; and caused the dayspring to know his place;
Job38:13 That it might take hold of the ends of the earth
Job38:14 It is turned as clay to the seal; and they stand as a garment.
Job38:15 And from the wicked their light is withholden
Job38:16 Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been opened unto thee? or hast thou seen the doors of the shadow of death?
Job38:18 Hast thou perceived the breadth of the earth? declare if thou knowest it all.
Job38:19 Where is the way where light dwelleth? and as for darkness
Job38:20 That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof
Job38:21 Knowest thou it
Job38:22 Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the treasures of the hail
Job38:23 Which I have reserved against the time of trouble
Job38:24 By what way is the light parted
Job38:25 Who hath divided a watercourse for the overflowing of waters
Job38:26 To cause it to rain on the earth
Job38:27 To satisfy the desolate and waste ground; and to cause the bud of the tender herb to spring forth?
Job38:28 Hath the rain a father? or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 Out of whose womb came the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven
Job38:30 The waters are hid as with a stone
Job38:31 Canst thou bind the sweet influences of Pleiades
Job38:32 Canst thou bring forth Mazzaroth in his season? or canst thou guide Arcturus with his sons?
Job38:33 Knowest thou the ordinances of heaven? canst thou set the dominion thereof in the earth?
Job38:34 Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Canst thou send lightnings
Job38:36 Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the heart?
Job38:37 Who can number the clouds in wisdom? or who can stay the bottles of heaven
Job38:38 When the dust groweth into hardness
Job38:39 Wilt thou hunt the prey for the lion? or fill the appetite of the young lions
Job38:40 When they couch in their dens
Job38:41 Who provideth for the raven his food? when his young ones cry unto God
Job39:1 Knowest thou the time when the wild goats of the rock bring forth? or canst thou mark when the hinds do calve?
Job39:2 Canst thou number the months that they fulfil? or knowest thou the time when they bring forth?
Job39:3 They bow themselves
Job39:4 Their young ones are in good liking
Job39:5 Who hath sent out the wild ass free? or who hath loosed the bands of the wild ass?
Job39:6 Whose house I have made the wilderness
Job39:7 He scorneth the multitude of the city
Job39:8 The range of the mountains is his pasture
Job39:9 Will the unicorn be willing to serve thee
Job39:10 Canst thou bind the unicorn with his band in the furrow? or will he harrow the valleys after thee?
Job39:11 Wilt thou trust him
Job39:12 Wilt thou believe him
Job39:13 Gavest thou the goodly wings unto the peacocks? or wings and feathers unto the ostrich?
Job39:14 Which leaveth her eggs in the earth
Job39:15 And forgetteth that the foot may crush them
Job39:16 She is hardened against her young ones
Job39:17 Because God hath deprived her of wisdom
Job39:18 What time she lifteth up herself on high
Job39:19 Hast thou given the horse strength? hast thou clothed his neck with thunder?
Job39:20 Canst thou make him afraid as a grasshopper? the glory of his nostrils is terrible.
Job39:21 He paweth in the valley
Job39:22 He mocketh at fear
Job39:23 The quiver rattleth against him
Job39:24 He swalloweth the ground with fierceness and rage: neither believeth he that it is the sound of the trumpet.
Job39:25 He saith among the trumpets
Job39:26 Doth the hawk fly by thy wisdom
Job39:27 Doth the eagle mount up at thy command
Job39:28 She dwelleth and abideth on the rock
Job39:29 From thence she seeketh the prey
Job39:30 Her young ones also suck up blood: and where the slain are
Job40:1 Moreover the LORD answered Job
Job40:2 Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God
Job40:3 Then Job answered the LORD
Job40:4 Behold
Job40:5 Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea
Job40:6 Then answered the LORD unto Job out of the whirlwind
Job40:7 Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee
Job40:8 Wilt thou also disannul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me
Job40:9 Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?
Job40:10 Deck thyself now with majesty and excellency; and array thyself with glory and beauty.
Job40:11 Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one that is proud
Job40:12 Look on every one that is proud
Job40:13 Hide them in the dust together; and bind their faces in secret.
Job40:14 Then will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee.
Job40:15 Behold now behemoth
Job40:16 Lo now
Job40:17 He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.
Job40:18 His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.
Job40:19 He is the chief of the ways of God: he that made him can make his sword to approach unto him.
Job40:20 Surely the mountains bring him forth food
Job40:21 He lieth under the shady trees
Job40:22 The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.
Job40:23 Behold
Job40:24 He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.
Job41:1 Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? or his tongue with a cord which thou lettest down?
Job41:2 Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?
Job41:3 Will he make many supplications unto thee? will he speak soft words unto thee?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him for a servant for ever?
Job41:5 Wilt thou play with him as with a bird? or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?
Job41:6 Shall the companions make a banquet of him? shall they part him among the merchants?
Job41:7 Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?
Job41:8 Lay thine hand upon him
Job41:9 Behold
Job41:10 None is so fierce that dare stir him up: who then is able to stand before me?
Job41:11 Who hath prevented me
Job41:12 I will not conceal his parts
Job41:13 Who can discover the face of his garment? or who can come to him with his double bridle?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of his face? his teeth are terrible round about.
Job41:15 His scales are his pride
Job41:16 One is so near to another
Job41:17 They are joined one to another
Job41:18 By his neesings a light doth shine
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go burning lamps
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils goeth smoke
Job41:21 His breath kindleth coals
Job41:22 In his neck remaineth strength
Job41:23 The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.
Job41:24 His heart is as firm as a stone; yea
Job41:25 When he raiseth up himself
Job41:26 The sword of him that layeth at him cannot hold: the spear
Job41:27 He esteemeth iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into stubble.
Job41:29 Darts are counted as stubble: he laugheth at the shaking of a spear.
Job41:30 Sharp stones are under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.
Job41:31 He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment.
Job41:32 He maketh a path to shine after him; one would think the deep to be hoary.
Job41:33 Upon earth there is not his like
Job41:34 He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD
Job42:2 I know that thou canst do every thing
Job42:3 Who is he that hideth counsel without knowledge? therefore have I uttered that I understood not; things too wonderful for me
Job42:4 Hear
Job42:5 I have heard of thee by the hearing of the ear: but now mine eye seeth thee.
Job42:6 Wherefore I abhor myself
Job42:7 And it was so
Job42:8 Therefore take unto you now seven bullocks and seven rams
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went
Job42:10 And the LORD turned the captivity of Job
Job42:11 Then came there unto him all his brethren
Job42:12 So the LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: for he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 He had also seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 And he called the name of the first
Job42:15 And in all the land were no women found so fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.
Job42:16 After this lived Job an hundred and forty years
Job42:17 So Job died
Psalms1:1 Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly
Psalms1:2 But his delight is in the law of the LORD; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.
Psalms1:3 And he shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water
Psalms1:4 The ungodly are not so: but are like the chaff which the wind driveth away.
Psalms1:5 Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment
Psalms1:6 For the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.

Psalms18:2 The LORD is my rock
Psalms18:3 I will call upon the LORD
Psalms18:4 The sorrows of death compassed me
Psalms18:5 The sorrows of hell compassed me about: the snares of death prevented me.
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon the LORD
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations also of the hills moved and were shaken
Psalms18:8 There went up a smoke out of his nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens also
Psalms18:10 And he rode upon a cherub
Psalms18:11 He made darkness his secret place; his pavilion round about him were dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
Psalms18:12 At the brightness that was before him his thick clouds passed
Psalms18:13 The LORD also thundered in the heavens
Psalms18:14 Yea
Psalms18:15 Then the channels of waters were seen
Psalms18:16 He sent from above
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy
Psalms18:18 They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the LORD was my stay.
Psalms18:19 He brought me forth also into a large place; he delivered me
Psalms18:20 The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD
Psalms18:22 For all his judgments were before me
Psalms18:23 I was also upright before him
Psalms18:24 Therefore hath the LORD recompensed me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful; with an upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright;
Psalms18:26 With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself froward.
Psalms18:27 For thou wilt save the afflicted people; but wilt bring down high looks.
Psalms18:28 For thou wilt light my candle: the LORD my God will enlighten my darkness.
Psalms18:29 For by thee I have run through a troop; and by my God have I leaped over a wall.
Psalms18:30 As for God
Psalms18:31 For who is God save the LORD? or who is a rock save our God?
Psalms18:32 It is God that girdeth me with strength
Psalms18:33 He maketh my feet like hinds' feet
Psalms18:34 He teacheth my hands to war
Psalms18:35 Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up
Psalms18:36 Thou hast enlarged my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I have pursued mine enemies
Psalms18:38 I have wounded them that they were not able to rise: they are fallen under my feet.
Psalms18:39 For thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle: thou hast subdued under me those that rose up against me.
Psalms18:40 Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies; that I might destroy them that hate me.
Psalms18:41 They cried
Psalms18:42 Then did I beat them small as the dust before the wind: I did cast them out as the dirt in the streets.
Psalms18:43 Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; and thou hast made me the head of the heathen: a people whom I have not known shall serve me.
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear of me
Psalms18:45 The strangers shall fade away
Psalms18:46 The LORD liveth; and blessed be my rock; and let the God of my salvation be exalted.
Psalms18:47 It is God that avengeth me
Psalms18:48 He delivereth me from mine enemies: yea
Psalms18:49 Therefore will I give thanks unto thee
Psalms18:50 Great deliverance giveth he to his king; and sheweth mercy to his anointed

Psalms22:2 O my God
Psalms22:3 But thou art holy
Psalms22:4 Our fathers trusted in thee: they trusted
Psalms22:5 They cried unto thee
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm
Psalms22:7 All they that see me laugh me to scorn: they shoot out the lip
Psalms22:8 He trusted on the LORD that he would deliver him: let him deliver him
Psalms22:9 But thou art he that took me out of the womb: thou didst make me hope when I was upon my mother's breasts.
Psalms22:10 I was cast upon thee from the womb: thou art my God from my mother's belly.
Psalms22:11 Be not far from me; for trouble is near; for there is none to help.
Psalms22:12 Many bulls have compassed me: strong bulls of Bashan have beset me round.
Psalms22:13 They gaped upon me with their mouths
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
Psalms22:16 For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.
Psalms22:17 I may tell all my bones: they look and stare upon me.
Psalms22:18 They part my garments among them
Psalms22:19 But be not thou far from me
Psalms22:20 Deliver my soul from the sword; my darling from the power of the dog.
Psalms22:21 Save me from the lion's mouth: for thou hast heard me from the horns of the unicorns.
Psalms22:22 I will declare thy name unto my brethren: in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.

Psalms22:26 The meek shall eat and be satisfied: they shall praise the LORD that seek him: your heart shall live for ever.
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the world shall remember and turn unto the LORD: and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before thee.
Psalms22:28 For the kingdom is the LORD's: and he is the governor among the nations.

Psalms31:3 For thou art my rock and my fortress; therefore for thy name's sake lead me

Psalms31:5 Into thine hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me
Psalms31:6 I have hated them that regard lying vanities: but I trust in the LORD.
Psalms31:7 I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;
Psalms31:8 And hast not shut me up into the hand of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large room.
Psalms31:9 Have mercy upon me
Psalms31:10 For my life is spent with grief
Psalms31:11 I was a reproach among all mine enemies
Psalms31:12 I am forgotten as a dead man out of mind: I am like a broken vessel.

Psalms56:6 They gather themselves together

Psalms56:9 When I cry unto thee
Psalms56:10 In God will I praise his word: in the LORD will I praise his word.
Psalms56:11 In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
Psalms56:12 Thy vows are upon me
Psalms56:13 For thou hast delivered my soul from death: wilt not thou deliver my feet from falling

Psalms59:7 Behold
Psalms59:8 But thou

Psalms60:5 That thy beloved may be delivered; save with thy right hand

Psalms60:8 Moab is my washpot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe: Philistia

Psalms60:10 Wilt not thou
Psalms60:11 Give us help from trouble: for vain is the help of man.
Psalms60:12 Through God we shall do valiantly: for he it is that shall tread down our enemies.

Psalms69:1 Save me
Psalms69:2 I sink in deep mire
Psalms69:3 I am weary of my crying: my throat is dried: mine eyes fail while I wait for my God.
Psalms69:4 They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of mine head: they that would destroy me
Psalms69:5 O God
Psalms69:6 Let not them that wait on thee
Psalms69:7 Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; shame hath covered my face.
Psalms69:8 I am become a stranger unto my brethren
Psalms69:9 For the zeal of thine house hath eaten me up; and the reproaches of them that reproached thee are fallen upon me.
Psalms69:10 When I wept
Psalms69:11 I made sackcloth also my garment; and I became a proverb to them.
Psalms69:12 They that sit in the gate speak against me; and I was the song of the drunkards.
Psalms69:13 But as for me

Psalms69:15 Let not the waterflood overflow me

Psalms69:18 Draw nigh unto my soul
Psalms69:19 Thou hast known my reproach
Psalms69:20 Reproach hath broken my heart; and I am full of heaviness: and I looked for some to take pity
Psalms69:21 They gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
Psalms69:22 Let their table become a snare before them: and that which should have been for their welfare
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes be darkened

Psalms69:25 Let their habitation be desolate; and let none dwell in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten; and they talk to the grief of those whom thou hast wounded.
Psalms69:27 Add iniquity unto their iniquity: and let them not come into thy righteousness.
Psalms69:28 Let them be blotted out of the book of the living

Psalms73:1 Truly God is good to Israel

Psalms74:1 O God
Psalms74:2 Remember thy congregation
Psalms74:3 Lift up thy feet unto the perpetual desolations; even all that the enemy hath done wickedly in the sanctuary.
Psalms74:4 Thine enemies roar in the midst of thy congregations; they set up their ensigns for signs.
Psalms74:5 A man was famous according as he had lifted up axes upon the thick trees.
Psalms74:6 But now they break down the carved work thereof at once with axes and hammers.
Psalms74:7 They have cast fire into thy sanctuary
Psalms74:8 They said in their hearts
Psalms74:9 We see not our signs: there is no more any prophet: neither is there among us any that knoweth how long.
Psalms74:10 O God
Psalms74:11 Why withdrawest thou thy hand
Psalms74:12 For God is my King of old
Psalms74:13 Thou didst divide the sea by thy strength: thou brakest the heads of the dragons in the waters.
Psalms74:14 Thou brakest the heads of leviathan in pieces
Psalms74:15 Thou didst cleave the fountain and the flood: thou driedst up mighty rivers.

Psalms74:17 Thou hast set all the borders of the earth: thou hast made summer and winter.
Psalms74:18 Remember this

Psalms74:21 O let not the oppressed return ashamed: let the poor and needy praise thy name.

Psalms74:23 Forget not the voice of thine enemies: the tumult of those that rise up against thee increaseth continually.

Psalms77:2 In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord: my sore ran in the night
Psalms77:3 I remembered God
Psalms77:4 Thou holdest mine eyes waking: I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I have considered the days of old
Psalms77:6 I call to remembrance my song in the night: I commune with mine own heart: and my spirit made diligent search.

Psalms77:8 Is his mercy clean gone for ever? doth his promise fail for evermore?
Psalms77:9 Hath God forgotten to be gracious? hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah.

Psalms78:1 Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable: I will utter dark sayings of old:
Psalms78:3 Which we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from their children
Psalms78:5 For he established a testimony in Jacob
Psalms78:6 That the generation to come might know them
Psalms78:7 That they might set their hope in God
Psalms78:8 And might not be as their fathers
Psalms78:9 The children of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 They kept not the covenant of God
Psalms78:11 And forgat his works
Psalms78:12 Marvellous things did he in the sight of their fathers
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea
Psalms78:14 In the daytime also he led them with a cloud
Psalms78:15 He clave the rocks in the wilderness
Psalms78:16 He brought streams also out of the rock
Psalms78:17 And they sinned yet more against him by provoking the most High in the wilderness.
Psalms78:18 And they tempted God in their heart by asking meat for their lust.
Psalms78:19 Yea
Psalms78:20 Behold
Psalms78:21 Therefore the LORD heard this
Psalms78:22 Because they believed not in God
Psalms78:23 Though he had commanded the clouds from above
Psalms78:24 And had rained down manna upon them to eat
Psalms78:25 Man did eat angels' food: he sent them meat to the full.
Psalms78:26 He caused an east wind to blow in the heaven: and by his power he brought in the south wind.
Psalms78:27 He rained flesh also upon them as dust
Psalms78:28 And he let it fall in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 So they did eat
Psalms78:30 They were not estranged from their lust. But while their meat was yet in their mouths
Psalms78:31 The wrath of God came upon them
Psalms78:32 For all this they sinned still
Psalms78:33 Therefore their days did he consume in vanity
Psalms78:34 When he slew them
Psalms78:35 And they remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 Nevertheless they did flatter him with their mouth
Psalms78:37 For their heart was not right with him
Psalms78:38 But he
Psalms78:39 For he remembered that they were but flesh; a wind that passeth away
Psalms78:40 How oft did they provoke him in the wilderness
Psalms78:41 Yea
Psalms78:42 They remembered not his hand
Psalms78:43 How he had wrought his signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 And had turned their rivers into blood; and their floods
Psalms78:45 He sent divers sorts of flies among them
Psalms78:46 He gave also their increase unto the caterpiller
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail
Psalms78:48 He gave up their cattle also to the hail
Psalms78:49 He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger
Psalms78:50 He made a way to his anger; he spared not their soul from death
Psalms78:51 And smote all the firstborn in Egypt; the chief of their strength in the tabernacles of Ham:
Psalms78:52 But made his own people to go forth like sheep
Psalms78:53 And he led them on safely
Psalms78:54 And he brought them to the border of his sanctuary
Psalms78:55 He cast out the heathen also before them
Psalms78:56 Yet they tempted and provoked the most high God
Psalms78:57 But turned back
Psalms78:58 For they provoked him to anger with their high places
Psalms78:59 When God heard this
Psalms78:60 So that he forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh
Psalms78:61 And delivered his strength into captivity
Psalms78:62 He gave his people over also unto the sword; and was wroth with his inheritance.
Psalms78:63 The fire consumed their young men; and their maidens were not given to marriage.
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword; and their widows made no lamentation.
Psalms78:65 Then the LORD awaked as one out of sleep
Psalms78:66 And he smote his enemies in the hinder parts: he put them to a perpetual reproach.
Psalms78:67 Moreover he refused the tabernacle of Joseph
Psalms78:68 But chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 And he built his sanctuary like high palaces
Psalms78:70 He chose David also his servant
Psalms78:71 From following the ewes great with young he brought him to feed Jacob his people
Psalms78:72 So he fed them according to the integrity of his heart; and guided them by the skilfulness of his hands.

Psalms89:17 For thou art the glory of their strength: and in thy favour our horn shall be exalted.
Psalms89:18 For the LORD is our defence; and the Holy One of Israel is our king.
Psalms89:19 Then thou spakest in vision to thy holy one
Psalms89:20 I have found David my servant; with my holy oil have I anointed him:

Psalms89:23 And I will beat down his foes before his face
Psalms89:24 But my faithfulness and my mercy shall be with him: and in my name shall his horn be exalted.

Psalms89:26 He shall cry unto me
Psalms89:27 Also I will make him my firstborn
Psalms89:28 My mercy will I keep for him for evermore
Psalms89:29 His seed also will I make to endure for ever

Psalms89:31 If they break my statutes
Psalms89:32 Then will I visit their transgression with the rod
Psalms89:33 Nevertheless my lovingkindness will I not utterly take from him
Psalms89:34 My covenant will I not break
Psalms89:35 Once have I sworn by my holiness that I will not lie unto David.
Psalms89:36 His seed shall endure for ever
Psalms89:37 It shall be established for ever as the moon
Psalms89:38 But thou hast cast off and abhorred
Psalms89:39 Thou hast made void the covenant of thy servant: thou hast profaned his crown by casting it to the ground.
Psalms89:40 Thou hast broken down all his hedges; thou hast brought his strong holds to ruin.
Psalms89:41 All that pass by the way spoil him: he is a reproach to his neighbours.
Psalms89:42 Thou hast set up the right hand of his adversaries; thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice.
Psalms89:43 Thou hast also turned the edge of his sword
Psalms89:44 Thou hast made his glory to cease
Psalms89:45 The days of his youth hast thou shortened: thou hast covered him with shame. Selah.

Psalms104:1 Bless the LORD
Psalms104:2 Who coverest thyself with light as with a garment: who stretchest out the heavens like a curtain:
Psalms104:3 Who layeth the beams of his chambers in the waters: who maketh the clouds his chariot: who walketh upon the wings of the wind:
Psalms104:4 Who maketh his angels spirits; his ministers a flaming fire:
Psalms104:5 Who laid the foundations of the earth
Psalms104:6 Thou coveredst it with the deep as with a garment: the waters stood above the mountains.
Psalms104:7 At thy rebuke they fled; at the voice of thy thunder they hasted away.
Psalms104:8 They go up by the mountains; they go down by the valleys unto the place which thou hast founded for them.
Psalms104:9 Thou hast set a bound that they may not pass over; that they turn not again to cover the earth.
Psalms104:10 He sendeth the springs into the valleys
Psalms104:11 They give drink to every beast of the field: the wild asses quench their thirst.
Psalms104:12 By them shall the fowls of the heaven have their habitation
Psalms104:13 He watereth the hills from his chambers: the earth is satisfied with the fruit of thy works.
Psalms104:14 He causeth the grass to grow for the cattle
Psalms104:15 And wine that maketh glad the heart of man
Psalms104:16 The trees of the LORD are full of sap; the cedars of Lebanon
Psalms104:17 Where the birds make their nests: as for the stork
Psalms104:18 The high hills are a refuge for the wild goats; and the rocks for the conies.
Psalms104:19 He appointed the moon for seasons: the sun knoweth his going down.
Psalms104:20 Thou makest darkness
Psalms104:21 The young lions roar after their prey
Psalms104:22 The sun ariseth
Psalms104:23 Man goeth forth unto his work and to his labour until the evening.
Psalms104:24 O LORD
Psalms104:25 So is this great and wide sea
Psalms104:26 There go the ships: there is that leviathan
Psalms104:27 These wait all upon thee; that thou mayest give them their meat in due season.
Psalms104:28 That thou givest them they gather: thou openest thine hand
Psalms104:29 Thou hidest thy face
Psalms104:30 Thou sendest forth thy spirit
Psalms104:31 The glory of the LORD shall endure for ever: the LORD shall rejoice in his works.
Psalms104:32 He looketh on the earth
Psalms104:33 I will sing unto the LORD as long as I live: I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
Psalms104:34 My meditation of him shall be sweet: I will be glad in the LORD.
Psalms104:35 Let the sinners be consumed out of the earth
Psalms105:1 O give thanks unto the LORD; call upon his name: make known his deeds among the people.
Psalms105:2 Sing unto him
Psalms105:3 Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD.
Psalms105:4 Seek the LORD
Psalms105:5 Remember his marvellous works that he hath done; his wonders
Psalms105:6 O ye seed of Abraham his servant
Psalms105:7 He is the LORD our God: his judgments are in all the earth.
Psalms105:8 He hath remembered his covenant for ever
Psalms105:9 Which covenant he made with Abraham
Psalms105:10 And confirmed the same unto Jacob for a law
Psalms105:11 Saying
Psalms105:12 When they were but a few men in number; yea
Psalms105:13 When they went from one nation to another
Psalms105:14 He suffered no man to do them wrong: yea
Psalms105:15 Saying
Psalms105:16 Moreover he called for a famine upon the land: he brake the whole staff of bread.
Psalms105:17 He sent a man before them
Psalms105:18 Whose feet they hurt with fetters: he was laid in iron:
Psalms105:19 Until the time that his word came: the word of the LORD tried him.
Psalms105:20 The king sent and loosed him; even the ruler of the people
Psalms105:21 He made him lord of his house
Psalms105:22 To bind his princes at his pleasure; and teach his senators wisdom.
Psalms105:23 Israel also came into Egypt; and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
Psalms105:24 And he increased his people greatly; and made them stronger than their enemies.
Psalms105:25 He turned their heart to hate his people
Psalms105:26 He sent Moses his servant; and Aaron whom he had chosen.
Psalms105:27 They shewed his signs among them
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness
Psalms105:29 He turned their waters into blood
Psalms105:30 Their land brought forth frogs in abundance
Psalms105:31 He spake
Psalms105:32 He gave them hail for rain
Psalms105:33 He smote their vines also and their fig trees; and brake the trees of their coasts.
Psalms105:34 He spake
Psalms105:35 And did eat up all the herbs in their land
Psalms105:36 He smote also all the firstborn in their land
Psalms105:37 He brought them forth also with silver and gold: and there was not one feeble person among their tribes.
Psalms105:38 Egypt was glad when they departed: for the fear of them fell upon them.
Psalms105:39 He spread a cloud for a covering; and fire to give light in the night.
Psalms105:40 The people asked
Psalms105:41 He opened the rock
Psalms105:42 For he remembered his holy promise
Psalms105:43 And he brought forth his people with joy
Psalms105:44 And gave them the lands of the heathen: and they inherited the labour of the people;
Psalms105:45 That they might observe his statutes
Psalms106:1 Praise ye the LORD. O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms106:2 Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD? who can shew forth all his praise?
Psalms106:3 Blessed are they that keep judgment
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 That I may see the good of thy chosen
Psalms106:6 We have sinned with our fathers
Psalms106:7 Our fathers understood not thy wonders in Egypt; they remembered not the multitude of thy mercies; but provoked him at the sea
Psalms106:8 Nevertheless he saved them for his name's sake
Psalms106:9 He rebuked the Red sea also
Psalms106:10 And he saved them from the hand of him that hated them
Psalms106:11 And the waters covered their enemies: there was not one of them left.
Psalms106:12 Then believed they his words; they sang his praise.
Psalms106:13 They soon forgat his works; they waited not for his counsel:
Psalms106:14 But lusted exceedingly in the wilderness
Psalms106:15 And he gave them their request; but sent leanness into their soul.
Psalms106:16 They envied Moses also in the camp
Psalms106:17 The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan and covered the company of Abiram.
Psalms106:18 And a fire was kindled in their company; the flame burned up the wicked.
Psalms106:19 They made a calf in Horeb
Psalms106:20 Thus they changed their glory into the similitude of an ox that eateth grass.
Psalms106:21 They forgat God their saviour
Psalms106:22 Wondrous works in the land of Ham
Psalms106:23 Therefore he said that he would destroy them
Psalms106:24 Yea
Psalms106:25 But murmured in their tents
Psalms106:26 Therefore he lifted up his hand against them
Psalms106:27 To overthrow their seed also among the nations
Psalms106:28 They joined themselves also unto Baalpeor
Psalms106:29 Thus they provoked him to anger with their inventions: and the plague brake in upon them.
Psalms106:30 Then stood up Phinehas
Psalms106:31 And that was counted unto him for righteousness unto all generations for evermore.
Psalms106:32 They angered him also at the waters of strife
Psalms106:33 Because they provoked his spirit
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the nations
Psalms106:35 But were mingled among the heathen
Psalms106:36 And they served their idols: which were a snare unto them.
Psalms106:37 Yea
Psalms106:38 And shed innocent blood
Psalms106:39 Thus were they defiled with their own works
Psalms106:40 Therefore was the wrath of the LORD kindled against his people
Psalms106:41 And he gave them into the hand of the heathen; and they that hated them ruled over them.
Psalms106:42 Their enemies also oppressed them
Psalms106:43 Many times did he deliver them; but they provoked him with their counsel
Psalms106:44 Nevertheless he regarded their affliction
Psalms106:45 And he remembered for them his covenant
Psalms106:46 He made them also to be pitied of all those that carried them captives.
Psalms106:47 Save us
Psalms106:48 Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting to everlasting: and let all the people say

Psalms107:2 Let the redeemed of the LORD say so

Psalms107:7 And he led them forth by the right way

Psalms107:24 These see the works of the LORD
Psalms107:25 For he commandeth
Psalms107:26 They mount up to the heaven
Psalms107:27 They reel to and fro
Psalms107:28 Then they cry unto the LORD in their trouble
Psalms107:29 He maketh the storm a calm
Psalms107:30 Then are they glad because they be quiet; so he bringeth them unto their desired haven.

Psalms107:32 Let them exalt him also in the congregation of the people

Psalms108:1 O God
Psalms108:2 Awake
Psalms108:3 I will praise thee
Psalms108:4 For thy mercy is great above the heavens: and thy truth reacheth unto the clouds.
Psalms108:5 Be thou exalted
Psalms108:6 That thy beloved may be delivered: save with thy right hand
Psalms108:7 God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice
Psalms108:8 Gilead is mine; Manasseh is mine; Ephraim also is the strength of mine head; Judah is my lawgiver;
Psalms108:9 Moab is my washpot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe; over Philistia will I triumph.
Psalms108:10 Who will bring me into the strong city? who will lead me into Edom?
Psalms108:11 Wilt not thou
Psalms108:12 Give us help from trouble: for vain is the help of man.
Psalms108:13 Through God we shall do valiantly: for he it is that shall tread down our enemies.
Psalms109:1 Hold not thy peace
Psalms109:2 For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful are opened against me: they have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
Psalms109:3 They compassed me about also with words of hatred; and fought against me without a cause.
Psalms109:4 For my love they are my adversaries: but I give myself unto prayer.
Psalms109:5 And they have rewarded me evil for good
Psalms109:6 Set thou a wicked man over him: and let Satan stand at his right hand.
Psalms109:7 When he shall be judged
Psalms109:8 Let his days be few; and let another take his office.
Psalms109:9 Let his children be fatherless
Psalms109:10 Let his children be continually vagabonds
Psalms109:11 Let the extortioner catch all that he hath; and let the strangers spoil his labour.
Psalms109:12 Let there be none to extend mercy unto him: neither let there be any to favour his fatherless children.
Psalms109:13 Let his posterity be cut off; and in the generation following let their name be blotted out.
Psalms109:14 Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered with the LORD; and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
Psalms109:15 Let them be before the LORD continually
Psalms109:16 Because that he remembered not to shew mercy
Psalms109:17 As he loved cursing
Psalms109:18 As he clothed himself with cursing like as with his garment
Psalms109:19 Let it be unto him as the garment which covereth him
Psalms109:20 Let this be the reward of mine adversaries from the LORD
Psalms109:21 But do thou for me
Psalms109:22 For I am poor and needy
Psalms109:23 I am gone like the shadow when it declineth: I am tossed up and down as the locust.
Psalms109:24 My knees are weak through fasting; and my flesh faileth of fatness.
Psalms109:25 I became also a reproach unto them: when they looked upon me they shaked their heads.
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 That they may know that this is thy hand; that thou
Psalms109:28 Let them curse
Psalms109:29 Let mine adversaries be clothed with shame
Psalms109:30 I will greatly praise the LORD with my mouth; yea
Psalms109:31 For he shall stand at the right hand of the poor

Psalms111:7 The works of his hands are verity and judgment; all his commandments are sure.

Psalms111:10 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.

Psalms119:1 Blessed are the undefiled in the way
Psalms119:2 Blessed are they that keep his testimonies
Psalms119:3 They also do no iniquity: they walk in his ways.
Psalms119:4 Thou hast commanded us to keep thy precepts diligently.
Psalms119:5 O that my ways were directed to keep thy statutes!
Psalms119:6 Then shall I not be ashamed
Psalms119:7 I will praise thee with uprightness of heart
Psalms119:8 I will keep thy statutes: O forsake me not utterly.
Psalms119:9 Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? by taking heed thereto according to thy word.
Psalms119:10 With my whole heart have I sought thee: O let me not wander from thy commandments.
Psalms119:11 Thy word have I hid in mine heart
Psalms119:12 Blessed art thou
Psalms119:13 With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.
Psalms119:14 I have rejoiced in the way of thy testimonies
Psalms119:15 I will meditate in thy precepts
Psalms119:16 I will delight myself in thy statutes: I will not forget thy word.
Psalms119:17 Deal bountifully with thy servant
Psalms119:18 Open thou mine eyes
Psalms119:19 I am a stranger in the earth: hide not thy commandments from me.
Psalms119:20 My soul breaketh for the longing that it hath unto thy judgments at all times.
Psalms119:21 Thou hast rebuked the proud that are cursed
Psalms119:22 Remove from me reproach and contempt; for I have kept thy testimonies.
Psalms119:23 Princes also did sit and speak against me: but thy servant did meditate in thy statutes.
Psalms119:24 Thy testimonies also are my delight and my counsellors.
Psalms119:25 My soul cleaveth unto the dust: quicken thou me according to thy word.
Psalms119:26 I have declared my ways
Psalms119:27 Make me to understand the way of thy precepts: so shall I talk of thy wondrous works.
Psalms119:28 My soul melteth for heaviness: strengthen thou me according unto thy word.
Psalms119:29 Remove from me the way of lying: and grant me thy law graciously.
Psalms119:30 I have chosen the way of truth: thy judgments have I laid before me.
Psalms119:31 I have stuck unto thy testimonies: O LORD
Psalms119:32 I will run the way of thy commandments
Psalms119:33 Teach me
Psalms119:34 Give me understanding
Psalms119:35 Make me to go in the path of thy commandments; for therein do I delight.
Psalms119:36 Incline my heart unto thy testimonies
Psalms119:37 Turn away mine eyes from beholding vanity; and quicken thou me in thy way.
Psalms119:38 Stablish thy word unto thy servant
Psalms119:39 Turn away my reproach which I fear: for thy judgments are good.
Psalms119:40 Behold
Psalms119:41 Let thy mercies come also unto me
Psalms119:42 So shall I have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me: for I trust in thy word.
Psalms119:43 And take not the word of truth utterly out of my mouth; for I have hoped in thy judgments.
Psalms119:44 So shall I keep thy law continually for ever and ever.
Psalms119:45 And I will walk at liberty: for I seek thy precepts.
Psalms119:46 I will speak of thy testimonies also before kings
Psalms119:47 And I will delight myself in thy commandments
Psalms119:48 My hands also will I lift up unto thy commandments
Psalms119:49 Remember the word unto thy servant
Psalms119:50 This is my comfort in my affliction: for thy word hath quickened me.
Psalms119:51 The proud have had me greatly in derision: yet have I not declined from thy law.
Psalms119:52 I remembered thy judgments of old
Psalms119:53 Horror hath taken hold upon me because of the wicked that forsake thy law.
Psalms119:54 Thy statutes have been my songs in the house of my pilgrimage.
Psalms119:55 I have remembered thy name
Psalms119:56 This I had
Psalms119:57 Thou art my portion
Psalms119:58 I intreated thy favour with my whole heart: be merciful unto me according to thy word.
Psalms119:59 I thought on my ways
Psalms119:60 I made haste
Psalms119:61 The bands of the wicked have robbed me: but I have not forgotten thy law.
Psalms119:62 At midnight I will rise to give thanks unto thee because of thy righteous judgments.
Psalms119:63 I am a companion of all them that fear thee
Psalms119:64 The earth
Psalms119:65 Thou hast dealt well with thy servant
Psalms119:66 Teach me good judgment and knowledge: for I have believed thy commandments.
Psalms119:67 Before I was afflicted I went astray: but now have I kept thy word.
Psalms119:68 Thou art good
Psalms119:69 The proud have forged a lie against me: but I will keep thy precepts with my whole heart.
Psalms119:70 Their heart is as fat as grease; but I delight in thy law.
Psalms119:71 It is good for me that I have been afflicted; that I might learn thy statutes.
Psalms119:72 The law of thy mouth is better unto me than thousands of gold and silver.
Psalms119:73 Thy hands have made me and fashioned me: give me understanding
Psalms119:74 They that fear thee will be glad when they see me; because I have hoped in thy word.
Psalms119:75 I know
Psalms119:76 Let
Psalms119:77 Let thy tender mercies come unto me
Psalms119:78 Let the proud be ashamed; for they dealt perversely with me without a cause: but I will meditate in thy precepts.
Psalms119:79 Let those that fear thee turn unto me
Psalms119:80 Let my heart be sound in thy statutes; that I be not ashamed.
Psalms119:81 My soul fainteth for thy salvation: but I hope in thy word.
Psalms119:82 Mine eyes fail for thy word
Psalms119:83 For I am become like a bottle in the smoke; yet do I not forget thy statutes.
Psalms119:84 How many are the days of thy servant? when wilt thou execute judgment on them that persecute me?
Psalms119:85 The proud have digged pits for me
Psalms119:86 All thy commandments are faithful: they persecute me wrongfully; help thou me.
Psalms119:87 They had almost consumed me upon earth; but I forsook not thy precepts.
Psalms119:88 Quicken me after thy lovingkindness; so shall I keep the testimony of thy mouth.
Psalms119:89 For ever
Psalms119:90 Thy faithfulness is unto all generations: thou hast established the earth
Psalms119:91 They continue this day according to thine ordinances: for all are thy servants.
Psalms119:92 Unless thy law had been my delights
Psalms119:93 I will never forget thy precepts: for with them thou hast quickened me.
Psalms119:94 I am thine
Psalms119:95 The wicked have waited for me to destroy me: but I will consider thy testimonies.
Psalms119:96 I have seen an end of all perfection: but thy commandment is exceeding broad.
Psalms119:97 O how I love thy law! it is my meditation all the day.
Psalms119:98 Thou through thy commandments hast made me wiser than mine enemies: for they are ever with me.
Psalms119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers: for thy testimonies are my meditation.
Psalms119:100 I understand more than the ancients
Psalms119:101 I have refrained my feet from every evil way
Psalms119:102 I have not departed from thy judgments: for thou hast taught me.
Psalms119:103 How sweet are thy words unto my taste! yea
Psalms119:104 Through thy precepts I get understanding: therefore I hate every false way.
Psalms119:105 Thy word is a lamp unto my feet
Psalms119:106 I have sworn
Psalms119:107 I am afflicted very much: quicken me
Psalms119:108 Accept
Psalms119:109 My soul is continually in my hand: yet do I not forget thy law.
Psalms119:110 The wicked have laid a snare for me: yet I erred not from thy precepts.
Psalms119:111 Thy testimonies have I taken as an heritage for ever: for they are the rejoicing of my heart.
Psalms119:112 I have inclined mine heart to perform thy statutes alway
Psalms119:113 I hate vain thoughts: but thy law do I love.
Psalms119:114 Thou art my hiding place and my shield: I hope in thy word.
Psalms119:115 Depart from me
Psalms119:116 Uphold me according unto thy word
Psalms119:117 Hold thou me up
Psalms119:118 Thou hast trodden down all them that err from thy statutes: for their deceit is falsehood.
Psalms119:119 Thou puttest away all the wicked of the earth like dross: therefore I love thy testimonies.
Psalms119:120 My flesh trembleth for fear of thee; and I am afraid of thy judgments.
Psalms119:121 I have done judgment and justice: leave me not to mine oppressors.
Psalms119:122 Be surety for thy servant for good: let not the proud oppress me.
Psalms119:123 Mine eyes fail for thy salvation
Psalms119:124 Deal with thy servant according unto thy mercy
Psalms119:125 I am thy servant; give me understanding
Psalms119:126 It is time for thee
Psalms119:127 Therefore I love thy commandments above gold; yea
Psalms119:128 Therefore I esteem all thy precepts concerning all things to be right; and I hate every false way.
Psalms119:129 Thy testimonies are wonderful: therefore doth my soul keep them.
Psalms119:130 The entrance of thy words giveth light; it giveth understanding unto the simple.
Psalms119:131 I opened my mouth
Psalms119:132 Look thou upon me
Psalms119:133 Order my steps in thy word: and let not any iniquity have dominion over me.
Psalms119:134 Deliver me from the oppression of man: so will I keep thy precepts.
Psalms119:135 Make thy face to shine upon thy servant; and teach me thy statutes.
Psalms119:136 Rivers of waters run down mine eyes
Psalms119:137 Righteous art thou
Psalms119:138 Thy testimonies that thou hast commanded are righteous and very faithful.
Psalms119:139 My zeal hath consumed me
Psalms119:140 Thy word is very pure: therefore thy servant loveth it.
Psalms119:141 I am small and despised: yet do not I forget thy precepts.
Psalms119:142 Thy righteousness is an everlasting righteousness
Psalms119:143 Trouble and anguish have taken hold on me: yet thy commandments are my delights.
Psalms119:144 The righteousness of thy testimonies is everlasting: give me understanding
Psalms119:145 I cried with my whole heart; hear me
Psalms119:146 I cried unto thee; save me
Psalms119:147 I prevented the dawning of the morning
Psalms119:148 Mine eyes prevent the night watches
Psalms119:149 Hear my voice according unto thy lovingkindness: O LORD
Psalms119:150 They draw nigh that follow after mischief: they are far from thy law.
Psalms119:151 Thou art near
Psalms119:152 Concerning thy testimonies
Psalms119:153 Consider mine affliction
Psalms119:154 Plead my cause
Psalms119:155 Salvation is far from the wicked: for they seek not thy statutes.
Psalms119:156 Great are thy tender mercies
Psalms119:157 Many are my persecutors and mine enemies; yet do I not decline from thy testimonies.
Psalms119:158 I beheld the transgressors
Psalms119:159 Consider how I love thy precepts: quicken me
Psalms119:160 Thy word is true from the beginning: and every one of thy righteous judgments endureth for ever.
Psalms119:161 Princes have persecuted me without a cause: but my heart standeth in awe of thy word.
Psalms119:162 I rejoice at thy word
Psalms119:163 I hate and abhor lying: but thy law do I love.
Psalms119:164 Seven times a day do I praise thee because of thy righteous judgments.
Psalms119:165 Great peace have they which love thy law: and nothing shall offend them.
Psalms119:166 LORD
Psalms119:167 My soul hath kept thy testimonies; and I love them exceedingly.
Psalms119:168 I have kept thy precepts and thy testimonies: for all my ways are before thee.
Psalms119:169 Let my cry come near before thee
Psalms119:170 Let my supplication come before thee: deliver me according to thy word.
Psalms119:171 My lips shall utter praise
Psalms119:172 My tongue shall speak of thy word: for all thy commandments are righteousness.
Psalms119:173 Let thine hand help me; for I have chosen thy precepts.
Psalms119:174 I have longed for thy salvation
Psalms119:175 Let my soul live
Psalms119:176 I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek thy servant; for I do not forget thy commandments.

Psalms136:1 O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:2 O give thanks unto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:3 O give thanks to the Lord of lords: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:4 To him who alone doeth great wonders: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:5 To him that by wisdom made the heavens: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:6 To him that stretched out the earth above the waters: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:7 To him that made great lights: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:8 The sun to rule by day: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:9 The moon and stars to rule by night: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:10 To him that smote Egypt in their firstborn: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:11 And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:12 With a strong hand
Psalms136:13 To him which divided the Red sea into parts: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:14 And made Israel to pass through the midst of it: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:15 But overthrew Pharaoh and his host in the Red sea: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:16 To him which led his people through the wilderness: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:17 To him which smote great kings: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:18 And slew famous kings: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:19 Sihon king of the Amorites: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:20 And Og the king of Bashan: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:21 And gave their land for an heritage: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:22 Even an heritage unto Israel his servant: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:23 Who remembered us in our low estate: for his mercy endureth for ever:
Psalms136:24 And hath redeemed us from our enemies: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:25 Who giveth food to all flesh: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Psalms136:26 O give thanks unto the God of heaven: for his mercy endureth for ever.
