Jeremiah51:12 Set up the standard upon the walls of Babylon
Jeremiah51:13 O thou that dwellest upon many waters
Jeremiah51:14 The LORD of hosts hath sworn by himself
Jeremiah51:15 He hath made the earth by his power
Jeremiah51:16 When he uttereth his voice
Jeremiah51:17 Every man is brutish by his knowledge; every founder is confounded by the graven image: for his molten image is falsehood
Jeremiah51:18 They are vanity
Jeremiah51:19 The portion of Jacob is not like them; for he is the former of all things: and Israel is the rod of his inheritance: the LORD of hosts is his name.
Jeremiah51:20 Thou art my battle axe and weapons of war: for with thee will I break in pieces the nations
Jeremiah51:21 And with thee will I break in pieces the horse and his rider; and with thee will I break in pieces the chariot and his rider;
Jeremiah51:22 With thee also will I break in pieces man and woman; and with thee will I break in pieces old and young; and with thee will I break in pieces the young man and the maid;
Jeremiah51:23 I will also break in pieces with thee the shepherd and his flock; and with thee will I break in pieces the husbandman and his yoke of oxen; and with thee will I break in pieces captains and rulers.
Jeremiah51:24 And I will render unto Babylon and to all the inhabitants of Chaldea all their evil that they have done in Zion in your sight
Jeremiah51:25 Behold
Jeremiah51:26 And they shall not take of thee a stone for a corner
Jeremiah51:27 Set ye up a standard in the land
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare against her the nations with the kings of the Medes
Jeremiah51:29 And the land shall tremble and sorrow: for every purpose of the LORD shall be performed against Babylon
Jeremiah51:30 The mighty men of Babylon have forborn to fight
Jeremiah51:31 One post shall run to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 And that the passages are stopped
Jeremiah51:33 For thus saith the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon hath devoured me
Jeremiah51:35 The violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus saith the LORD; Behold
Jeremiah51:37 And Babylon shall become heaps
Jeremiah51:38 They shall roar together like lions: they shall yell as lions' whelps.
Jeremiah51:39 In their heat I will make their feasts
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How is Sheshach taken! and how is the praise of the whole earth surprised! how is Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!
Jeremiah51:42 The sea is come up upon Babylon: she is covered with the multitude of the waves thereof.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities are a desolation
Jeremiah51:44 And I will punish Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 My people
Jeremiah51:46 And lest your heart faint
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore
Jeremiah51:48 Then the heaven and the earth
Jeremiah51:49 As Babylon hath caused the slain of Israel to fall
Jeremiah51:50 Ye that have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are confounded
Jeremiah51:52 Wherefore
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon should mount up to heaven
Jeremiah51:54 A sound of a cry cometh from Babylon
Jeremiah51:55 Because the LORD hath spoiled Babylon
Jeremiah51:56 Because the spoiler is come upon her
Jeremiah51:57 And I will make drunk her princes
Jeremiah51:58 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; The broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly broken
Jeremiah51:59 The word which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Neriah
Jeremiah51:60 So Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:61 And Jeremiah said to Seraiah
Jeremiah51:62 Then shalt thou say
Jeremiah51:63 And it shall be
Jeremiah51:64 And thou shalt say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was one and twenty years old when he began to reign
Jeremiah52:2 And he did that which was evil in the eyes of the LORD
Jeremiah52:3 For through the anger of the LORD it came to pass in Jerusalem and Judah
Jeremiah52:4 And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was besieged unto the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 And in the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city was broken up
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king
Jeremiah52:9 Then they took the king
Jeremiah52:10 And the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes: he slew also all the princes of Judah in Riblah.
Jeremiah52:11 Then he put out the eyes of Zedekiah; and the king of Babylon bound him in chains
Jeremiah52:12 Now in the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 And burned the house of the LORD
Jeremiah52:14 And all the army of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive certain of the poor of the people
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left certain of the poor of the land for vinedressers and for husbandmen.
Jeremiah52:17 Also the pillars of brass that were in the house of the LORD
Jeremiah52:18 The caldrons also
Jeremiah52:19 And the basons
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 And concerning the pillars
Jeremiah52:22 And a chapiter of brass was upon it; and the height of one chapiter was five cubits
Jeremiah52:23 And there were ninety and six pomegranates on a side; and all the pomegranates upon the network were an hundred round about.
Jeremiah52:24 And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 He took also out of the city an eunuch
Jeremiah52:26 So Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them
Jeremiah52:27 And the king of Babylon smote them
Jeremiah52:28 This is the people whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year three thousand Jews and three and twenty:
Jeremiah52:29 In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar he carried away captive from Jerusalem eight hundred thirty and two persons:
Jeremiah52:30 In the three and twentieth year of Nebuchadrezzar Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive of the Jews seven hundred forty and five persons: all the persons were four thousand and six hundred.
Jeremiah52:31 And it came to pass in the seven and thirtieth year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 And spake kindly unto him
Jeremiah52:33 And changed his prison garments: and he did continually eat bread before him all the days of his life.
Jeremiah52:34 And for his diet
Lamentations1:1 How doth the city sit solitary
Lamentations1:2 She weepeth sore in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah is gone into captivity because of affliction
Lamentations1:4 The ways of Zion do mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries are the chief
Lamentations1:6 And from the daughter of Zion all her beauty is departed: her princes are become like harts that find no pasture
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem remembered in the days of her affliction and of her miseries all her pleasant things that she had in the days of old
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem hath grievously sinned; therefore she is removed: all that honoured her despise her
Lamentations1:9 Her filthiness is in her skirts; she remembereth not her last end; therefore she came down wonderfully: she had no comforter. O LORD
Lamentations1:10 The adversary hath spread out his hand upon all her pleasant things: for she hath seen that the heathen entered into her sanctuary
Lamentations1:11 All her people sigh
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 From above hath he sent fire into my bones
Lamentations1:14 The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand: they are wreathed
Lamentations1:15 The LORD hath trodden under foot all my mighty men in the midst of me: he hath called an assembly against me to crush my young men: the LORD hath trodden the virgin
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; mine eye
Lamentations1:17 Zion spreadeth forth her hands
Lamentations1:18 The LORD is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment: hear
Lamentations1:19 I called for my lovers
Lamentations1:20 Behold
Lamentations1:21 They have heard that I sigh: there is none to comfort me: all mine enemies have heard of my trouble; they are glad that thou hast done it: thou wilt bring the day that thou hast called
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before thee; and do unto them
Lamentations2:1 How hath the LORD covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud in his anger
Lamentations2:2 The LORD hath swallowed up all the habitations of Jacob
Lamentations2:3 He hath cut off in his fierce anger all the horn of Israel: he hath drawn back his right hand from before the enemy
Lamentations2:4 He hath bent his bow like an enemy: he stood with his right hand as an adversary
Lamentations2:5 The LORD was as an enemy: he hath swallowed up Israel
Lamentations2:6 And he hath violently taken away his tabernacle
Lamentations2:7 The LORD hath cast off his altar
Lamentations2:8 The LORD hath purposed to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion: he hath stretched out a line
Lamentations2:9 Her gates are sunk into the ground; he hath destroyed and broken her bars: her king and her princes are among the Gentiles: the law is no more; her prophets also find no vision from the LORD.
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion sit upon the ground
Lamentations2:11 Mine eyes do fail with tears
Lamentations2:12 They say to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What thing shall I take to witness for thee? what thing shall I liken to thee
Lamentations2:14 Thy prophets have seen vain and foolish things for thee: and they have not discovered thine iniquity
Lamentations2:15 All that pass by clap their hands at thee; they hiss and wag their head at the daughter of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:16 All thine enemies have opened their mouth against thee: they hiss and gnash the teeth: they say
Lamentations2:17 The LORD hath done that which he had devised; he hath fulfilled his word that he had commanded in the days of old: he hath thrown down
Lamentations2:18 Their heart cried unto the LORD
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 Behold
Lamentations2:21 The young and the old lie on the ground in the streets: my virgins and my young men are fallen by the sword; thou hast slain them in the day of thine anger; thou hast killed
Lamentations2:22 Thou hast called as in a solemn day my terrors round about
Lamentations3:1 I AM the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He hath led me
Lamentations3:3 Surely against me is he turned; he turneth his hand against me all the day.
Lamentations3:4 My flesh and my skin hath he made old; he hath broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He hath builded against me
Lamentations3:6 He hath set me in dark places
Lamentations3:7 He hath hedged me about
Lamentations3:8 Also when I cry and shout
Lamentations3:9 He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone
Lamentations3:10 He was unto me as a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He hath turned aside my ways
Lamentations3:12 He hath bent his bow
Lamentations3:13 He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.
Lamentations3:14 I was a derision to all my people; and their song all the day.
Lamentations3:15 He hath filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones
Lamentations3:17 And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace: I forgat prosperity.
Lamentations3:18 And I said
Lamentations3:19 Remembering mine affliction and my misery
Lamentations3:20 My soul hath them still in remembrance
Lamentations3:21 This I recall to my mind
Lamentations3:22 It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 The LORD is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The LORD is good unto them that wait for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that a man should both hope and quietly wait for the salvation of the LORD.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man that he bear the yoke of his youth.
Lamentations3:28 He sitteth alone and keepeth silence
Lamentations3:29 He putteth his mouth in the dust; if so be there may be hope.
Lamentations3:30 He giveth his cheek to him that smiteth him: he is filled full with reproach.
Lamentations3:31 For the LORD will not cast off for ever:
Lamentations3:32 But though he cause grief
Lamentations3:33 For he doth not afflict willingly nor grieve the children of men.
Lamentations3:34 To crush under his feet all the prisoners of the earth.
Lamentations3:35 To turn aside the right of a man before the face of the most High
Lamentations3:36 To subvert a man in his cause
Lamentations3:37 Who is he that saith
Lamentations3:38 Out of the mouth of the most High proceedeth not evil and good?
Lamentations3:39 Wherefore doth a living man complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us search and try our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our heart with our hands unto God in the heavens.
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and have rebelled: thou hast not pardoned.
Lamentations3:43 Thou hast covered with anger
Lamentations3:44 Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud
Lamentations3:45 Thou hast made us as the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the people.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.
Lamentations3:47 Fear and a snare is come upon us
Lamentations3:48 Mine eye runneth down with rivers of water for the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 Mine eye trickleth down
Lamentations3:50 Till the LORD look down
Lamentations3:51 Mine eye affecteth mine heart because of all the daughters of my city.
Lamentations3:52 Mine enemies chased me sore
Lamentations3:53 They have cut off my life in the dungeon
Lamentations3:54 Waters flowed over mine head; then I said
Lamentations3:55 I called upon thy name
Lamentations3:56 Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing
Lamentations3:57 Thou drewest near in the day that I called upon thee: thou saidst
Lamentations3:58 O LORD
Lamentations3:59 O LORD
Lamentations3:60 Thou hast seen all their vengeance and all their imaginations against me.
Lamentations3:61 Thou hast heard their reproach
Lamentations3:62 The lips of those that rose up against me
Lamentations3:63 Behold their sitting down
Lamentations3:64 Render unto them a recompence
Lamentations3:65 Give them sorrow of heart
Lamentations3:66 Persecute and destroy them in anger from under the heavens of the LORD.
Lamentations4:1 How is the gold become dim! how is the most fine gold changed! the stones of the sanctuary are poured out in the top of every street.
Lamentations4:2 The precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the sea monsters draw out the breast
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst: the young children ask bread
Lamentations4:5 They that did feed delicately are desolate in the streets: they that were brought up in scarlet embrace dunghills.
Lamentations4:6 For the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the punishment of the sin of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her Nazarites were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 Their visage is blacker than a coal; they are not known in the streets: their skin cleaveth to their bones; it is withered
Lamentations4:9 They that be slain with the sword are better than they that be slain with hunger: for these pine away
Lamentations4:10 The hands of the pitiful women have sodden their own children: they were their meat in the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations4:11 The LORD hath accomplished his fury; he hath poured out his fierce anger
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth
Lamentations4:13 For the sins of her prophets
Lamentations4:14 They have wandered as blind men in the streets
Lamentations4:15 They cried unto them
Lamentations4:16 The anger of the LORD hath divided them; he will no more regard them: they respected not the persons of the priests
Lamentations4:17 As for us
Lamentations4:18 They hunt our steps
Lamentations4:19 Our persecutors are swifter than the eagles of the heaven: they pursued us upon the mountains
Lamentations4:20 The breath of our nostrils
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of thine iniquity is accomplished
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance is turned to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We are orphans and fatherless
Lamentations5:4 We have drunken our water for money; our wood is sold unto us.
Lamentations5:5 Our necks are under persecution: we labour
Lamentations5:6 We have given the hand to the Egyptians
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers have sinned
Lamentations5:8 Servants have ruled over us: there is none that doth deliver us out of their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We gat our bread with the peril of our lives because of the sword of the wilderness.
Lamentations5:10 Our skin was black like an oven because of the terrible famine.
Lamentations5:11 They ravished the women in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes are hanged up by their hand: the faces of elders were not honoured.
Lamentations5:13 They took the young men to grind
Lamentations5:14 The elders have ceased from the gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our heart is ceased; our dance is turned into mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown is fallen from our head: woe unto us
Lamentations5:17 For this our heart is faint; for these things our eyes are dim.
Lamentations5:18 Because of the mountain of Zion
Lamentations5:19 Thou
Lamentations5:20 Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever
Lamentations5:21 Turn thou us unto thee
Lamentations5:22 But thou hast utterly rejected us; thou art very wroth against us.
Ezekiel1:1 Now it came to pass in the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 In the fifth day of the month
Ezekiel1:3 The word of the LORD came expressly unto Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 And I looked
Ezekiel1:5 Also out of the midst thereof came the likeness of four living creatures. And this was their appearance; they had the likeness of a man.
Ezekiel1:6 And every one had four faces
Ezekiel1:7 And their feet were straight feet; and the sole of their feet was like the sole of a calf's foot: and they sparkled like the colour of burnished brass.
Ezekiel1:8 And they had the hands of a man under their wings on their four sides; and they four had their faces and their wings.
Ezekiel1:9 Their wings were joined one to another; they turned not when they went; they went every one straight forward.
Ezekiel1:10 As for the likeness of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 Thus were their faces: and their wings were stretched upward; two wings of every one were joined one to another
Ezekiel1:12 And they went every one straight forward: whither the spirit was to go
Ezekiel1:13 As for the likeness of the living creatures
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures ran and returned as the appearance of a flash of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 Now as I beheld the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their work was like unto the colour of a beryl: and they four had one likeness: and their appearance and their work was as it were a wheel in the middle of a wheel.
Ezekiel1:17 When they went
Ezekiel1:18 As for their rings
Ezekiel1:19 And when the living creatures went
Ezekiel1:20 Whithersoever the spirit was to go
Ezekiel1:21 When those went
Ezekiel1:22 And the likeness of the firmament upon the heads of the living creature was as the colour of the terrible crystal
Ezekiel1:23 And under the firmament were their wings straight
Ezekiel1:24 And when they went
Ezekiel1:25 And there was a voice from the firmament that was over their heads
Ezekiel1:26 And above the firmament that was over their heads was the likeness of a throne
Ezekiel1:27 And I saw as the colour of amber
Ezekiel1:28 As the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud in the day of rain

Ezekiel13:22 Because with lies ye have made the heart of the righteous sad
Ezekiel13:23 Therefore ye shall see no more vanity

Ezekiel16:15 But thou didst trust in thine own beauty
Ezekiel16:16 And of thy garments thou didst take
Ezekiel16:17 Thou hast also taken thy fair jewels of my gold and of my silver

Ezekiel16:20 Moreover thou hast taken thy sons and thy daughters

Ezekiel16:22 And in all thine abominations and thy whoredoms thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth
Ezekiel16:23 And it came to pass after all thy wickedness
Ezekiel16:24 That thou hast also built unto thee an eminent place
Ezekiel16:25 Thou hast built thy high place at every head of the way
Ezekiel16:26 Thou hast also committed fornication with the Egyptians thy neighbours
Ezekiel16:27 Behold
Ezekiel16:28 Thou hast played the whore also with the Assyrians
Ezekiel16:29 Thou hast moreover multiplied thy fornication in the land of Canaan unto Chaldea; and yet thou wast not satisfied therewith.
Ezekiel16:30 How weak is thine heart
Ezekiel16:31 In that thou buildest thine eminent place in the head of every way
Ezekiel16:32 But as a wife that committeth adultery
Ezekiel16:33 They give gifts to all whores: but thou givest thy gifts to all thy lovers
Ezekiel16:34 And the contrary is in thee from other women in thy whoredoms
Ezekiel16:35 Wherefore

Ezekiel16:37 Behold

Ezekiel16:39 And I will also give thee into their hand
Ezekiel16:40 They shall also bring up a company against thee
Ezekiel16:41 And they shall burn thine houses with fire
Ezekiel16:42 So will I make my fury toward thee to rest
Ezekiel16:43 Because thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth
Ezekiel16:44 Behold
Ezekiel16:45 Thou art thy mother's daughter
Ezekiel16:46 And thine elder sister is Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 Yet hast thou not walked after their ways
Ezekiel16:48 As I live
Ezekiel16:49 Behold

Ezekiel16:51 Neither hath Samaria committed half of thy sins; but thou hast multiplied thine abominations more than they
Ezekiel16:52 Thou also
Ezekiel16:53 When I shall bring again their captivity
Ezekiel16:54 That thou mayest bear thine own shame
Ezekiel16:55 When thy sisters
Ezekiel16:56 For thy sister Sodom was not mentioned by thy mouth in the day of thy pride
Ezekiel16:57 Before thy wickedness was discovered
Ezekiel16:58 Thou hast borne thy lewdness and thine abominations
Ezekiel16:59 For thus saith the Lord GOD; I will even deal with thee as thou hast done
Ezekiel16:60 Nevertheless I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth
Ezekiel16:61 Then thou shalt remember thy ways
Ezekiel16:62 And I will establish my covenant with thee; and thou shalt know that I am the LORD:
Ezekiel16:63 That thou mayest remember

Ezekiel27:2 Now
Ezekiel27:3 And say unto Tyrus
Ezekiel27:4 Thy borders are in the midst of the seas
Ezekiel27:5 They have made all thy ship boards of fir trees of Senir: they have taken cedars from Lebanon to make masts for thee.
Ezekiel27:6 Of the oaks of Bashan have they made thine oars; the company of the Ashurites have made thy benches of ivory
Ezekiel27:7 Fine linen with broidered work from Egypt was that which thou spreadest forth to be thy sail; blue and purple from the isles of Elishah was that which covered thee.
Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Zidon and Arvad were thy mariners: thy wise men
Ezekiel27:9 The ancients of Gebal and the wise men thereof were in thee thy calkers: all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in thee to occupy thy merchandise.
Ezekiel27:10 They of Persia and of Lud and of Phut were in thine army
Ezekiel27:11 The men of Arvad with thine army were upon thy walls round about
Ezekiel27:12 Tarshish was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of all kind of riches; with silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 They of the house of Togarmah traded in thy fairs with horses and horsemen and mules.
Ezekiel27:15 The men of Dedan were thy merchants; many isles were the merchandise of thine hand: they brought thee for a present horns of ivory and ebony.
Ezekiel27:16 Syria was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of the wares of thy making: they occupied in thy fairs with emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus was thy merchant in the multitude of the wares of thy making
Ezekiel27:19 Dan also and Javan going to and fro occupied in thy fairs: bright iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan was thy merchant in precious clothes for chariots.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia
Ezekiel27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 These were thy merchants in all sorts of things
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish did sing of thee in thy market: and thou wast replenished
Ezekiel27:26 Thy rowers have brought thee into great waters: the east wind hath broken thee in the midst of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Thy riches
Ezekiel27:28 The suburbs shall shake at the sound of the cry of thy pilots.
Ezekiel27:29 And all that handle the oar
Ezekiel27:30 And shall cause their voice to be heard against thee
Ezekiel27:31 And they shall make themselves utterly bald for thee
Ezekiel27:32 And in their wailing they shall take up a lamentation for thee
Ezekiel27:33 When thy wares went forth out of the seas
Ezekiel27:34 In the time when thou shalt be broken by the seas in the depths of the waters thy merchandise and all thy company in the midst of thee shall fall.
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the isles shall be astonished at thee
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the people shall hiss at thee; thou shalt be a terror

Ezekiel31:14 To the end that none of all the trees by the waters exalt themselves for their height

This is Pharaoh and all his multitude

Ezekiel40:1 In the five and twentieth year of our captivity
Ezekiel40:2 In the visions of God brought he me into the land of Israel
Ezekiel40:3 And he brought me thither
Ezekiel40:4 And the man said unto me
Ezekiel40:5 And behold a wall on the outside of the house round about
Ezekiel40:6 Then came he unto the gate which looketh toward the east
Ezekiel40:7 And every little chamber was one reed long
Ezekiel40:8 He measured also the porch of the gate within
Ezekiel40:9 Then measured he the porch of the gate
Ezekiel40:10 And the little chambers of the gate eastward were three on this side
Ezekiel40:11 And he measured the breadth of the entry of the gate
Ezekiel40:12 The space also before the little chambers was one cubit on this side
Ezekiel40:13 He measured then the gate from the roof of one little chamber to the roof of another: the breadth was five and twenty cubits
Ezekiel40:14 He made also posts of threescore cubits
Ezekiel40:15 And from the face of the gate of the entrance unto the face of the porch of the inner gate were fifty cubits.
Ezekiel40:16 And there were narrow windows to the little chambers
Ezekiel40:17 Then brought he me into the outward court
Ezekiel40:18 And the pavement by the side of the gates over against the length of the gates was the lower pavement.
Ezekiel40:19 Then he measured the breadth from the forefront of the lower gate unto the forefront of the inner court without
Ezekiel40:20 And the gate of the outward court that looked toward the north
Ezekiel40:21 And the little chambers thereof were three on this side and three on that side; and the posts thereof and the arches thereof were after the measure of the first gate: the length thereof was fifty cubits
Ezekiel40:22 And their windows
Ezekiel40:23 And the gate of the inner court was over against the gate toward the north
Ezekiel40:24 After that he brought me toward the south
Ezekiel40:25 And there were windows in it and in the arches thereof round about
Ezekiel40:26 And there were seven steps to go up to it
Ezekiel40:27 And there was a gate in the inner court toward the south: and he measured from gate to gate toward the south an hundred cubits.
Ezekiel40:28 And he brought me to the inner court by the south gate: and he measured the south gate according to these measures;
Ezekiel40:29 And the little chambers thereof
Ezekiel40:30 And the arches round about were five and twenty cubits long
Ezekiel40:31 And the arches thereof were toward the utter court; and palm trees were upon the posts thereof: and the going up to it had eight steps.
Ezekiel40:32 And he brought me into the inner court toward the east: and he measured the gate according to these measures.
Ezekiel40:33 And the little chambers thereof
Ezekiel40:34 And the arches thereof were toward the outward court; and palm trees were upon the posts thereof
Ezekiel40:35 And he brought me to the north gate
Ezekiel40:36 The little chambers thereof
Ezekiel40:37 And the posts thereof were toward the utter court; and palm trees were upon the posts thereof
Ezekiel40:38 And the chambers and the entries thereof were by the posts of the gates
Ezekiel40:39 And in the porch of the gate were two tables on this side
Ezekiel40:40 And at the side without
Ezekiel40:41 Four tables were on this side
Ezekiel40:42 And the four tables were of hewn stone for the burnt offering
Ezekiel40:43 And within were hooks
Ezekiel40:44 And without the inner gate were the chambers of the singers in the inner court
Ezekiel40:45 And he said unto me
Ezekiel40:46 And the chamber whose prospect is toward the north is for the priests
Ezekiel40:47 So he measured the court
Ezekiel40:48 And he brought me to the porch of the house
Ezekiel40:49 The length of the porch was twenty cubits

Ezekiel41:4 So he measured the length thereof

Ezekiel43:2 And

Ezekiel43:6 And I heard him speaking unto me out of the house; and the man stood by me.

Ezekiel43:14 And from the bottom upon the ground even to the lower settle shall be two cubits

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah came Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon unto Jerusalem
Daniel1:2 And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 And the king spake unto Ashpenaz the master of his eunuchs
Daniel1:4 Children in whom was no blemish
Daniel1:5 And the king appointed them a daily provision of the king's meat
Daniel1:6 Now among these were of the children of Judah
Daniel1:7 Unto whom the prince of the eunuchs gave names: for he gave unto Daniel the name of Belteshazzar; and to Hananiah
Daniel1:8 But Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the portion of the king's meat
Daniel1:9 Now God had brought Daniel into favour and tender love with the prince of the eunuchs.
Daniel1:10 And the prince of the eunuchs said unto Daniel
Daniel1:11 Then said Daniel to Melzar
Daniel1:12 Prove thy servants
Daniel1:13 Then let our countenances be looked upon before thee
Daniel1:14 So he consented to them in this matter
Daniel1:15 And at the end of ten days their countenances appeared fairer and fatter in flesh than all the children which did eat the portion of the king's meat.
Daniel1:16 Thus Melzar took away the portion of their meat
Daniel1:17 As for these four children
Daniel1:18 Now at the end of the days that the king had said he should bring them in
Daniel1:19 And the king communed with them; and among them all was found none like Daniel
Daniel1:20 And in all matters of wisdom and understanding
Daniel1:21 And Daniel continued even unto the first year of king Cyrus.
Daniel2:1 And in the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar Nebuchadnezzar dreamed dreams
Daniel2:2 Then the king commanded to call the magicians
Daniel2:3 And the king said unto them
Daniel2:4 Then spake the Chaldeans to the king in Syriack
Daniel2:5 The king answered and said to the Chaldeans
Daniel2:6 But if ye shew the dream
Daniel2:7 They answered again and said
Daniel2:8 The king answered and said
Daniel2:9 But if ye will not make known unto me the dream
Daniel2:10 The Chaldeans answered before the king
Daniel2:11 And it is a rare thing that the king requireth
Daniel2:12 For this cause the king was angry and very furious
Daniel2:13 And the decree went forth that the wise men should be slain; and they sought Daniel and his fellows to be slain.
Daniel2:14 Then Daniel answered with counsel and wisdom to Arioch the captain of the king's guard
Daniel2:15 He answered and said to Arioch the king's captain
Daniel2:16 Then Daniel went in
Daniel2:17 Then Daniel went to his house
Daniel2:18 That they would desire mercies of the God of heaven concerning this secret; that Daniel and his fellows should not perish with the rest of the wise men of Babylon.
Daniel2:19 Then was the secret revealed unto Daniel in a night vision. Then Daniel blessed the God of heaven.
Daniel2:20 Daniel answered and said
Daniel2:21 And he changeth the times and the seasons: he removeth kings
Daniel2:22 He revealeth the deep and secret things: he knoweth what is in the darkness
Daniel2:23 I thank thee
Daniel2:24 Therefore Daniel went in unto Arioch
Daniel2:25 Then Arioch brought in Daniel before the king in haste
Daniel2:26 The king answered and said to Daniel
Daniel2:27 Daniel answered in the presence of the king
Daniel2:28 But there is a God in heaven that revealeth secrets
Daniel2:29 As for thee
Daniel2:30 But as for me
Daniel2:31 Thou
Daniel2:32 This image's head was of fine gold
Daniel2:33 His legs of iron
Daniel2:34 Thou sawest till that a stone was cut out without hands
Daniel2:35 Then was the iron
Daniel2:36 This is the dream; and we will tell the interpretation thereof before the king.
Daniel2:37 Thou
Daniel2:38 And wheresoever the children of men dwell
Daniel2:39 And after thee shall arise another kingdom inferior to thee
Daniel2:40 And the fourth kingdom shall be strong as iron: forasmuch as iron breaketh in pieces and subdueth all things: and as iron that breaketh all these
Daniel2:41 And whereas thou sawest the feet and toes
Daniel2:42 And as the toes of the feet were part of iron
Daniel2:43 And whereas thou sawest iron mixed with miry clay

Daniel2:45 Forasmuch as thou sawest that the stone was cut out of the mountain without hands

Daniel2:47 The king answered unto Daniel
Daniel2:48 Then the king made Daniel a great man
Daniel2:49 Then Daniel requested of the king

Daniel8:23 And in the latter time of their kingdom
Daniel8:24 And his power shall be mighty
Daniel8:25 And through his policy also he shall cause craft to prosper in his hand; and he shall magnify himself in his heart
Daniel8:26 And the vision of the evening and the morning which was told is true: wherefore shut thou up the vision; for it shall be for many days.
Daniel8:27 And I Daniel fainted
Daniel9:1 In the first year of Darius the son of Ahasuerus
Daniel9:2 In the first year of his reign I Daniel understood by books the number of the years
Daniel9:3 And I set my face unto the Lord God
Daniel9:4 And I prayed unto the LORD my God
Daniel9:5 We have sinned
Daniel9:6 Neither have we hearkened unto thy servants the prophets
Daniel9:7 O LORD
Daniel9:8 O Lord
Daniel9:9 To the Lord our God belong mercies and forgivenesses
Daniel9:10 Neither have we obeyed the voice of the LORD our God
Daniel9:11 Yea
Daniel9:12 And he hath confirmed his words
Daniel9:13 As it is written in the law of Moses
Daniel9:14 Therefore hath the LORD watched upon the evil
Daniel9:15 And now
Daniel9:16 O LORD
Daniel9:17 Now therefore
Daniel9:18 O my God
Daniel9:19 O Lord
Daniel9:20 And whiles I was speaking
Daniel9:21 Yea
Daniel9:22 And he informed me
Daniel9:23 At the beginning of thy supplications the commandment came forth
Daniel9:24 Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people and upon thy holy city
Daniel9:25 Know therefore and understand
Daniel9:26 And after threescore and two weeks shall Messiah be cut off
Daniel9:27 And he shall confirm the covenant with many for one week: and in the midst of the week he shall cause the sacrifice and the oblation to cease
Daniel10:1 In the third year of Cyrus king of Persia a thing was revealed unto Daniel
Daniel10:2 In those days I Daniel was mourning three full weeks.
Daniel10:3 I ate no pleasant bread
Daniel10:4 And in the four and twentieth day of the first month
Daniel10:5 Then I lifted up mine eyes
Daniel10:6 His body also was like the beryl
Daniel10:7 And I Daniel alone saw the vision: for the men that were with me saw not the vision; but a great quaking fell upon them
Daniel10:8 Therefore I was left alone
Daniel10:9 Yet heard I the voice of his words: and when I heard the voice of his words
Daniel10:10 And
Daniel10:11 And he said unto me
Daniel10:12 Then said he unto me
Daniel10:13 But the prince of the kingdom of Persia withstood me one and twenty days: but
Daniel10:14 Now I am come to make thee understand what shall befall thy people in the latter days: for yet the vision is for many days.
Daniel10:15 And when he had spoken such words unto me
Daniel10:16 And
Daniel10:17 For how can the servant of this my lord talk with this my lord? for as for me
Daniel10:18 Then there came again and touched me one like the appearance of a man
Daniel10:19 And said
Daniel10:20 Then said he
Daniel10:21 But I will shew thee that which is noted in the scripture of truth: and there is none that holdeth with me in these things
Daniel11:1 Also I in the first year of Darius the Mede
Daniel11:2 And now will I shew thee the truth. Behold
Daniel11:3 And a mighty king shall stand up
Daniel11:4 And when he shall stand up
Daniel11:5 And the king of the south shall be strong
Daniel11:6 And in the end of years they shall join themselves together; for the king's daughter of the south shall come to the king of the north to make an agreement: but she shall not retain the power of the arm; neither shall he stand
Daniel11:7 But out of a branch of her roots shall one stand up in his estate
Daniel11:8 And shall also carry captives into Egypt their gods
Daniel11:9 So the king of the south shall come into his kingdom
Daniel11:10 But his sons shall be stirred up
Daniel11:11 And the king of the south shall be moved with choler
Daniel11:12 And when he hath taken away the multitude
Daniel11:13 For the king of the north shall return
Daniel11:14 And in those times there shall many stand up against the king of the south: also the robbers of thy people shall exalt themselves to establish the vision; but they shall fall.
Daniel11:15 So the king of the north shall come
Daniel11:16 But he that cometh against him shall do according to his own will
Daniel11:17 He shall also set his face to enter with the strength of his whole kingdom
Daniel11:18 After this shall he turn his face unto the isles
Daniel11:19 Then he shall turn his face toward the fort of his own land: but he shall stumble and fall
Daniel11:20 Then shall stand up in his estate a raiser of taxes in the glory of the kingdom: but within few days he shall be destroyed
Daniel11:21 And in his estate shall stand up a vile person
Daniel11:22 And with the arms of a flood shall they be overflown from before him
Daniel11:23 And after the league made with him he shall work deceitfully: for he shall come up
Daniel11:24 He shall enter peaceably even upon the fattest places of the province; and he shall do that which his fathers have not done
Daniel11:25 And he shall stir up his power and his courage against the king of the south with a great army; and the king of the south shall be stirred up to battle with a very great and mighty army; but he shall not stand: for they shall forecast devices against him.
Daniel11:26 Yea
Daniel11:27 And both of these kings' hearts shall be to do mischief
Daniel11:28 Then shall he return into his land with great riches; and his heart shall be against the holy covenant; and he shall do exploits
Daniel11:29 At the time appointed he shall return
Daniel11:30 For the ships of Chittim shall come against him: therefore he shall be grieved
Daniel11:31 And arms shall stand on his part
Daniel11:32 And such as do wickedly against the covenant shall he corrupt by flatteries: but the people that do know their God shall be strong
Daniel11:33 And they that understand among the people shall instruct many: yet they shall fall by the sword
Daniel11:34 Now when they shall fall
Daniel11:35 And some of them of understanding shall fall
Daniel11:36 And the king shall do according to his will; and he shall exalt himself
Daniel11:37 Neither shall he regard the God of his fathers
Daniel11:38 But in his estate shall he honour the God of forces: and a god whom his fathers knew not shall he honour with gold
Daniel11:39 Thus shall he do in the most strong holds with a strange god
Daniel11:40 And at the time of the end shall the king of the south push at him: and the king of the north shall come against him like a whirlwind
Daniel11:41 He shall enter also into the glorious land
Daniel11:42 He shall stretch forth his hand also upon the countries: and the land of Egypt shall not escape.
Daniel11:43 But he shall have power over the treasures of gold and of silver
Daniel11:44 But tidings out of the east and out of the north shall trouble him: therefore he shall go forth with great fury to destroy
Daniel11:45 And he shall plant the tabernacles of his palace between the seas in the glorious holy mountain; yet he shall come to his end
Daniel12:1 And at that time shall Michael stand up
Daniel12:2 And many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake
Daniel12:3 And they that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament; and they that turn many to righteousness as the stars for ever and ever.
Daniel12:4 But thou
Daniel12:5 Then I Daniel looked
Daniel12:6 And one said to the man clothed in linen
Daniel12:7 And I heard the man clothed in linen
Daniel12:8 And I heard
Daniel12:9 And he said
Daniel12:10 Many shall be purified
Daniel12:11 And from the time that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away
Daniel12:12 Blessed is he that waiteth
Daniel12:13 But go thou thy way till the end be: for thou shalt rest
Hosea1:1 The word of the LORD that came unto Hosea
Hosea1:2 The beginning of the word of the LORD by Hosea. And the LORD said to Hosea

Hosea1:4 And the LORD said unto him
Hosea1:5 And it shall come to pass at that day
Hosea1:6 And she conceived again
Hosea1:7 But I will have mercy upon the house of Judah
Hosea1:8 Now when she had weaned Loruhamah
Hosea1:9 Then said God
Hosea1:10 Yet the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea
Hosea1:11 Then shall the children of Judah and the children of Israel be gathered together
Hosea2:1 Say ye unto your brethren
Hosea2:2 Plead with your mother
Hosea2:3 Lest I strip her naked
Hosea2:4 And I will not have mercy upon her children; for they be the children of whoredoms.
Hosea2:5 For their mother hath played the harlot: she that conceived them hath done shamefully: for she said
Hosea2:6 Therefore
Hosea2:7 And she shall follow after her lovers
Hosea2:8 For she did not know that I gave her corn
Hosea2:9 Therefore will I return
Hosea2:10 And now will I discover her lewdness in the sight of her lovers
Hosea2:11 I will also cause all her mirth to cease
Hosea2:12 And I will destroy her vines and her fig trees
Hosea2:13 And I will visit upon her the days of Baalim
Hosea2:14 Therefore
Hosea2:15 And I will give her her vineyards from thence
Hosea2:16 And it shall be at that day
Hosea2:17 For I will take away the names of Baalim out of her mouth
Hosea2:18 And in that day will I make a covenant for them with the beasts of the field and with the fowls of heaven
Hosea2:19 And I will betroth thee unto me for ever; yea
Hosea2:20 I will even betroth thee unto me in faithfulness: and thou shalt know the LORD.
Hosea2:21 And it shall come to pass in that day
Hosea2:22 And the earth shall hear the corn
Hosea2:23 And I will sow her unto me in the earth; and I will have mercy upon her that had not obtained mercy; and I will say to them which were not my people

Hosea3:2 So I bought her to me for fifteen pieces of silver
Hosea3:3 And I said unto her
Hosea3:4 For the children of Israel shall abide many days without a king
Hosea3:5 Afterward shall the children of Israel return
Hosea4:1 Hear the word of the LORD
Hosea4:2 By swearing
Hosea4:3 Therefore shall the land mourn
Hosea4:4 Yet let no man strive
Hosea4:5 Therefore shalt thou fall in the day

Hosea4:8 They eat up the sin of my people
Hosea4:9 And there shall be
Hosea4:10 For they shall eat
Hosea4:11 Whoredom and wine and new wine take away the heart.
Hosea4:12 My people ask counsel at their stocks
Hosea4:13 They sacrifice upon the tops of the mountains
Hosea4:14 I will not punish your daughters when they commit whoredom
Hosea4:15 Though thou
Hosea4:16 For Israel slideth back as a backsliding heifer: now the LORD will feed them as a lamb in a large place.

Hosea4:18 Their drink is sour: they have committed whoredom continually: her rulers with shame do love
Hosea4:19 The wind hath bound her up in her wings
Hosea5:1 Hear ye this
Hosea5:2 And the revolters are profound to make slaughter
Hosea5:3 I know Ephraim
Hosea5:4 They will not frame their doings to turn unto their God: for the spirit of whoredoms is in the midst of them
Hosea5:5 And the pride of Israel doth testify to his face: therefore shall Israel and Ephraim fall in their iniquity: Judah also shall fall with them.
Hosea5:6 They shall go with their flocks and with their herds to seek the LORD; but they shall not find him; he hath withdrawn himself from them.
Hosea5:7 They have dealt treacherously against the LORD: for they have begotten strange children: now shall a month devour them with their portions.
Hosea5:8 Blow ye the cornet in Gibeah
Hosea5:9 Ephraim shall be desolate in the day of rebuke: among the tribes of Israel have I made known that which shall surely be.
Hosea5:10 The princes of Judah were like them that remove the bound: therefore I will pour out my wrath upon them like water.
Hosea5:11 Ephraim is oppressed and broken in judgment
Hosea5:12 Therefore will I be unto Ephraim as a moth
Hosea5:13 When Ephraim saw his sickness
Hosea5:14 For I will be unto Ephraim as a lion
Hosea5:15 I will go and return to my place

Hosea6:2 After two days will he revive us: in the third day he will raise us up
Hosea6:3 Then shall we know
Hosea6:4 O Ephraim
Hosea6:5 Therefore have I hewed them by the prophets; I have slain them by the words of my mouth: and thy judgments are as the light that goeth forth.
Hosea6:6 For I desired mercy
Hosea6:7 But they like men have transgressed the covenant: there have they dealt treacherously against me.
Hosea6:8 Gilead is a city of them that work iniquity
Hosea6:9 And as troops of robbers wait for a man
Hosea6:10 I have seen an horrible thing in the house of Israel: there is the whoredom of Ephraim
Hosea6:11 Also
Hosea7:1 When I would have healed Israel
Hosea7:2 And they consider not in their hearts that I remember all their wickedness: now their own doings have beset them about; they are before my face.
Hosea7:3 They make the king glad with their wickedness
Hosea7:4 They are all adulterers
Hosea7:5 In the day of our king the princes have made him sick with bottles of wine; he stretched out his hand with scorners.

Hosea7:7 They are all hot as an oven
Hosea7:8 Ephraim
Hosea7:9 Strangers have devoured his strength
Hosea7:10 And the pride of Israel testifieth to his face: and they do not return to the LORD their God

Hosea7:15 Though I have bound and strengthened their arms

Hosea8:5 Thy calf

Joel1:3 Tell ye your children of it

Joel1:6 For a nation is come up upon my land

Joel2:5 Like the noise of chariots on the tops of mountains shall they leap
Joel2:6 Before their face the people shall be much pained: all faces shall gather blackness.

Joel2:8 Neither shall one thrust another; they shall walk every one in his path: and when they fall upon the sword

Amos1:1 The words of Amos
Amos1:2 And he said
Amos1:3 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Damascus
Amos1:4 But I will send a fire into the house of Hazael
Amos1:5 I will break also the bar of Damascus
Amos1:6 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Gaza
Amos1:7 But I will send a fire on the wall of Gaza
Amos1:8 And I will cut off the inhabitant from Ashdod
Amos1:9 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Tyrus
Amos1:10 But I will send a fire on the wall of Tyrus
Amos1:11 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Edom
Amos1:12 But I will send a fire upon Teman
Amos1:13 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of the children of Ammon
Amos1:14 But I will kindle a fire in the wall of Rabbah
Amos1:15 And their king shall go into captivity
Amos2:1 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Moab
Amos2:2 But I will send a fire upon Moab
Amos2:3 And I will cut off the judge from the midst thereof
Amos2:4 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Judah
Amos2:5 But I will send a fire upon Judah
Amos2:6 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Israel
Amos2:7 That pant after the dust of the earth on the head of the poor
Amos2:8 And they lay themselves down upon clothes laid to pledge by every altar
Amos2:9 Yet destroyed I the Amorite before them
Amos2:10 Also I brought you up from the land of Egypt
Amos2:11 And I raised up of your sons for prophets
Amos2:12 But ye gave the Nazarites wine to drink; and commanded the prophets
Amos2:13 Behold
Amos2:14 Therefore the flight shall perish from the swift
Amos2:15 Neither shall he stand that handleth the bow; and he that is swift of foot shall not deliver himself: neither shall he that rideth the horse deliver himself.
Amos2:16 And he that is courageous among the mighty shall flee away naked in that day
Amos3:1 Hear this word that the LORD hath spoken against you
Amos3:2 You only have I known of all the families of the earth: therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.
Amos3:3 Can two walk together
Amos3:4 Will a lion roar in the forest
Amos3:5 Can a bird fall in a snare upon the earth
Amos3:6 Shall a trumpet be blown in the city
Amos3:7 Surely the Lord GOD will do nothing
Amos3:8 The lion hath roared
Amos3:9 Publish in the palaces at Ashdod
Amos3:10 For they know not to do right
Amos3:11 Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; An adversary there shall be even round about the land; and he shall bring down thy strength from thee
Amos3:12 Thus saith the LORD; As the shepherd taketh out of the mouth of the lion two legs
Amos3:13 Hear ye
Amos3:14 That in the day that I shall visit the transgressions of Israel upon him I will also visit the altars of Bethel: and the horns of the altar shall be cut off
Amos3:15 And I will smite the winter house with the summer house; and the houses of ivory shall perish

Amos4:2 The Lord GOD hath sworn by his holiness
Amos4:3 And ye shall go out at the breaches
Amos4:4 Come to Bethel
Amos4:5 And offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving with leaven
Amos4:6 And I also have given you cleanness of teeth in all your cities
Amos4:7 And also I have withholden the rain from you
Amos4:8 So two or three cities wandered unto one city
Amos4:9 I have smitten you with blasting and mildew: when your gardens and your vineyards and your fig trees and your olive trees increased
Amos4:10 I have sent among you the pestilence after the manner of Egypt: your young men have I slain with the sword
Amos4:11 I have overthrown some of you
Amos4:12 Therefore thus will I do unto thee
Amos4:13 For
Amos5:1 Hear ye this word which I take up against you
Amos5:2 The virgin of Israel is fallen; she shall no more rise: she is forsaken upon her land; there is none to raise her up.
Amos5:3 For thus saith the Lord GOD; The city that went out by a thousand shall leave an hundred
Amos5:4 For thus saith the LORD unto the house of Israel
Amos5:5 But seek not Bethel
Amos5:6 Seek the LORD
Amos5:7 Ye who turn judgment to wormwood
Amos5:8 Seek him that maketh the seven stars and Orion
Amos5:9 That strengtheneth the spoiled against the strong
Amos5:10 They hate him that rebuketh in the gate
Amos5:11 Forasmuch therefore as your treading is upon the poor
Amos5:12 For I know your manifold transgressions and your mighty sins: they afflict the just
Amos5:13 Therefore the prudent shall keep silence in that time; for it is an evil time.
Amos5:14 Seek good
Amos5:15 Hate the evil
Amos5:16 Therefore the LORD
Amos5:17 And in all vineyards shall be wailing: for I will pass through thee
Amos5:18 Woe unto you that desire the day of the LORD! to what end is it for you? the day of the LORD is darkness
Amos5:19 As if a man did flee from a lion
Amos5:20 Shall not the day of the LORD be darkness
Amos5:21 I hate
Amos5:22 Though ye offer me burnt offerings and your meat offerings
Amos5:23 Take thou away from me the noise of thy songs; for I will not hear the melody of thy viols.
Amos5:24 But let judgment run down as waters
Amos5:25 Have ye offered unto me sacrifices and offerings in the wilderness forty years
Amos5:26 But ye have borne the tabernacle of your Moloch and Chiun your images
Amos5:27 Therefore will I cause you to go into captivity beyond Damascus

Amos6:2 Pass ye unto Calneh
Amos6:3 Ye that put far away the evil day

Amos6:6 That drink wine in bowls

Obadiah1:1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus saith the Lord GOD concerning Edom; We have heard a rumour from the LORD
Obadiah1:2 Behold
Obadiah1:3 The pride of thine heart hath deceived thee
Obadiah1:4 Though thou exalt thyself as the eagle
Obadiah1:5 If thieves came to thee
Obadiah1:6 How are the things of Esau searched out! how are his hidden things sought up!
Obadiah1:7 All the men of thy confederacy have brought thee even to the border: the men that were at peace with thee have deceived thee
Obadiah1:8 Shall I not in that day
Obadiah1:9 And thy mighty men
Obadiah1:10 For thy violence against thy brother Jacob shame shall cover thee
Obadiah1:11 In the day that thou stoodest on the other side
Obadiah1:12 But thou shouldest not have looked on the day of thy brother in the day that he became a stranger; neither shouldest thou have rejoiced over the children of Judah in the day of their destruction; neither shouldest thou have spoken proudly in the day of distress.
Obadiah1:13 Thou shouldest not have entered into the gate of my people in the day of their calamity; yea
Obadiah1:14 Neither shouldest thou have stood in the crossway
Obadiah1:15 For the day of the LORD is near upon all the heathen: as thou hast done
Obadiah1:16 For as ye have drunk upon my holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But upon mount Zion shall be deliverance
Obadiah1:18 And the house of Jacob shall be a fire
Obadiah1:19 And they of the south shall possess the mount of Esau; and they of the plain the Philistines: and they shall possess the fields of Ephraim
Obadiah1:20 And the captivity of this host of the children of Israel shall possess that of the Canaanites
Obadiah1:21 And saviours shall come up on mount Zion to judge the mount of Esau; and the kingdom shall be the LORD's.

Jonah1:5 Then the mariners were afraid
Jonah1:6 So the shipmaster came to him
Jonah1:7 And they said every one to his fellow

Jonah1:9 And he said unto them

Jonah1:11 Then said they unto him
Jonah1:12 And he said unto them

Jonah1:14 Wherefore they cried unto the LORD
Jonah1:15 So they look up Jonah
Jonah1:16 Then the men feared the LORD exceedingly
Jonah1:17 Now the LORD had prepared a great fish to swallow up Jonah. And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.

Jonah2:8 They that observe lying vanities forsake their own mercy.
Jonah2:9 But I will sacrifice unto thee with the voice of thanksgiving; I will pay that that I have vowed. Salvation is of the LORD.

Matthew1:1 The book of the generation of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham begat Isaac; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat Judas and his brethren;
Matthew1:3 And Judas begat Phares and Zara of Thamar; and Phares begat Esrom; and Esrom begat Aram;
Matthew1:4 And Aram begat Aminadab; and Aminadab begat Naasson; and Naasson begat Salmon;
Matthew1:5 And Salmon begat Booz of Rachab; and Booz begat Obed of Ruth; and Obed begat Jesse;
Matthew1:6 And Jesse begat David the king; and David the king begat Solomon of her that had been the wife of Urias;
Matthew1:7 And Solomon begat Roboam; and Roboam begat Abia; and Abia begat Asa;
Matthew1:8 And Asa begat Josaphat; and Josaphat begat Joram; and Joram begat Ozias;
Matthew1:9 And Ozias begat Joatham; and Joatham begat Achaz; and Achaz begat Ezekias;
Matthew1:10 And Ezekias begat Manasses; and Manasses begat Amon; and Amon begat Josias;
Matthew1:11 And Josias begat Jechonias and his brethren
Matthew1:12 And after they were brought to Babylon
Matthew1:13 And Zorobabel begat Abiud; and Abiud begat Eliakim; and Eliakim begat Azor;
Matthew1:14 And Azor begat Sadoc; and Sadoc begat Achim; and Achim begat Eliud;
Matthew1:15 And Eliud begat Eleazar; and Eleazar begat Matthan; and Matthan begat Jacob;
Matthew1:16 And Jacob begat Joseph the husband of Mary
Matthew1:17 So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David until the carrying away into Babylon are fourteen generations; and from the carrying away into Babylon unto Christ are fourteen generations.

Matthew1:22 Now all this was done
Matthew1:23 Behold
Matthew1:24 Then Joseph being raised from sleep did as the angel of the Lord had bidden him
Matthew1:25 And knew her not till she had brought forth her firstborn son: and he called his name JESUS.

Matthew6:30 Wherefore
Matthew6:31 Therefore take no thought
Matthew6:32 (For after all these things do the Gentiles seek:) for your heavenly Father knoweth that ye have need of all these things.
Matthew6:33 But seek ye first the kingdom of God
Matthew6:34 Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.

Matthew23:1 Then spake Jesus to the multitude
Matthew23:2 Saying The scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses' seat:
Matthew23:3 All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe
Matthew23:4 For they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne

Matthew23:9 And call no man your father upon the earth: for one is your Father

Matthew24:7 For nation shall rise against nation

Matthew24:10 And then shall many be offended
Matthew24:11 And many false prophets shall rise
Matthew24:12 And because iniquity shall abound
Matthew24:13 But he that shall endure unto the end
Matthew24:14 And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.

Matthew24:17 Let him which is on the housetop not come down to take any thing out of his house:
Matthew24:18 Neither let him which is in the field return back to take his clothes.

Matthew24:51 And shall cut him asunder

Matthew25:43 I was a stranger

John1:6 There was a man sent from God
John1:7 The same came for a witness
John1:8 He was not that Light
John1:9 That was the true Light
John1:10 He was in the world
John1:11 He came unto his own
John1:12 But as many as received him
John1:13 Which were born
John1:14 And the Word was made flesh
John1:15 John bare witness of him
John1:16 And of his fulness have all we received
John1:17 For the law was given by Moses
John1:18 No man hath seen God at any time
John1:19 And this is the record of John
John1:20 And he confessed
John1:21 And they asked him
John1:22 Then said they unto him
John1:23 He said
John1:24 And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.
John1:25 And they asked him
John1:26 John answered them
John1:27 He it is
John1:28 These things were done in Bethabara beyond Jordan
John1:29 The next day John seeth Jesus coming unto him
John1:30 This is he of whom I said
John1:31 And I knew him not: but that he should be made manifest to Israel
John1:32 And John bare record
John1:33 And I knew him not: but he that sent me to baptize with water
John1:34 And I saw
John1:35 Again the next day after John stood
John1:36 And looking upon Jesus as he walked
John1:37 And the two disciples heard him speak
John1:38 Then Jesus turned
John1:39 He saith unto them
John1:40 One of the two which heard John speak
John1:41 He first findeth his own brother Simon
John1:42 And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him
John1:43 The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee
John1:44 Now Philip was of Bethsaida
John1:45 Philip findeth Nathanael
John1:46 And Nathanael said unto him
John1:47 Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him
John1:48 Nathanael saith unto him
John1:49 Nathanael answered and saith unto him
John1:50 Jesus answered and said unto him
John1:51 And he saith unto him
John2:1 And the third day there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there:
John2:2 And both Jesus was called
John2:3 And when they wanted wine
John2:4 Jesus saith unto her
John2:5 His mother saith unto the servants
John2:6 And there were set there six waterpots of stone
John2:7 Jesus saith unto them
John2:8 And he saith unto them
John2:9 When the ruler of the feast had tasted the water that was made wine
John2:10 And saith unto him
John2:11 This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee
John2:12 After this he went down to Capernaum
John2:13 And the Jews' passover was at hand
John2:14 And found in the temple those that sold oxen and sheep and doves
John2:15 And when he had made a scourge of small cords
John2:16 And said unto them that sold doves
John2:17 And his disciples remembered that it was written
John2:18 Then answered the Jews and said unto him
John2:19 Jesus answered and said unto them
John2:20 Then said the Jews
John2:21 But he spake of the temple of his body.
John2:22 When therefore he was risen from the dead
John2:23 Now when he was in Jerusalem at the passover
John2:24 But Jesus did not commit himself unto them
John2:25 And needed not that any should testify of man: for he knew what was in man.
John3:1 There was a man of the Pharisees
John3:2 The same came to Jesus by night
John3:3 Jesus answered and said unto him
John3:4 Nicodemus saith unto him
John3:5 Jesus answered
John3:6 That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
John3:7 Marvel not that I said unto thee
John3:8 The wind bloweth where it listeth
John3:9 Nicodemus answered and said unto him
John3:10 Jesus answered and said unto him
John3:11 Verily
John3:12 If I have told you earthly things
John3:13 And no man hath ascended up to heaven
John3:14 And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness
John3:15 That whosoever believeth in him should not perish
John3:16 For God so loved the world
John3:17 For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.
John3:18 He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already
John3:19 And this is the condemnation
John3:20 For every one that doeth evil hateth the light
John3:21 But he that doeth truth cometh to the light
John3:22 After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judaea; and there he tarried with them
John3:23 And John also was baptizing in Aenon near to Salim
John3:24 For John was not yet cast into prison.
John3:25 Then there arose a question between some of John's disciples and the Jews about purifying.
John3:26 And they came unto John
John3:27 John answered and said
John3:28 Ye yourselves bear me witness
John3:29 He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom
John3:30 He must increase
John3:31 He that cometh from above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly
John3:32 And what he hath seen and heard
John3:33 He that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.
John3:34 For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.
John3:35 The Father loveth the Son
John3:36 He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
John4:1 When therefore the LORD knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John
John4:2 (Though Jesus himself baptized not
John4:3 He left Judaea
John4:4 And he must needs go through Samaria.
John4:5 Then cometh he to a city of Samaria
John4:6 Now Jacob's well was there. Jesus therefore
John4:7 There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her
John4:8 (For his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat.)
John4:9 Then saith the woman of Samaria unto him
John4:10 Jesus answered and said unto her
John4:11 The woman saith unto him
John4:12 Art thou greater than our father Jacob
John4:13 Jesus answered and said unto her
John4:14 But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.
John4:15 The woman saith unto him
John4:16 Jesus saith unto her
John4:17 The woman answered and said
John4:18 For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.
John4:19 The woman saith unto him
John4:20 Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say
John4:21 Jesus saith unto her
John4:22 Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.
John4:23 But the hour cometh
John4:24 God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.
John4:25 The woman saith unto him
John4:26 Jesus saith unto her
John4:27 And upon this came his disciples
John4:28 The woman then left her waterpot
John4:29 Come
John4:30 Then they went out of the city
John4:31 In the mean while his disciples prayed him
John4:32 But he said unto them
John4:33 Therefore said the disciples one to another
John4:34 Jesus saith unto them
John4:35 Say not ye
John4:36 And he that reapeth receiveth wages
John4:37 And herein is that saying true
John4:38 I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured
John4:39 And many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman
John4:40 So when the Samaritans were come unto him
John4:41 And many more believed because of his own word;
John4:42 And said unto the woman
John4:43 Now after two days he departed thence
John4:44 For Jesus himself testified
John4:45 Then when he was come into Galilee
John4:46 So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee
John4:47 When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judaea into Galilee
John4:48 Then said Jesus unto him

John4:50 Jesus saith unto him
John4:51 And as he was now going down
John4:52 Then enquired he of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him
John4:53 So the father knew that it was at the same hour
John4:54 This is again the second miracle that Jesus did
John5:1 After this there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
John5:2 Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep market a pool
John5:3 In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk
John5:4 For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool
John5:5 And a certain man was there
John5:6 When Jesus saw him lie
John5:7 The impotent man answered him
John5:8 Jesus saith unto him
John5:9 And immediately the man was made whole
John5:10 The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured
John5:11 He answered them
John5:12 Then asked they him
John5:13 And he that was healed wist not who it was: for Jesus had conveyed himself away
John5:14 Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple
John5:15 The man departed
John5:16 And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus
John5:17 But Jesus answered them
John5:18 Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him
John5:19 Then answered Jesus and said unto them
John5:20 For the Father loveth the Son
John5:21 For as the Father raiseth up the dead
John5:22 For the Father judgeth no man
John5:23 That all men should honour the Son
John5:24 Verily
John5:25 Verily
John5:26 For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;
John5:27 And hath given him authority to execute judgment also
John5:28 Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming
John5:29 And shall come forth; they that have done good
John5:30 I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear
John5:31 If I bear witness of myself
John5:32 There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.
John5:33 Ye sent unto John
John5:34 But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say
John5:35 He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.
John5:36 But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish
John5:37 And the Father himself
John5:38 And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent
John5:39 Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me.
John5:40 And ye will not come to me
John5:41 I receive not honour from men.
John5:42 But I know you
John5:43 I am come in my Father's name
John5:44 How can ye believe
John5:45 Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you
John5:46 For had ye believed Moses
John5:47 But if ye believe not his writings
John6:1 After these things Jesus went over the sea of Galilee
John6:2 And a great multitude followed him
John6:3 And Jesus went up into a mountain
John6:4 And the passover
John6:5 When Jesus then lifted up his eyes
John6:6 And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.

John6:8 One of his disciples
John6:9 There is a lad here
John6:10 And Jesus said
John6:11 And Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given thanks
John6:12 When they were filled
John6:13 Therefore they gathered them together
John6:14 Then those men
John6:15 When Jesus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force
John6:16 And when even was now come
John6:17 And entered into a ship
John6:18 And the sea arose by reason of a great wind that blew.
John6:19 So when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs
John6:20 But he saith unto them
John6:21 Then they willingly received him into the ship: and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went.
John6:22 The day following
John6:23 (Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread
John6:24 When the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there
John6:25 And when they had found him on the other side of the sea
John6:26 Jesus answered them and said
John6:27 Labour not for the meat which perisheth
John6:28 Then said they unto him
John6:29 Jesus answered and said unto them
John6:30 They said therefore unto him
John6:31 Our fathers did eat manna in the desert; as it is written
John6:32 Then Jesus said unto them
John6:33 For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven
John6:34 Then said they unto him
John6:35 And Jesus said unto them
John6:36 But I said unto you
John6:37 All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.
John6:38 For I came down from heaven
John6:39 And this is the Father's will which hath sent me
John6:40 And this is the will of him that sent me
John6:41 The Jews then murmured at him
John6:42 And they said
John6:43 Jesus therefore answered and said unto them
John6:44 No man can come to me
John6:45 It is written in the prophets
John6:46 Not that any man hath seen the Father
John6:47 Verily
John6:48 I am that bread of life.
John6:49 Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness
John6:50 This is the bread which cometh down from heaven
John6:51 I am the living bread which came down from heaven: if any man eat of this bread
John6:52 The Jews therefore strove among themselves
John6:53 Then Jesus said unto them
John6:54 Whoso eateth my flesh
John6:55 For my flesh is meat indeed
John6:56 He that eateth my flesh
John6:57 As the living Father hath sent me
John6:58 This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna
John6:59 These things said he in the synagogue
John6:60 Many therefore of his disciples
John6:61 When Jesus knew in himself that his disciples murmured at it
John6:62 What and if ye shall see the Son of man ascend up where he was before?
John6:63 It is the spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I speak unto you
John6:64 But there are some of you that believe not. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not
John6:65 And he said
John6:66 From that time many of his disciples went back
John6:67 Then said Jesus unto the twelve
John6:68 Then Simon Peter answered him
John6:69 And we believe and are sure that thou art that Christ
John6:70 Jesus answered them
John6:71 He spake of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him
John7:1 After these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry
John7:2 Now the Jew's feast of tabernacles was at hand.
John7:3 His brethren therefore said unto him
John7:4 For there is no man that doeth any thing in secret
John7:5 For neither did his brethren believe in him.
John7:6 Then Jesus said unto them
John7:7 The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth
John7:8 Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast: for my time is not yet full come.
John7:9 When he had said these words unto them
John7:10 But when his brethren were gone up
John7:11 Then the Jews sought him at the feast
John7:12 And there was much murmuring among the people concerning him: for some said
John7:13 Howbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.
John7:14 Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple
John7:15 And the Jews marvelled
John7:16 Jesus answered them
John7:17 If any man will do his will
John7:18 He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him
John7:19 Did not Moses give you the law
John7:20 The people answered and said
John7:21 Jesus answered and said unto them
John7:22 Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses
John7:23 If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision
John7:24 Judge not according to the appearance
John7:25 Then said some of them of Jerusalem
John7:26 But
John7:27 Howbeit we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh
John7:28 Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught
John7:29 But I know him: for I am from him
John7:30 Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him
John7:31 And many of the people believed on him
John7:32 The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.
John7:33 Then said Jesus unto them
John7:34 Ye shall seek me
John7:35 Then said the Jews among themselves
John7:36 What manner of saying is this that he said
John7:37 In the last day
John7:38 He that believeth on me
John7:39 (But this spake he of the Spirit
John7:40 Many of the people therefore
John7:41 Others said
John7:42 Hath not the scripture said
John7:43 So there was a division among the people because of him.
John7:44 And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.
John7:45 Then came the officers to the chief priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them
John7:46 The officers answered
John7:47 Then answered them the Pharisees
John7:48 Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?
John7:49 But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed.
John7:50 Nicodemus saith unto them
John7:51 Doth our law judge any man
John7:52 They answered and said unto him
John7:53 And every man went unto his own house.

John8:2 And early in the morning he came again into the temple

John8:4 They say unto him
John8:5 Now Moses in the law commanded us
John8:6 This they said
John8:7 So when they continued asking him
John8:8 And again he stooped down
John8:9 And they which heard it
John8:10 When Jesus had lifted up himself
John8:11 She said
John8:12 Then spake Jesus again unto them
John8:13 The Pharisees therefore said unto him
John8:14 Jesus answered and said unto them
John8:15 Ye judge after the flesh; I judge no man.
John8:16 And yet if I judge
John8:17 It is also written in your law
John8:18 I am one that bear witness of myself
John8:19 Then said they unto him
John8:20 These words spake Jesus in the treasury
John8:21 Then said Jesus again unto them
John8:22 Then said the Jews
John8:23 And he said unto them
John8:24 I said therefore unto you
John8:25 Then said they unto him
John8:26 I have many things to say and to judge of you: but he that sent me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I have heard of him.
John8:27 They understood not that he spake to them of the Father.
John8:28 Then said Jesus unto them
John8:29 And he that sent me is with me: the Father hath not left me alone; for I do always those things that please him.
John8:30 As he spake these words
John8:31 Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on him
John8:32 And ye shall know the truth
John8:33 They answered him
John8:34 Jesus answered them
John8:35 And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: but the Son abideth ever.
John8:36 If the Son therefore shall make you free
John8:37 I know that ye are Abraham's seed; but ye seek to kill me
John8:38 I speak that which I have seen with my Father: and ye do that which ye have seen with your father.
John8:39 They answered and said unto him
John8:40 But now ye seek to kill me
John8:41 Ye do the deeds of your father. Then said they to him
John8:42 Jesus said unto them
John8:43 Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot hear my word.
John8:44 Ye are of your father the devil
John8:45 And because I tell you the truth
John8:46 Which of you convinceth me of sin? And if I say the truth
John8:47 He that is of God heareth God's words: ye therefore hear them not
John8:48 Then answered the Jews
John8:49 Jesus answered
John8:50 And I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.
John8:51 Verily
John8:52 Then said the Jews unto him
John8:53 Art thou greater than our father Abraham
John8:54 Jesus answered
John8:55 Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say
John8:56 Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day: and he saw it
John8:57 Then said the Jews unto him
John8:58 Jesus said unto them
John8:59 Then took they up stones to cast at him: but Jesus hid himself

John10:2 But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.

John10:4 And when he putteth forth his own sheep
John10:5 And a stranger will they not follow

John10:7 Then said Jesus unto them again
John10:8 All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.
John10:9 I am the door: by me if any man enter in
John10:10 The thief cometh not
John10:11 I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.
John10:12 But he that is an hireling
John10:13 The hireling fleeth
John10:14 I am the good shepherd
John10:15 As the Father knoweth me
John10:16 And other sheep I have
John10:17 Therefore doth my Father love me
John10:18 No man taketh it from me

John10:21 Others said

John10:28 And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish
John10:29 My Father
John10:30 I and my Father are one.

John10:33 The Jews answered him
John10:34 Jesus answered them
John10:35 If he called them gods

John10:37 If I do not the works of my Father
John10:38 But if I do

John12:1 Then Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany
John12:2 There they made him a supper; and Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.
John12:3 Then took Mary a pound of ointment of spikenard
John12:4 Then saith one of his disciples
John12:5 Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence
John12:6 This he said
