Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 (Which he had promised afore by his prophets in the holy scriptures
Romans1:3 Concerning his Son Jesus Christ our Lord
Romans1:4 And declared to be the Son of God with power
Romans1:5 By whom we have received grace and apostleship
Romans1:6 Among whom are ye also the called of Jesus Christ:
Romans1:7 To all that be in Rome
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 For God is my witness
Romans1:10 Making request
Romans1:11 For I long to see you
Romans1:12 That is
Romans1:13 Now I would not have you ignorant
Romans1:14 I am debtor both to the Greeks
Romans1:15 So
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation to every one that believeth; to the Jew first
Romans1:17 For therein is the righteousness of God revealed from faith to faith: as it is written
Romans1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men
Romans1:19 Because that which may be known of God is manifest in them; for God hath shewed it unto them.
Romans1:20 For the invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen
Romans1:21 Because that
Romans1:22 Professing themselves to be wise
Romans1:23 And changed the glory of the uncorruptible God into an image made like to corruptible man
Romans1:24 Wherefore God also gave them up to uncleanness through the lusts of their own hearts
Romans1:25 Who changed the truth of God into a lie
Romans1:26 For this cause God gave them up unto vile affections: for even their women did change the natural use into that which is against nature:
Romans1:27 And likewise also the men
Romans1:28 And even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge
Romans1:29 Being filled with all unrighteousness
Romans1:30 Backbiters
Romans1:31 Without understanding
Romans1:32 Who knowing the judgment of God
Romans2:1 Therefore thou art inexcusable
Romans2:2 But we are sure that the judgment of God is according to truth against them which commit such things.
Romans2:3 And thinkest thou this
Romans2:4 Or despisest thou the riches of his goodness and forbearance and longsuffering; not knowing that the goodness of God leadeth thee to repentance?
Romans2:5 But after thy hardness and impenitent heart treasurest up unto thyself wrath against the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God;
Romans2:6 Who will render to every man according to his deeds:
Romans2:7 To them who by patient continuance in well doing seek for glory and honour and immortality
Romans2:8 But unto them that are contentious
Romans2:9 Tribulation and anguish
Romans2:10 But glory
Romans2:11 For there is no respect of persons with God.
Romans2:12 For as many as have sinned without law shall also perish without law: and as many as have sinned in the law shall be judged by the law;
Romans2:13 (For not the hearers of the law are just before God
Romans2:14 For when the Gentiles
Romans2:15 Which shew the work of the law written in their hearts
Romans2:16 In the day when God shall judge the secrets of men by Jesus Christ according to my gospel.
Romans2:17 Behold
Romans2:18 And knowest his will
Romans2:19 And art confident that thou thyself art a guide of the blind
Romans2:20 An instructor of the foolish
Romans2:21 Thou therefore which teachest another
Romans2:22 Thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery
Romans2:23 Thou that makest thy boast of the law
Romans2:24 For the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles through you
Romans2:25 For circumcision verily profiteth
Romans2:26 Therefore if the uncircumcision keep the righteousness of the law
Romans2:27 And shall not uncircumcision which is by nature
Romans2:28 For he is not a Jew
Romans2:29 But he is a Jew
Romans3:1 What advantage then hath the Jew? or what profit is there of circumcision?
Romans3:2 Much every way: chiefly
Romans3:3 For what if some did not believe? shall their unbelief make the faith of God without effect?
Romans3:4 God forbid: yea
Romans3:5 But if our unrighteousness commend the righteousness of God
Romans3:6 God forbid: for then how shall God judge the world?
Romans3:7 For if the truth of God hath more abounded through my lie unto his glory; why yet am I also judged as a sinner?
Romans3:8 And not rather
Romans3:9 What then? are we better than they? No
Romans3:10 As it is written
Romans3:11 There is none that understandeth
Romans3:12 They are all gone out of the way
Romans3:13 Their throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit; the poison of asps is under their lips:
Romans3:14 Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness:
Romans3:15 Their feet are swift to shed blood:
Romans3:16 Destruction and misery are in their ways:
Romans3:17 And the way of peace have they not known:
Romans3:18 There is no fear of God before their eyes.
Romans3:19 Now we know that what things soever the law saith
Romans3:20 Therefore by the deeds of the law there shall no flesh be justified in his sight: for by the law is the knowledge of sin.
Romans3:21 But now the righteousness of God without the law is manifested
Romans3:22 Even the righteousness of God which is by faith of Jesus Christ unto all and upon all them that believe: for there is no difference:
Romans3:23 For all have sinned
Romans3:24 Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:
Romans3:25 Whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood
Romans3:26 To declare
Romans3:27 Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? of works? Nay: but by the law of faith.
Romans3:28 Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.
Romans3:29 Is he the God of the Jews only? is he not also of the Gentiles? Yes
Romans3:30 Seeing it is one God
Romans3:31 Do we then make void the law through faith? God forbid: yea
Romans4:1 What shall we say then that Abraham our father
Romans4:2 For if Abraham were justified by works
Romans4:3 For what saith the scripture? Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 Now to him that worketh is the reward not reckoned of grace
Romans4:5 But to him that worketh not
Romans4:6 Even as David also describeth the blessedness of the man
Romans4:7 Saying
Romans4:8 Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin.
Romans4:9 Cometh this blessedness then upon the circumcision only
Romans4:10 How was it then reckoned? when he was in circumcision
Romans4:11 And he received the sign of circumcision
Romans4:12 And the father of circumcision to them who are not of the circumcision only
Romans4:13 For the promise
Romans4:14 For if they which are of the law be heirs
Romans4:15 Because the law worketh wrath: for where no law is
Romans4:16 Therefore it is of faith
Romans4:17 (As it is written
Romans4:18 Who against hope believed in hope
Romans4:19 And being not weak in faith
Romans4:20 He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith
Romans4:21 And being fully persuaded that
Romans4:22 And therefore it was imputed to him for righteousness.
Romans4:23 Now it was not written for his sake alone
Romans4:24 But for us also
Romans4:25 Who was delivered for our offences
Romans5:1 Therefore being justified by faith
Romans5:2 By whom also we have access by faith into this grace wherein we stand
Romans5:3 And not only so
Romans5:4 And patience
Romans5:5 And hope maketh not ashamed; because the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost which is given unto us.
Romans5:6 For when we were yet without strength
Romans5:7 For scarcely for a righteous man will one die: yet peradventure for a good man some would even dare to die.
Romans5:8 But God commendeth his love toward us
Romans5:9 Much more then
Romans5:10 For if
Romans5:11 And not only so
Romans5:12 Wherefore
Romans5:13 (For until the law sin was in the world: but sin is not imputed when there is no law.
Romans5:14 Nevertheless death reigned from Adam to Moses
Romans5:15 But not as the offence
Romans5:16 And not as it was by one that sinned
Romans5:17 For if by one man's offence death reigned by one; much more they which receive abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness shall reign in life by one
Romans5:18 Therefore as by the offence of one judgment came upon all men to condemnation; even so by the righteousness of one the free gift came upon all men unto justification of life.
Romans5:19 For as by one man's disobedience many were made sinners
Romans5:20 Moreover the law entered
Romans5:21 That as sin hath reigned unto death
Romans6:1 What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin
Romans6:2 God forbid. How shall we
Romans6:3 Know ye not
Romans6:4 Therefore we are buried with him by baptism into death: that like as Christ was raised up from the dead by the glory of the Father
Romans6:5 For if we have been planted together in the likeness of his death
Romans6:6 Knowing this
Romans6:7 For he that is dead is freed from sin.
Romans6:8 Now if we be dead with Christ
Romans6:9 Knowing that Christ being raised from the dead dieth no more; death hath no more dominion over him.
Romans6:10 For in that he died
Romans6:11 Likewise reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin
Romans6:12 Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body
Romans6:13 Neither yield ye your members as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin: but yield yourselves unto God
Romans6:14 For sin shall not have dominion over you: for ye are not under the law
Romans6:15 What then? shall we sin
Romans6:16 Know ye not
Romans6:17 But God be thanked
Romans6:18 Being then made free from sin
Romans6:19 I speak after the manner of men because of the infirmity of your flesh: for as ye have yielded your members servants to uncleanness and to iniquity unto iniquity; even so now yield your members servants to righteousness unto holiness.
Romans6:20 For when ye were the servants of sin
Romans6:21 What fruit had ye then in those things whereof ye are now ashamed? for the end of those things is death.
Romans6:22 But now being made free from sin
Romans6:23 For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.

Romans7:2 For the woman which hath an husband is bound by the law to her husband so long as he liveth; but if the husband be dead
Romans7:3 So then if
Romans7:4 Wherefore
Romans7:5 For when we were in the flesh
Romans7:6 But now we are delivered from the law
Romans7:7 What shall we say then? Is the law sin? God forbid. Nay
Romans7:8 But sin
Romans7:9 For I was alive without the law once: but when the commandment came
Romans7:10 And the commandment
Romans7:11 For sin
Romans7:12 Wherefore the law is holy
Romans7:13 Was then that which is good made death unto me? God forbid. But sin
Romans7:14 For we know that the law is spiritual: but I am carnal
Romans7:15 For that which I do I allow not: for what I would
Romans7:16 If then I do that which I would not
Romans7:17 Now then it is no more I that do it
Romans7:18 For I know that in me (that is
Romans7:19 For the good that I would I do not: but the evil which I would not
Romans7:20 Now if I do that I would not
Romans7:21 I find then a law
Romans7:22 For I delight in the law of God after the inward man:
Romans7:23 But I see another law in my members
Romans7:24 O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?
Romans7:25 I thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord. So then with the mind I myself serve the law of God; but with the flesh the law of sin.
Romans8:1 There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus
Romans8:2 For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death.
Romans8:3 For what the law could not do
Romans8:4 That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us
Romans8:5 For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
Romans8:6 For to be carnally minded is death; but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.
Romans8:7 Because the carnal mind is enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God
Romans8:8 So then they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
Romans8:9 But ye are not in the flesh
Romans8:10 And if Christ be in you
Romans8:11 But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you
Romans8:12 Therefore
Romans8:13 For if ye live after the flesh
Romans8:14 For as many as are led by the Spirit of God
Romans8:15 For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption
Romans8:16 The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit
Romans8:17 And if children
Romans8:18 For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us.
Romans8:19 For the earnest expectation of the creature waiteth for the manifestation of the sons of God.
Romans8:20 For the creature was made subject to vanity
Romans8:21 Because the creature itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption into the glorious liberty of the children of God.
Romans8:22 For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.
Romans8:23 And not only they
Romans8:24 For we are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope: for what a man seeth
Romans8:25 But if we hope for that we see not
Romans8:26 Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.
Romans8:27 And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit
Romans8:28 And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God
Romans8:29 For whom he did foreknow
Romans8:30 Moreover whom he did predestinate
Romans8:31 What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us
Romans8:32 He that spared not his own Son
Romans8:33 Who shall lay any thing to the charge of God's elect? It is God that justifieth.
Romans8:34 Who is he that condemneth? It is Christ that died
Romans8:35 Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation
Romans8:36 As it is written
Romans8:37 Nay
Romans8:38 For I am persuaded

Romans14:18 For he that in these things serveth Christ is acceptable to God
Romans14:19 Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace
Romans14:20 For meat destroy not the work of God. All things indeed are pure; but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence.
Romans14:21 It is good neither to eat flesh
Romans14:22 Hast thou faith? have it to thyself before God. Happy is he that condemneth not himself in that thing which he alloweth.
Romans14:23 And he that doubteth is damned if he eat

1Corinthians7:39 The wife is bound by the law as long as her husband liveth; but if her husband be dead
1Corinthians7:40 But she is happier if she so abide
1Corinthians8:1 Now as touching things offered unto idols
1Corinthians8:2 And if any man think that he knoweth any thing
1Corinthians8:3 But if any man love God
1Corinthians8:4 As concerning therefore the eating of those things that are offered in sacrifice unto idols
1Corinthians8:5 For though there be that are called gods
1Corinthians8:6 But to us there is but one God
1Corinthians8:7 Howbeit there is not in every man that knowledge: for some with conscience of the idol unto this hour eat it as a thing offered unto an idol; and their conscience being weak is defiled.
1Corinthians8:8 But meat commendeth us not to God: for neither
1Corinthians8:9 But take heed lest by any means this liberty of your's become a stumblingblock to them that are weak.
1Corinthians8:10 For if any man see thee which hast knowledge sit at meat in the idol's temple
1Corinthians8:11 And through thy knowledge shall the weak brother perish
1Corinthians8:12 But when ye sin so against the brethren
1Corinthians8:13 Wherefore
1Corinthians9:1 Am I not an apostle? am I not free? have I not seen Jesus Christ our Lord? are not ye my work in the Lord?
1Corinthians9:2 If I be not an apostle unto others
1Corinthians9:3 Mine answer to them that do examine me is this
1Corinthians9:4 Have we not power to eat and to drink?
1Corinthians9:5 Have we not power to lead about a sister
1Corinthians9:6 Or I only and Barnabas
1Corinthians9:7 Who goeth a warfare any time at his own charges? who planteth a vineyard
1Corinthians9:8 Say I these things as a man? or saith not the law the same also?
1Corinthians9:9 For it is written in the law of Moses
1Corinthians9:10 Or saith he it altogether for our sakes? For our sakes
1Corinthians9:11 If we have sown unto you spiritual things
1Corinthians9:12 If others be partakers of this power over you
1Corinthians9:13 Do ye not know that they which minister about holy things live of the things of the temple? and they which wait at the altar are partakers with the altar?
1Corinthians9:14 Even so hath the Lord ordained that they which preach the gospel should live of the gospel.
1Corinthians9:15 But I have used none of these things: neither have I written these things
1Corinthians9:16 For though I preach the gospel
1Corinthians9:17 For if I do this thing willingly
1Corinthians9:18 What is my reward then? Verily that
1Corinthians9:19 For though I be free from all men
1Corinthians9:20 And unto the Jews I became as a Jew
1Corinthians9:21 To them that are without law
1Corinthians9:22 To the weak became I as weak
1Corinthians9:23 And this I do for the gospel's sake
1Corinthians9:24 Know ye not that they which run in a race run all
1Corinthians9:25 And every man that striveth for the mastery is temperate in all things. Now they do it to obtain a corruptible crown; but we an incorruptible.
1Corinthians9:26 I therefore so run
1Corinthians9:27 But I keep under my body
1Corinthians10:1 Moreover
1Corinthians10:2 And were all baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
1Corinthians10:3 And did all eat the same spiritual meat;
1Corinthians10:4 And did all drink the same spiritual drink: for they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them: and that Rock was Christ.
1Corinthians10:5 But with many of them God was not well pleased: for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
1Corinthians10:6 Now these things were our examples
1Corinthians10:7 Neither be ye idolaters
1Corinthians10:8 Neither let us commit fornication
1Corinthians10:9 Neither let us tempt Christ
1Corinthians10:10 Neither murmur ye
1Corinthians10:11 Now all these things happened unto them for ensamples: and they are written for our admonition
1Corinthians10:12 Wherefore let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.
1Corinthians10:13 There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful
1Corinthians10:14 Wherefore
1Corinthians10:15 I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
1Corinthians10:16 The cup of blessing which we bless
1Corinthians10:17 For we being many are one bread
1Corinthians10:18 Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
1Corinthians10:19 What say I then? that the idol is any thing
1Corinthians10:20 But I say
1Corinthians10:21 Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord
1Corinthians10:22 Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?
1Corinthians10:23 All things are lawful for me
1Corinthians10:24 Let no man seek his own
1Corinthians10:25 Whatsoever is sold in the shambles
1Corinthians10:26 For the earth is the Lord's

1Corinthians11:1 Be ye followers of me
1Corinthians11:2 Now I praise you
1Corinthians11:3 But I would have you know
1Corinthians11:4 Every man praying or prophesying
1Corinthians11:5 But every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head uncovered dishonoureth her head: for that is even all one as if she were shaven.
1Corinthians11:6 For if the woman be not covered
1Corinthians11:7 For a man indeed ought not to cover his head
1Corinthians11:8 For the man is not of the woman: but the woman of the man.
1Corinthians11:9 Neither was the man created for the woman; but the woman for the man.
1Corinthians11:10 For this cause ought the woman to have power on her head because of the angels.
1Corinthians11:11 Nevertheless neither is the man without the woman
1Corinthians11:12 For as the woman is of the man
1Corinthians11:13 Judge in yourselves: is it comely that a woman pray unto God uncovered?
1Corinthians11:14 Doth not even nature itself teach you
1Corinthians11:15 But if a woman have long hair
1Corinthians11:16 But if any man seem to be contentious
1Corinthians11:17 Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not
1Corinthians11:18 For first of all
1Corinthians11:19 For there must be also heresies among you
1Corinthians11:20 When ye come together therefore into one place
1Corinthians11:21 For in eating every one taketh before other his own supper: and one is hungry
1Corinthians11:22 What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God
1Corinthians11:23 For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you
1Corinthians11:24 And when he had given thanks
1Corinthians11:25 After the same manner also he took the cup
1Corinthians11:26 For as often as ye eat this bread

1Corinthians11:30 For this cause many are weak and sickly among you

1Corinthians11:32 But when we are judged
1Corinthians11:33 Wherefore

1Corinthians12:1 Now concerning spiritual gifts
1Corinthians12:2 Ye know that ye were Gentiles
1Corinthians12:3 Wherefore I give you to understand
1Corinthians12:4 Now there are diversities of gifts
1Corinthians12:5 And there are differences of administrations
1Corinthians12:6 And there are diversities of operations
1Corinthians12:7 But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to every man to profit withal.
1Corinthians12:8 For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom; to another the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
1Corinthians12:9 To another faith by the same Spirit; to another the gifts of healing by the same Spirit;
1Corinthians12:10 To another the working of miracles; to another prophecy; to another discerning of spirits; to another divers kinds of tongues; to another the interpretation of tongues:
1Corinthians12:11 But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit
1Corinthians12:12 For as the body is one
1Corinthians12:13 For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body
1Corinthians12:14 For the body is not one member
1Corinthians12:15 If the foot shall say
1Corinthians12:16 And if the ear shall say
1Corinthians12:17 If the whole body were an eye
1Corinthians12:18 But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body
1Corinthians12:19 And if they were all one member
1Corinthians12:20 But now are they many members
1Corinthians12:21 And the eye cannot say unto the hand
1Corinthians12:22 Nay
1Corinthians12:23 And those members of the body
1Corinthians12:24 For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together
1Corinthians12:25 That there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same care one for another.
1Corinthians12:26 And whether one member suffer
1Corinthians12:27 Now ye are the body of Christ
1Corinthians12:28 And God hath set some in the church
1Corinthians12:29 Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?
1Corinthians12:30 Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
1Corinthians12:31 But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.

1Corinthians13:5 Doth not behave itself unseemly
1Corinthians13:6 Rejoiceth not in iniquity

1Corinthians13:11 When I was a child

1Corinthians14:2 For he that speaketh in an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men
1Corinthians14:3 But he that prophesieth speaketh unto men to edification

1Corinthians14:6 Now
1Corinthians14:7 And even things without life giving sound
1Corinthians14:8 For if the trumpet give an uncertain sound
1Corinthians14:9 So likewise ye
1Corinthians14:10 There are
1Corinthians14:11 Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voice
1Corinthians14:12 Even so ye
1Corinthians14:13 Wherefore let him that speaketh in an unknown tongue pray that he may interpret.
1Corinthians14:14 For if I pray in an unknown tongue
1Corinthians14:15 What is it then? I will pray with the spirit
1Corinthians14:16 Else when thou shalt bless with the spirit
1Corinthians14:17 For thou verily givest thanks well
1Corinthians14:18 I thank my God
1Corinthians14:19 Yet in the church I had rather speak five words with my understanding

1Corinthians14:24 But if all prophesy

1Corinthians14:28 But if there be no interpreter

1Corinthians14:30 If any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by
1Corinthians14:31 For ye may all prophesy one by one
1Corinthians14:32 And the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets.
1Corinthians14:33 For God is not the author of confusion
1Corinthians14:34 Let your women keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to speak; but they are commanded to be under obedience as also saith the law.
1Corinthians14:35 And if they will learn any thing
1Corinthians14:36 What? came the word of God out from you? or came it unto you only?
1Corinthians14:37 If any man think himself to be a prophet
1Corinthians14:38 But if any man be ignorant
1Corinthians14:39 Wherefore
1Corinthians14:40 Let all things be done decently and in order.
1Corinthians15:1 Moreover
1Corinthians15:2 By which also ye are saved
1Corinthians15:3 For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received
1Corinthians15:4 And that he was buried
1Corinthians15:5 And that he was seen of Cephas
1Corinthians15:6 After that
1Corinthians15:7 After that
1Corinthians15:8 And last of all he was seen of me also
1Corinthians15:9 For I am the least of the apostles
1Corinthians15:10 But by the grace of God I am what I am: and his grace which was bestowed upon me was not in vain; but I laboured more abundantly than they all: yet not I
1Corinthians15:11 Therefore whether it were I or they
1Corinthians15:12 Now if Christ be preached that he rose from the dead
1Corinthians15:13 But if there be no resurrection of the dead
1Corinthians15:14 And if Christ be not risen
1Corinthians15:15 Yea
1Corinthians15:16 For if the dead rise not
1Corinthians15:17 And if Christ be not raised
1Corinthians15:18 Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are perished.
1Corinthians15:19 If in this life only we have hope in Christ
1Corinthians15:20 But now is Christ risen from the dead
1Corinthians15:21 For since by man came death
1Corinthians15:22 For as in Adam all die
1Corinthians15:23 But every man in his own order: Christ the firstfruits; afterward they that are Christ's at his coming.
1Corinthians15:24 Then cometh the end
1Corinthians15:25 For he must reign
1Corinthians15:26 The last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.
1Corinthians15:27 For he hath put all things under his feet. But when he saith all things are put under him
1Corinthians15:28 And when all things shall be subdued unto him
1Corinthians15:29 Else what shall they do which are baptized for the dead
1Corinthians15:30 And why stand we in jeopardy every hour?
1Corinthians15:31 I protest by your rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord
1Corinthians15:32 If after the manner of men I have fought with beasts at Ephesus
1Corinthians15:33 Be not deceived: evil communications corrupt good manners.
1Corinthians15:34 Awake to righteousness
1Corinthians15:35 But some man will say
1Corinthians15:36 Thou fool
1Corinthians15:37 And that which thou sowest
1Corinthians15:38 But God giveth it a body as it hath pleased him
1Corinthians15:39 All flesh is not the same flesh: but there is one kind of flesh of men
1Corinthians15:40 There are also celestial bodies
1Corinthians15:41 There is one glory of the sun
1Corinthians15:42 So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
1Corinthians15:43 It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
1Corinthians15:44 It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body
1Corinthians15:45 And so it is written
1Corinthians15:46 Howbeit that was not first which is spiritual
1Corinthians15:47 The first man is of the earth
1Corinthians15:48 As is the earthy
1Corinthians15:49 And as we have borne the image of the earthy
1Corinthians15:50 Now this I say
1Corinthians15:51 Behold
1Corinthians15:52 In a moment
1Corinthians15:53 For this corruptible must put on incorruption
1Corinthians15:54 So when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption
1Corinthians15:55 O death
1Corinthians15:56 The sting of death is sin; and the strength of sin is the law.
1Corinthians15:57 But thanks be to God
1Corinthians15:58 Therefore

1Corinthians16:2 Upon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store
1Corinthians16:3 And when I come
1Corinthians16:4 And if it be meet that I go also
1Corinthians16:5 Now I will come unto you
1Corinthians16:6 And it may be that I will abide
1Corinthians16:7 For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you
1Corinthians16:8 But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.
1Corinthians16:9 For a great door and effectual is opened unto me
1Corinthians16:10 Now if Timotheus come

1Corinthians16:12 As touching our brother Apollos
1Corinthians16:13 Watch ye

1Corinthians16:15 I beseech you
1Corinthians16:16 That ye submit yourselves unto such

1Corinthians16:18 For they have refreshed my spirit and your's: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.

1Corinthians16:22 If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ
1Corinthians16:23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
1Corinthians16:24 My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

2Corinthians9:2 For I know the forwardness of your mind
2Corinthians9:3 Yet have I sent the brethren
2Corinthians9:4 Lest haply if they of Macedonia come with me
2Corinthians9:5 Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren
2Corinthians9:6 But this I say
2Corinthians9:7 Every man according as he purposeth in his heart
2Corinthians9:8 And God is able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye
2Corinthians9:9 (As it is written
2Corinthians9:10 Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food
2Corinthians9:11 Being enriched in every thing to all bountifulness
2Corinthians9:12 For the administration of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints
2Corinthians9:13 Whiles by the experiment of this ministration they glorify God for your professed subjection unto the gospel of Christ
2Corinthians9:14 And by their prayer for you
2Corinthians9:15 Thanks be unto God for his unspeakable gift.
2Corinthians10:1 Now I Paul myself beseech you by the meekness and gentleness of Christ
2Corinthians10:2 But I beseech you
2Corinthians10:3 For though we walk in the flesh
2Corinthians10:4 (For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal
2Corinthians10:5 Casting down imaginations
2Corinthians10:6 And having in a readiness to revenge all disobedience
2Corinthians10:7 Do ye look on things after the outward appearance? If any man trust to himself that he is Christ's
2Corinthians10:8 For though I should boast somewhat more of our authority
2Corinthians10:9 That I may not seem as if I would terrify you by letters.
2Corinthians10:10 For his letters
2Corinthians10:11 Let such an one think this
2Corinthians10:12 For we dare not make ourselves of the number
2Corinthians10:13 But we will not boast of things without our measure
2Corinthians10:14 For we stretch not ourselves beyond our measure
2Corinthians10:15 Not boasting of things without our measure
2Corinthians10:16 To preach the gospel in the regions beyond you
2Corinthians10:17 But he that glorieth
2Corinthians10:18 For not he that commendeth himself is approved
2Corinthians11:1 Would to God ye could bear with me a little in my folly: and indeed bear with me.
2Corinthians11:2 For I am jealous over you with godly jealousy: for I have espoused you to one husband
2Corinthians11:3 But I fear
2Corinthians11:4 For if he that cometh preacheth another Jesus
2Corinthians11:5 For I suppose I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.
2Corinthians11:6 But though I be rude in speech
2Corinthians11:7 Have I committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted
2Corinthians11:8 I robbed other churches
2Corinthians11:9 And when I was present with you
2Corinthians11:10 As the truth of Christ is in me
2Corinthians11:11 Wherefore? because I love you not? God knoweth.
2Corinthians11:12 But what I do
2Corinthians11:13 For such are false apostles
2Corinthians11:14 And no marvel; for Satan himself is transformed into an angel of light.
2Corinthians11:15 Therefore it is no great thing if his ministers also be transformed as the ministers of righteousness; whose end shall be according to their works.
2Corinthians11:16 I say again
2Corinthians11:17 That which I speak
2Corinthians11:18 Seeing that many glory after the flesh
2Corinthians11:19 For ye suffer fools gladly
2Corinthians11:20 For ye suffer
2Corinthians11:21 I speak as concerning reproach
2Corinthians11:22 Are they Hebrews? so am I. Are they Israelites? so am I. Are they the seed of Abraham? so am I.
2Corinthians11:23 Are they ministers of Christ? (I speak as a fool) I am more; in labours more abundant
2Corinthians11:24 Of the Jews five times received I forty stripes save one.
2Corinthians11:25 Thrice was I beaten with rods
2Corinthians11:26 In journeyings often
2Corinthians11:27 In weariness and painfulness
2Corinthians11:28 Beside those things that are without
2Corinthians11:29 Who is weak
2Corinthians11:30 If I must needs glory
2Corinthians11:31 The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ
2Corinthians11:32 In Damascus the governor under Aretas the king kept the city of the Damascenes with a garrison
2Corinthians11:33 And through a window in a basket was I let down by the wall
2Corinthians12:1 It is not expedient for me doubtless to glory. I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.
2Corinthians12:2 I knew a man in Christ above fourteen years ago
2Corinthians12:3 And I knew such a man
2Corinthians12:4 How that he was caught up into paradise
2Corinthians12:5 Of such an one will I glory: yet of myself I will not glory
2Corinthians12:6 For though I would desire to glory
2Corinthians12:7 And lest I should be exalted above measure through the abundance of the revelations
2Corinthians12:8 For this thing I besought the Lord thrice
2Corinthians12:9 And he said unto me
2Corinthians12:10 Therefore I take pleasure in infirmities
2Corinthians12:11 I am become a fool in glorying; ye have compelled me: for I ought to have been commended of you: for in nothing am I behind the very chiefest apostles
2Corinthians12:12 Truly the signs of an apostle were wrought among you in all patience
2Corinthians12:13 For what is it wherein ye were inferior to other churches
2Corinthians12:14 Behold
2Corinthians12:15 And I will very gladly spend and be spent for you; though the more abundantly I love you
2Corinthians12:16 But be it so
2Corinthians12:17 Did I make a gain of you by any of them whom I sent unto you?
2Corinthians12:18 I desired Titus
2Corinthians12:19 Again
2Corinthians12:20 For I fear
2Corinthians12:21 And lest
2Corinthians13:1 This is the third time I am coming to you. In the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every word be established.
2Corinthians13:2 I told you before
2Corinthians13:3 Since ye seek a proof of Christ speaking in me
2Corinthians13:4 For though he was crucified through weakness
2Corinthians13:5 Examine yourselves
2Corinthians13:6 But I trust that ye shall know that we are not reprobates.
2Corinthians13:7 Now I pray to God that ye do no evil; not that we should appear approved
2Corinthians13:8 For we can do nothing against the truth
2Corinthians13:9 For we are glad
2Corinthians13:10 Therefore I write these things being absent
2Corinthians13:11 Finally
2Corinthians13:12 Greet one another with an holy kiss.
2Corinthians13:13 All the saints salute you.

Galatians1:1 Paul
Galatians1:2 And all the brethren which are with me
Galatians1:3 Grace be to you and peace from God the Father
Galatians1:4 Who gave himself for our sins
Galatians1:5 To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
Galatians1:6 I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:
Galatians1:7 Which is not another; but there be some that trouble you
Galatians1:8 But though we
Galatians1:9 As we said before
Galatians1:10 For do I now persuade men
Galatians1:11 But I certify you
Galatians1:12 For I neither received it of man
Galatians1:13 For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews' religion
Galatians1:14 And profited in the Jews' religion above many my equals in mine own nation
Galatians1:15 But when it pleased God
Galatians1:16 To reveal his Son in me
Galatians1:17 Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia
Galatians1:18 Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter
Galatians1:19 But other of the apostles saw I none
Galatians1:20 Now the things which I write unto you
Galatians1:21 Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;
Galatians1:22 And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea which were in Christ:
Galatians1:23 But they had heard only
Galatians1:24 And they glorified God in me.
Galatians2:1 Then fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas
Galatians2:2 And I went up by revelation
Galatians2:3 But neither Titus
Galatians2:4 And that because of false brethren unawares brought in
Galatians2:5 To whom we gave place by subjection
Galatians2:6 But of these who seemed to be somewhat
Galatians2:7 But contrariwise
Galatians2:8 (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision
Galatians2:9 And when James
Galatians2:10 Only they would that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.
Galatians2:11 But when Peter was come to Antioch
Galatians2:12 For before that certain came from James
Galatians2:13 And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.
Galatians2:14 But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel
Galatians2:15 We who are Jews by nature
Galatians2:16 Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law
Galatians2:17 But if
Galatians2:18 For if I build again the things which I destroyed
Galatians2:19 For I through the law am dead to the law
Galatians2:20 I am crucified with Christ: neverthless I live; yet not I
Galatians2:21 I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law
Galatians3:1 O foolish Galatians
Galatians3:2 This only would I learn of you
Galatians3:3 Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit
Galatians3:4 Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.
Galatians3:5 He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit
Galatians3:6 Even as Abraham believed God
Galatians3:7 Know ye therefore that they which are of faith
Galatians3:8 And the scripture
Galatians3:9 So then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful Abraham.
Galatians3:10 For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written
Galatians3:11 But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God
Galatians3:12 And the law is not of faith: but
Galatians3:13 Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law
Galatians3:14 That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
Galatians3:15 Brethren
Galatians3:16 Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not
Galatians3:17 And this I say
Galatians3:18 For if the inheritance be of the law
Galatians3:19 Wherefore then serveth the law? It was added because of transgressions
Galatians3:20 Now a mediator is not a mediator of one
Galatians3:21 Is the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could have given life
Galatians3:22 But the scripture hath concluded all under sin
Galatians3:23 But before faith came
Galatians3:24 Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ
Galatians3:25 But after that faith is come
Galatians3:26 For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.
Galatians3:27 For as many of you as have been baptized into Christ have put on Christ.
Galatians3:28 There is neither Jew nor Greek
Galatians3:29 And if ye be Christ's
Galatians4:1 Now I say
Galatians4:2 But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.
Galatians4:3 Even so we
Galatians4:4 But when the fulness of the time was come
Galatians4:5 To redeem them that were under the law
Galatians4:6 And because ye are sons
Galatians4:7 Wherefore thou art no more a servant
Galatians4:8 Howbeit then
Galatians4:9 But now
Galatians4:10 Ye observe days
Galatians4:11 I am afraid of you
Galatians4:12 Brethren
Galatians4:13 Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.
Galatians4:14 And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not
Galatians4:15 Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record
Galatians4:16 Am I therefore become your enemy
Galatians4:17 They zealously affect you
Galatians4:18 But it is good to be zealously affected always in a good thing
Galatians4:19 My little children
Galatians4:20 I desire to be present with you now
Galatians4:21 Tell me
Galatians4:22 For it is written
Galatians4:23 But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.
Galatians4:24 Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai
Galatians4:25 For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia
Galatians4:26 But Jerusalem which is above is free
Galatians4:27 For it is written
Galatians4:28 Now we
Galatians4:29 But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit
Galatians4:30 Nevertheless what saith the scripture? Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.
Galatians4:31 So then
Galatians5:1 Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free
Galatians5:2 Behold
Galatians5:3 For I testify again to every man that is circumcised
Galatians5:4 Christ is become of no effect unto you
Galatians5:5 For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.
Galatians5:6 For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing
Galatians5:7 Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?
Galatians5:8 This persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you.
Galatians5:9 A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.
Galatians5:10 I have confidence in you through the Lord
Galatians5:11 And I
Galatians5:12 I would they were even cut off which trouble you.
Galatians5:13 For
Galatians5:14 For all the law is fulfilled in one word
Galatians5:15 But if ye bite and devour one another
Galatians5:16 This I say then
Galatians5:17 For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit
Galatians5:18 But if ye be led of the Spirit
Galatians5:19 Now the works of the flesh are manifest
Galatians5:20 Idolatry
Galatians5:21 Envyings
Galatians5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love
Galatians5:23 Meekness
Galatians5:24 And they that are Christ's have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts.
Galatians5:25 If we live in the Spirit
Galatians5:26 Let us not be desirous of vain glory
Galatians6:1 Brethren
Galatians6:2 Bear ye one another's burdens
Galatians6:3 For if a man think himself to be something
Galatians6:4 But let every man prove his own work
Galatians6:5 For every man shall bear his own burden.
Galatians6:6 Let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.
Galatians6:7 Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth
Galatians6:8 For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.
Galatians6:9 And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap
Galatians6:10 As we have therefore opportunity
Galatians6:11 Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.
Galatians6:12 As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh
Galatians6:13 For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised
Galatians6:14 But God forbid that I should glory
Galatians6:15 For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing
Galatians6:16 And as many as walk according to this rule
Galatians6:17 From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.

Ephesians1:15 Wherefore I also

Ephesians1:22 And hath put all things under his feet

Ephesians2:2 Wherein in time past ye walked according to the course of this world
Ephesians2:3 Among whom also we all had our conversation in times past in the lusts of our flesh
Ephesians2:4 But God
Ephesians2:5 Even when we were dead in sins
Ephesians2:6 And hath raised us up together
Ephesians2:7 That in the ages to come he might shew the exceeding riches of his grace in his kindness toward us through Christ Jesus.
Ephesians2:8 For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God:
Ephesians2:9 Not of works
Ephesians2:10 For we are his workmanship
Ephesians2:11 Wherefore remember
Ephesians2:12 That at that time ye were without Christ
Ephesians2:13 But now in Christ Jesus ye who sometimes were far off are made nigh by the blood of Christ.
Ephesians2:14 For he is our peace
Ephesians2:15 Having abolished in his flesh the enmity
Ephesians2:16 And that he might reconcile both unto God in one body by the cross
Ephesians2:17 And came and preached peace to you which were afar off
Ephesians2:18 For through him we both have access by one Spirit unto the Father.
Ephesians2:19 Now therefore ye are no more strangers and foreigners
Ephesians2:20 And are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets
Ephesians2:21 In whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord:
Ephesians2:22 In whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of God through the Spirit.

Ephesians3:5 Which in other ages was not made known unto the sons of men

Ephesians3:7 Whereof I was made a minister

Ephesians3:12 In whom we have boldness and access with confidence by the faith of him.

Ephesians5:23 For the husband is the head of the wife
Ephesians5:24 Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ
Ephesians5:25 Husbands
Ephesians5:26 That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word
Ephesians5:27 That he might present it to himself a glorious church
Ephesians5:28 So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.
Ephesians5:29 For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it
Ephesians5:30 For we are members of his body
Ephesians5:31 For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother
Ephesians5:32 This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.
Ephesians5:33 Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife see that she reverence her husband.

Philippians1:1 Paul and Timotheus
Philippians1:2 Grace be unto you
Philippians1:3 I thank my God upon every remembrance of you
Philippians1:4 Always in every prayer of mine for you all making request with joy
Philippians1:5 For your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now;
Philippians1:6 Being confident of this very thing
Philippians1:7 Even as it is meet for me to think this of you all
Philippians1:8 For God is my record
Philippians1:9 And this I pray
Philippians1:10 That ye may approve things that are excellent; that ye may be sincere and without offence till the day of Christ.
Philippians1:11 Being filled with the fruits of righteousness
Philippians1:12 But I would ye should understand
Philippians1:13 So that my bonds in Christ are manifest in all the palace
Philippians1:14 And many of the brethren in the Lord
Philippians1:15 Some indeed preach Christ even of envy and strife; and some also of good will:
Philippians1:16 The one preach Christ of contention
Philippians1:17 But the other of love
Philippians1:18 What then? notwithstanding
Philippians1:19 For I know that this shall turn to my salvation through your prayer
Philippians1:20 According to my earnest expectation and my hope
Philippians1:21 For to me to live is Christ
Philippians1:22 But if I live in the flesh
Philippians1:23 For I am in a strait betwixt two
Philippians1:24 Nevertheless to abide in the flesh is more needful for you.
Philippians1:25 And having this confidence
Philippians1:26 That your rejoicing may be more abundant in Jesus Christ for me by my coming to you again.
Philippians1:27 Only let your conversation be as it becometh the gospel of Christ: that whether I come and see you
Philippians1:28 And in nothing terrified by your adversaries: which is to them an evident token of perdition
Philippians1:29 For unto you it is given in the behalf of Christ
Philippians1:30 Having the same conflict which ye saw in me

Philippians2:2 Fulfil ye my joy
Philippians2:3 Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves.

Philippians2:5 Let this mind be in you
