Philippians2:7 But made himself of no reputation
Philippians2:8 And being found in fashion as a man
Philippians2:9 Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him
Philippians2:10 That at the name of Jesus every knee should bow
Philippians2:11 And that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord
Philippians2:12 Wherefore
Philippians2:13 For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure.
Philippians2:14 Do all things without murmurings and disputings:
Philippians2:15 That ye may be blameless and harmless
Philippians2:16 Holding forth the word of life; that I may rejoice in the day of Christ
Philippians2:17 Yea
Philippians2:18 For the same cause also do ye joy
Philippians2:19 But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus shortly unto you
Philippians2:20 For I have no man likeminded
Philippians2:21 For all seek their own
Philippians2:22 But ye know the proof of him
Philippians2:23 Him therefore I hope to send presently
Philippians2:24 But I trust in the Lord that I also myself shall come shortly.
Philippians2:25 Yet I supposed it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus
Philippians2:26 For he longed after you all
Philippians2:27 For indeed he was sick nigh unto death: but God had mercy on him; and not on him only
Philippians2:28 I sent him therefore the more carefully
Philippians2:29 Receive him therefore in the Lord with all gladness; and hold such in reputation:
Philippians2:30 Because for the work of Christ he was nigh unto death
Philippians3:1 Finally
Philippians3:2 Beware of dogs
Philippians3:3 For we are the circumcision
Philippians3:4 Though I might also have confidence in the flesh. If any other man thinketh that he hath whereof he might trust in the flesh
Philippians3:5 Circumcised the eighth day
Philippians3:6 Concerning zeal
Philippians3:7 But what things were gain to me
Philippians3:8 Yea doubtless
Philippians3:9 And be found in him
Philippians3:10 That I may know him
Philippians3:11 If by any means I might attain unto the resurrection of the dead.
Philippians3:12 Not as though I had already attained
Philippians3:13 Brethren
Philippians3:14 I press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.
Philippians3:15 Let us therefore
Philippians3:16 Nevertheless
Philippians3:17 Brethren
Philippians3:18 (For many walk
Philippians3:19 Whose end is destruction
Philippians3:20 For our conversation is in heaven; from whence also we look for the Saviour
Philippians3:21 Who shall change our vile body
Philippians4:1 Therefore
Philippians4:2 I beseech Euodias
Philippians4:3 And I intreat thee also
Philippians4:4 Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say
Philippians4:5 Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord is at hand.
Philippians4:6 Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
Philippians4:7 And the peace of God
Philippians4:8 Finally
Philippians4:9 Those things
Philippians4:10 But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly
Philippians4:11 Not that I speak in respect of want: for I have learned
Philippians4:12 I know both how to be abased
Philippians4:13 I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
Philippians4:14 Notwithstanding ye have well done
Philippians4:15 Now ye Philippians know also
Philippians4:16 For even in Thessalonica ye sent once and again unto my necessity.
Philippians4:17 Not because I desire a gift: but I desire fruit that may abound to your account.
Philippians4:18 But I have all
Philippians4:19 But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:20 Now unto God and our Father be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
Philippians4:21 Salute every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren which are with me greet you.
Philippians4:22 All the saints salute you
Philippians4:23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
Colossians1:1 Paul
Colossians1:2 To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace be unto you
Colossians1:3 We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ
Colossians1:4 Since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus
Colossians1:5 For the hope which is laid up for you in heaven
Colossians1:6 Which is come unto you
Colossians1:7 As ye also learned of Epaphras our dear fellowservant
Colossians1:8 Who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 For this cause we also
Colossians1:10 That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing
Colossians1:11 Strengthened with all might
Colossians1:12 Giving thanks unto the Father
Colossians1:13 Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness
Colossians1:14 In whom we have redemption through his blood
Colossians1:15 Who is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 For by him were all things created
Colossians1:17 And he is before all things
Colossians1:18 And he is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For it pleased the Father that in him should all fulness dwell;
Colossians1:20 And
Colossians1:21 And you
Colossians1:22 In the body of his flesh through death
Colossians1:23 If ye continue in the faith grounded and settled
Colossians1:24 Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you
Colossians1:25 Whereof I am made a minister
Colossians1:26 Even the mystery which hath been hid from ages and from generations
Colossians1:27 To whom God would make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you
Colossians1:28 Whom we preach
Colossians1:29 Whereunto I also labour
Colossians2:1 For I would that ye knew what great conflict I have for you
Colossians2:2 That their hearts might be comforted
Colossians2:3 In whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
Colossians2:4 And this I say
Colossians2:5 For though I be absent in the flesh
Colossians2:6 As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord
Colossians2:7 Rooted and built up in him
Colossians2:8 Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit
Colossians2:9 For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily.
Colossians2:10 And ye are complete in him
Colossians2:11 In whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands
Colossians2:12 Buried with him in baptism
Colossians2:13 And you
Colossians2:14 Blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us
Colossians2:15 And having spoiled principalities and powers
Colossians2:16 Let no man therefore judge you in meat
Colossians2:17 Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ.
Colossians2:18 Let no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels
Colossians2:19 And not holding the Head
Colossians2:20 Wherefore if ye be dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world
Colossians2:21 (Touch not; taste not; handle not;
Colossians2:22 Which all are to perish with the using;) after the commandments and doctrines of men?
Colossians2:23 Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom in will worship
Colossians3:1 If ye then be risen with Christ
Colossians3:2 Set your affection on things above
Colossians3:3 For ye are dead
Colossians3:4 When Christ
Colossians3:5 Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication
Colossians3:6 For which things' sake the wrath of God cometh on the children of disobedience:
Colossians3:7 In the which ye also walked some time
Colossians3:8 But now ye also put off all these; anger
Colossians3:9 Lie not one to another
Colossians3:10 And have put on the new man
Colossians3:11 Where there is neither Greek nor Jew
Colossians3:12 Put on therefore
Colossians3:13 Forbearing one another
Colossians3:14 And above all these things put on charity
Colossians3:15 And let the peace of God rule in your hearts
Colossians3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs
Colossians3:17 And whatsoever ye do in word or deed
Colossians3:18 Wives
Colossians3:19 Husbands
Colossians3:20 Children
Colossians3:21 Fathers
Colossians3:22 Servants
Colossians3:23 And whatsoever ye do
Colossians3:24 Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.
Colossians3:25 But he that doeth wrong shall receive for the wrong which he hath done: and there is no respect of persons.
Colossians4:1 Masters
Colossians4:2 Continue in prayer
Colossians4:3 Withal praying also for us
Colossians4:4 That I may make it manifest
Colossians4:5 Walk in wisdom toward them that are without
Colossians4:6 Let your speech be alway with grace
Colossians4:7 All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you
Colossians4:8 Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose
Colossians4:9 With Onesimus
Colossians4:10 Aristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you
Colossians4:11 And Jesus
Colossians4:12 Epaphras
Colossians4:13 For I bear him record
Colossians4:14 Luke
Colossians4:15 Salute the brethren which are in Laodicea
Colossians4:16 And when this epistle is read among you
Colossians4:17 And say to Archippus
Colossians4:18 The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.
1Thessalonians1:1 Paul
1Thessalonians1:2 We give thanks to God always for you all
1Thessalonians1:3 Remembering without ceasing your work of faith
1Thessalonians1:4 Knowing
1Thessalonians1:5 For our gospel came not unto you in word only
1Thessalonians1:6 And ye became followers of us
1Thessalonians1:7 So that ye were ensamples to all that believe in Macedonia and Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 For from you sounded out the word of the Lord not only in Macedonia and Achaia
1Thessalonians1:9 For they themselves shew of us what manner of entering in we had unto you
1Thessalonians1:10 And to wait for his Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 For yourselves
1Thessalonians2:2 But even after that we had suffered before
1Thessalonians2:3 For our exhortation was not of deceit
1Thessalonians2:4 But as we were allowed of God to be put in trust with the gospel
1Thessalonians2:5 For neither at any time used we flattering words
1Thessalonians2:6 Nor of men sought we glory
1Thessalonians2:7 But we were gentle among you
1Thessalonians2:8 So being affectionately desirous of you
1Thessalonians2:9 For ye remember
1Thessalonians2:10 Ye are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 As ye know how we exhorted and comforted and charged every one of you
1Thessalonians2:12 That ye would walk worthy of God
1Thessalonians2:13 For this cause also thank we God without ceasing
1Thessalonians2:14 For ye
1Thessalonians2:15 Who both killed the Lord Jesus
1Thessalonians2:16 Forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they might be saved
1Thessalonians2:17 But we
1Thessalonians2:18 Wherefore we would have come unto you
1Thessalonians2:19 For what is our hope
1Thessalonians2:20 For ye are our glory and joy.
1Thessalonians3:1 Wherefore when we could no longer forbear
1Thessalonians3:2 And sent Timotheus
1Thessalonians3:3 That no man should be moved by these afflictions: for yourselves know that we are appointed thereunto.
1Thessalonians3:4 For verily
1Thessalonians3:5 For this cause
1Thessalonians3:6 But now when Timotheus came from you unto us
1Thessalonians3:7 Therefore
1Thessalonians3:8 For now we live
1Thessalonians3:9 For what thanks can we render to God again for you
1Thessalonians3:10 Night and day praying exceedingly that we might see your face
1Thessalonians3:11 Now God himself and our Father
1Thessalonians3:12 And the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another
1Thessalonians3:13 To the end he may stablish your hearts unblameable in holiness before God
1Thessalonians4:1 Furthermore then we beseech you
1Thessalonians4:2 For ye know what commandments we gave you by the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 For this is the will of God
1Thessalonians4:4 That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour;
1Thessalonians4:5 Not in the lust of concupiscence
1Thessalonians4:6 That no man go beyond and defraud his brother in any matter: because that the Lord is the avenger of all such
1Thessalonians4:7 For God hath not called us unto uncleanness
1Thessalonians4:8 He therefore that despiseth
1Thessalonians4:9 But as touching brotherly love ye need not that I write unto you: for ye yourselves are taught of God to love one another.
1Thessalonians4:10 And indeed ye do it toward all the brethren which are in all Macedonia: but we beseech you
1Thessalonians4:11 And that ye study to be quiet
1Thessalonians4:12 That ye may walk honestly toward them that are without
1Thessalonians4:13 But I would not have you to be ignorant
1Thessalonians4:14 For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout
1Thessalonians4:17 Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds
1Thessalonians4:18 Wherefore comfort one another with these words.
1Thessalonians5:1 But of the times and the seasons
1Thessalonians5:2 For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.
1Thessalonians5:3 For when they shall say
1Thessalonians5:4 But ye
1Thessalonians5:5 Ye are all the children of light
1Thessalonians5:6 Therefore let us not sleep
1Thessalonians5:7 For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night.
1Thessalonians5:8 But let us
1Thessalonians5:9 For God hath not appointed us to wrath
1Thessalonians5:10 Who died for us
1Thessalonians5:11 Wherefore comfort yourselves together
1Thessalonians5:12 And we beseech you
1Thessalonians5:13 And to esteem them very highly in love for their work's sake. And be at peace among yourselves.
1Thessalonians5:14 Now we exhort you
1Thessalonians5:15 See that none render evil for evil unto any man; but ever follow that which is good
1Thessalonians5:16 Rejoice evermore.
1Thessalonians5:17 Pray without ceasing.
1Thessalonians5:18 In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.
1Thessalonians5:19 Quench not the Spirit.
1Thessalonians5:20 Despise not prophesyings.
1Thessalonians5:21 Prove all things; hold fast that which is good.
1Thessalonians5:22 Abstain from all appearance of evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians5:24 Faithful is he that calleth you
1Thessalonians5:25 Brethren
1Thessalonians5:26 Greet all the brethren with an holy kiss.
1Thessalonians5:27 I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.
1Thessalonians5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
2Thessalonians1:1 Paul
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace unto you
2Thessalonians1:3 We are bound to thank God always for you
2Thessalonians1:4 So that we ourselves glory in you in the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that ye endure:
2Thessalonians1:5 Which is a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God
2Thessalonians1:6 Seeing it is a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you;
2Thessalonians1:7 And to you who are troubled rest with us
2Thessalonians1:8 In flaming fire taking vengeance on them that know not God
2Thessalonians1:9 Who shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord
2Thessalonians1:10 When he shall come to be glorified in his saints
2Thessalonians1:11 Wherefore also we pray always for you
2Thessalonians1:12 That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 Now we beseech you
2Thessalonians2:2 That ye be not soon shaken in mind
2Thessalonians2:3 Let no man deceive you by any means: for that day shall not come
2Thessalonians2:4 Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God
2Thessalonians2:5 Remember ye not
2Thessalonians2:6 And now ye know what withholdeth that he might be revealed in his time.
2Thessalonians2:7 For the mystery of iniquity doth already work: only he who now letteth will let
2Thessalonians2:8 And then shall that Wicked be revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 Even him
2Thessalonians2:10 And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish; because they received not the love of the truth
2Thessalonians2:11 And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion
2Thessalonians2:12 That they all might be damned who believed not the truth
2Thessalonians2:13 But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you
2Thessalonians2:14 Whereunto he called you by our gospel
2Thessalonians2:15 Therefore
2Thessalonians2:16 Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself
2Thessalonians2:17 Comfort your hearts
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 And that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men: for all men have not faith.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful
2Thessalonians3:4 And we have confidence in the Lord touching you
2Thessalonians3:5 And the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God
2Thessalonians3:6 Now we command you
2Thessalonians3:7 For yourselves know how ye ought to follow us: for we behaved not ourselves disorderly among you;
2Thessalonians3:8 Neither did we eat any man's bread for nought; but wrought with labour and travail night and day
2Thessalonians3:9 Not because we have not power
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 For we hear that there are some which walk among you disorderly
2Thessalonians3:12 Now them that are such we command and exhort by our Lord Jesus Christ
2Thessalonians3:13 But ye
2Thessalonians3:14 And if any man obey not our word by this epistle
2Thessalonians3:15 Yet count him not as an enemy
2Thessalonians3:16 Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord be with you all.
2Thessalonians3:17 The salutation of Paul with mine own hand
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
1Timothy1:1 Paul
1Timothy1:2 Unto Timothy
1Timothy1:3 As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus
1Timothy1:4 Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies
1Timothy1:5 Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart
1Timothy1:6 From which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling;
1Timothy1:7 Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say
1Timothy1:8 But we know that the law is good
1Timothy1:9 Knowing this
1Timothy1:10 For whoremongers
1Timothy1:11 According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God
1Timothy1:12 And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord
1Timothy1:13 Who was before a blasphemer
1Timothy1:14 And the grace of our Lord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy1:15 This is a faithful saying
1Timothy1:16 Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy
1Timothy1:17 Now unto the King eternal
1Timothy1:18 This charge I commit unto thee
1Timothy1:19 Holding faith
1Timothy1:20 Of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan
1Timothy2:1 I exhort therefore
1Timothy2:2 For kings
1Timothy2:3 For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;
1Timothy2:4 Who will have all men to be saved
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God
1Timothy2:6 Who gave himself a ransom for all
1Timothy2:7 Whereunto I am ordained a preacher
1Timothy2:8 I will therefore that men pray every where
1Timothy2:9 In like manner also
1Timothy2:10 But (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works.
1Timothy2:11 Let the woman learn in silence with all subjection.
1Timothy2:12 But I suffer not a woman to teach
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was first formed
1Timothy2:14 And Adam was not deceived
1Timothy2:15 Notwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing
1Timothy3:1 This is a true saying
1Timothy3:2 A bishop then must be blameless
1Timothy3:3 Not given to wine
1Timothy3:4 One that ruleth well his own house
1Timothy3:5 (For if a man know not how to rule his own house
1Timothy3:6 Not a novice
1Timothy3:7 Moreover he must have a good report of them which are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
1Timothy3:8 Likewise must the deacons be grave
1Timothy3:9 Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.
1Timothy3:10 And let these also first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon
1Timothy3:11 Even so must their wives be grave
1Timothy3:12 Let the deacons be the husbands of one wife
1Timothy3:13 For they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree
1Timothy3:14 These things write I unto thee
1Timothy3:15 But if I tarry long
1Timothy3:16 And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh
1Timothy4:1 Now the Spirit speaketh expressly
1Timothy4:2 Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;
1Timothy4:3 Forbidding to marry
1Timothy4:4 For every creature of God is good
1Timothy4:5 For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
1Timothy4:6 If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things
1Timothy4:7 But refuse profane and old wives' fables
1Timothy4:8 For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things
1Timothy4:9 This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.
1Timothy4:10 For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach
1Timothy4:11 These things command and teach.
1Timothy4:12 Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers
1Timothy4:13 Till I come
1Timothy4:14 Neglect not the gift that is in thee
1Timothy4:15 Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all.
1Timothy4:16 Take heed unto thyself
1Timothy5:1 Rebuke not an elder
1Timothy5:2 The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters
1Timothy5:3 Honour widows that are widows indeed.
1Timothy5:4 But if any widow have children or nephews
1Timothy5:5 Now she that is a widow indeed
1Timothy5:6 But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.
1Timothy5:7 And these things give in charge
1Timothy5:8 But if any provide not for his own
1Timothy5:9 Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old
1Timothy5:10 Well reported of for good works; if she have brought up children
1Timothy5:11 But the younger widows refuse: for when they have begun to wax wanton against Christ
1Timothy5:12 Having damnation
1Timothy5:13 And withal they learn to be idle
1Timothy5:14 I will therefore that the younger women marry
1Timothy5:15 For some are already turned aside after Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If any man or woman that believeth have widows
1Timothy5:17 Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour
1Timothy5:18 For the scripture saith
1Timothy5:19 Against an elder receive not an accusation
1Timothy5:20 Them that sin rebuke before all
1Timothy5:21 I charge thee before God
1Timothy5:22 Lay hands suddenly on no man
1Timothy5:23 Drink no longer water
1Timothy5:24 Some men's sins are open beforehand
1Timothy5:25 Likewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise cannot be hid.
1Timothy6:1 Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour
1Timothy6:2 And they that have believing masters
1Timothy6:3 If any man teach otherwise
1Timothy6:4 He is proud
1Timothy6:5 Perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds
1Timothy6:6 But godliness with contentment is great gain.
1Timothy6:7 For we brought nothing into this world
1Timothy6:8 And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.
1Timothy6:9 But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after
1Timothy6:11 But thou
1Timothy6:12 Fight the good fight of faith
1Timothy6:13 I give thee charge in the sight of God
1Timothy6:14 That thou keep this commandment without spot
1Timothy6:15 Which in his times he shall shew
1Timothy6:16 Who only hath immortality
1Timothy6:17 Charge them that are rich in this world
1Timothy6:18 That they do good
1Timothy6:19 Laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come
1Timothy6:20 O Timothy
1Timothy6:21 Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen.
2Timothy1:1 Paul
2Timothy1:2 To Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I thank God
2Timothy1:4 Greatly desiring to see thee
2Timothy1:5 When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee
2Timothy1:6 Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God
2Timothy1:7 For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power
2Timothy1:8 Be not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord
2Timothy1:9 Who hath saved us
2Timothy1:10 But is now made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ
2Timothy1:11 Whereunto I am appointed a preacher
2Timothy1:12 For the which cause I also suffer these things: nevertheless I am not ashamed: for I know whom I have believed
2Timothy1:13 Hold fast the form of sound words
2Timothy1:14 That good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the Holy Ghost which dwelleth in us.
2Timothy1:15 This thou knowest
2Timothy1:16 The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft refreshed me
2Timothy1:17 But
2Timothy1:18 The Lord grant unto him that he may find mercy of the Lord in that day: and in how many things he ministered unto me at Ephesus
2Timothy2:1 Thou therefore
2Timothy2:2 And the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses
2Timothy2:3 Thou therefore endure hardness
2Timothy2:4 No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier.
2Timothy2:5 And if a man also strive for masteries
2Timothy2:6 The husbandman that laboureth must be first partaker of the fruits.
2Timothy2:7 Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.
2Timothy2:8 Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my gospel:
2Timothy2:9 Wherein I suffer trouble
2Timothy2:10 Therefore I endure all things for the elect's sakes
2Timothy2:11 It is a faithful saying: For if we be dead with him
2Timothy2:12 If we suffer
2Timothy2:13 If we believe not
2Timothy2:14 Of these things put them in remembrance
2Timothy2:15 Study to shew thyself approved unto God
2Timothy2:16 But shun profane and vain babblings: for they will increase unto more ungodliness.
2Timothy2:17 And their word will eat as doth a canker: of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus;
2Timothy2:18 Who concerning the truth have erred
2Timothy2:19 Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure
2Timothy2:20 But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver
2Timothy2:21 If a man therefore purge himself from these
2Timothy2:22 Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness
2Timothy2:23 But foolish and unlearned questions avoid
2Timothy2:24 And the servant of the Lord must not strive; but be gentle unto all men
2Timothy2:25 In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;
2Timothy2:26 And that they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil
2Timothy3:1 This know also
2Timothy3:2 For men shall be lovers of their own selves
2Timothy3:3 Without natural affection
2Timothy3:4 Traitors
2Timothy3:5 Having a form of godliness
2Timothy3:6 For of this sort are they which creep into houses
2Timothy3:7 Ever learning
2Timothy3:8 Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses
2Timothy3:9 But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men
2Timothy3:10 But thou hast fully known my doctrine
2Timothy3:11 Persecutions
2Timothy3:12 Yea
2Timothy3:13 But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse
2Timothy3:14 But continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of
2Timothy3:15 And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures
2Timothy3:16 All scripture is given by inspiration of God
2Timothy3:17 That the man of God may be perfect
2Timothy4:1 I charge thee therefore before God
2Timothy4:2 Preach the word; be instant in season
2Timothy4:3 For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers
2Timothy4:4 And they shall turn away their ears from the truth
2Timothy4:5 But watch thou in all things
2Timothy4:6 For I am now ready to be offered
2Timothy4:7 I have fought a good fight
2Timothy4:8 Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness
2Timothy4:9 Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:
2Timothy4:10 For Demas hath forsaken me
2Timothy4:11 Only Luke is with me. Take Mark
2Timothy4:12 And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.
2Timothy4:13 The cloke that I left at Troas with Carpus
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his works:
2Timothy4:15 Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly withstood our words.
2Timothy4:16 At my first answer no man stood with me
2Timothy4:17 Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me
2Timothy4:18 And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work
2Timothy4:19 Salute Prisca and Aquila
2Timothy4:20 Erastus abode at Corinth: but Trophimus have I left at Miletum sick.
2Timothy4:21 Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee
2Timothy4:22 The Lord Jesus Christ be with thy spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.
Titus1:1 Paul
Titus1:2 In hope of eternal life
Titus1:3 But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching
Titus1:4 To Titus
Titus1:5 For this cause left I thee in Crete
Titus1:6 If any be blameless
Titus1:7 For a bishop must be blameless
Titus1:8 But a lover of hospitality
Titus1:9 Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught
Titus1:10 For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers
Titus1:11 Whose mouths must be stopped
Titus1:12 One of themselves
Titus1:13 This witness is true. Wherefore rebuke them sharply
Titus1:14 Not giving heed to Jewish fables
Titus1:15 Unto the pure all things are pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled.
Titus1:16 They profess that they know God; but in works they deny him
Titus2:1 But speak thou the things which become sound doctrine:
Titus2:2 That the aged men be sober
Titus2:3 The aged women likewise
Titus2:4 That they may teach the young women to be sober
Titus2:5 To be discreet
Titus2:6 Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.
Titus2:7 In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness
Titus2:8 Sound speech
Titus2:9 Exhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters
Titus2:10 Not purloining
Titus2:11 For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men
Titus2:12 Teaching us that
Titus2:13 Looking for that blessed hope
Titus2:14 Who gave himself for us
Titus2:15 These things speak
Titus3:1 Put them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers
Titus3:2 To speak evil of no man
Titus3:3 For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish
Titus3:4 But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared
Titus3:5 Not by works of righteousness which we have done
Titus3:6 Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;
Titus3:7 That being justified by his grace
Titus3:8 This is a faithful saying
Titus3:9 But avoid foolish questions
Titus3:10 A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject;
Titus3:11 Knowing that he that is such is subverted
Titus3:12 When I shall send Artemas unto thee
Titus3:13 Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently
Titus3:14 And let our's also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses
Titus3:15 All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
Philemon1:1 Paul
Philemon1:2 And to our beloved Apphia
Philemon1:3 Grace to you
Philemon1:4 I thank my God
Philemon1:5 Hearing of thy love and faith
Philemon1:6 That the communication of thy faith may become effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.
Philemon1:7 For we have great joy and consolation in thy love
Philemon1:8 Wherefore
Philemon1:9 Yet for love's sake I rather beseech thee
Philemon1:10 I beseech thee for my son Onesimus
Philemon1:11 Which in time past was to thee unprofitable
Philemon1:12 Whom I have sent again: thou therefore receive him
Philemon1:13 Whom I would have retained with me
Philemon1:14 But without thy mind would I do nothing; that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity
Philemon1:15 For perhaps he therefore departed for a season
Philemon1:16 Not now as a servant
Philemon1:17 If thou count me therefore a partner
Philemon1:18 If he hath wronged thee
Philemon1:19 I Paul have written it with mine own hand
Philemon1:20 Yea
Philemon1:21 Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee
Philemon1:22 But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I trust that through your prayers I shall be given unto you.
Philemon1:23 There salute thee Epaphras
Philemon1:24 Marcus
Philemon1:25 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
Hebrews1:1 God
Hebrews1:2 Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his Son
Hebrews1:3 Who being the brightness of his glory
Hebrews1:4 Being made so much better than the angels
Hebrews1:5 For unto which of the angels said he at any time
Hebrews1:6 And again
Hebrews1:7 And of the angels he saith
Hebrews1:8 But unto the Son he saith
Hebrews1:9 Thou hast loved righteousness
Hebrews1:10 And
Hebrews1:11 They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall wax old as doth a garment;
Hebrews1:12 And as a vesture shalt thou fold them up
Hebrews1:13 But to which of the angels said he at any time
Hebrews1:14 Are they not all ministering spirits
Hebrews2:1 Therefore we ought to give the more earnest heed to the things which we have heard
Hebrews2:2 For if the word spoken by angels was stedfast
Hebrews2:3 How shall we escape
Hebrews2:4 God also bearing them witness
Hebrews2:5 For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come
Hebrews2:6 But one in a certain place testified
Hebrews2:7 Thou madest him a little lower than the angels; thou crownedst him with glory and honour
Hebrews2:8 Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet. For in that he put all in subjection under him
Hebrews2:9 But we see Jesus
Hebrews2:10 For it became him
Hebrews2:11 For both he that sanctifieth and they who are sanctified are all of one: for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brethren
Hebrews2:12 Saying
Hebrews2:13 And again
Hebrews2:14 Forasmuch then as the children are partakers of flesh and blood
Hebrews2:15 And deliver them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.
Hebrews2:16 For verily he took not on him the nature of angels; but he took on him the seed of Abraham.
Hebrews2:17 Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren
Hebrews2:18 For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted
Hebrews3:1 Wherefore
Hebrews3:2 Who was faithful to him that appointed him
Hebrews3:3 For this man was counted worthy of more glory than Moses
Hebrews3:4 For every house is builded by some man; but he that built all things is God.
Hebrews3:5 And Moses verily was faithful in all his house
Hebrews3:6 But Christ as a son over his own house; whose house are we
Hebrews3:7 Wherefore (as the Holy Ghost saith
Hebrews3:8 Harden not your hearts
Hebrews3:9 When your fathers tempted me
Hebrews3:10 Wherefore I was grieved with that generation
Hebrews3:11 So I sware in my wrath
Hebrews3:12 Take heed
Hebrews3:13 But exhort one another daily
Hebrews3:14 For we are made partakers of Christ
Hebrews3:15 While it is said
Hebrews3:16 For some
Hebrews3:17 But with whom was he grieved forty years? was it not with them that had sinned
Hebrews3:18 And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest
Hebrews3:19 So we see that they could not enter in because of unbelief.
Hebrews4:1 Let us therefore fear
Hebrews4:2 For unto us was the gospel preached
Hebrews4:3 For we which have believed do enter into rest
Hebrews4:4 For he spake in a certain place of the seventh day on this wise
Hebrews4:5 And in this place again
Hebrews4:6 Seeing therefore it remaineth that some must enter therein
Hebrews4:7 Again
Hebrews4:8 For if Joshua had given them rest
Hebrews4:9 There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.
Hebrews4:10 For he that is entered into his rest
Hebrews4:11 Let us labour therefore to enter into that rest
Hebrews4:12 For the word of God is quick
Hebrews4:13 Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do.
Hebrews4:14 Seeing then that we have a great high priest
Hebrews4:15 For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as we are
Hebrews4:16 Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of grace
Hebrews5:1 For every high priest taken from among men is ordained for men in things pertaining to God
Hebrews5:2 Who can have compassion on the ignorant
Hebrews5:3 And by reason hereof he ought
Hebrews5:4 And no man taketh this honour unto himself
Hebrews5:5 So also Christ glorified not himself to be made an high priest; but he that said unto him
Hebrews5:6 As he saith also in another place
Hebrews5:7 Who in the days of his flesh
Hebrews5:8 Though he were a Son
Hebrews5:9 And being made perfect
Hebrews5:10 Called of God an high priest after the order of Melchisedec.
Hebrews5:11 Of whom we have many things to say
Hebrews5:12 For when for the time ye ought to be teachers
Hebrews5:13 For every one that useth milk is unskilful in the word of righteousness: for he is a babe.
Hebrews5:14 But strong meat belongeth to them that are of full age
Hebrews6:1 Therefore leaving the principles of the doctrine of Christ
Hebrews6:2 Of the doctrine of baptisms
Hebrews6:3 And this will we do
Hebrews6:4 For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened
Hebrews6:5 And have tasted the good word of God
Hebrews6:6 If they shall fall away
Hebrews6:7 For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it
Hebrews6:8 But that which beareth thorns and briers is rejected
Hebrews6:9 But
Hebrews6:10 For God is not unrighteous to forget your work and labour of love
Hebrews6:11 And we desire that every one of you do shew the same diligence to the full assurance of hope unto the end:
Hebrews6:12 That ye be not slothful
Hebrews6:13 For when God made promise to Abraham
Hebrews6:14 Saying
Hebrews6:15 And so
Hebrews6:16 For men verily swear by the greater: and an oath for confirmation is to them an end of all strife.
Hebrews6:17 Wherein God
Hebrews6:18 That by two immutable things
Hebrews6:19 Which hope we have as an anchor of the soul
Hebrews6:20 Whither the forerunner is for us entered
Hebrews7:1 For this Melchisedec
Hebrews7:2 To whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all; first being by interpretation King of righteousness
Hebrews7:3 Without father
Hebrews7:4 Now consider how great this man was
Hebrews7:5 And verily they that are of the sons of Levi
Hebrews7:6 But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham
Hebrews7:7 And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.
Hebrews7:8 And here men that die receive tithes; but there he receiveth them
Hebrews7:9 And as I may so say
Hebrews7:10 For he was yet in the loins of his father
Hebrews7:11 If therefore perfection were by the Levitical priesthood
Hebrews7:12 For the priesthood being changed
Hebrews7:13 For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe
Hebrews7:14 For it is evident that our Lord sprang out of Juda; of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.
Hebrews7:15 And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest
Hebrews7:16 Who is made
Hebrews7:17 For he testifieth
Hebrews7:18 For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.
Hebrews7:19 For the law made nothing perfect
Hebrews7:20 And inasmuch as not without an oath he was made priest:
Hebrews7:21 (For those priests were made without an oath; but this with an oath by him that said unto him
Hebrews7:22 By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better testament.
Hebrews7:23 And they truly were many priests
Hebrews7:24 But this man
Hebrews7:25 Wherefore he is able also to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by him
Hebrews7:26 For such an high priest became us
Hebrews7:27 Who needeth not daily
Hebrews7:28 For the law maketh men high priests which have infirmity; but the word of the oath
Hebrews8:1 Now of the things which we have spoken this is the sum: We have such an high priest
Hebrews8:2 A minister of the sanctuary
Hebrews8:3 For every high priest is ordained to offer gifts and sacrifices: wherefore it is of necessity that this man have somewhat also to offer.
Hebrews8:4 For if he were on earth
Hebrews8:5 Who serve unto the example and shadow of heavenly things
Hebrews8:6 But now hath he obtained a more excellent ministry
Hebrews8:7 For if that first covenant had been faultless
Hebrews8:8 For finding fault with them
Hebrews8:9 Not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they continued not in my covenant
Hebrews8:10 For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days
Hebrews8:11 And they shall not teach every man his neighbour
Hebrews8:12 For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness
Hebrews8:13 In that he saith
Hebrews9:1 Then verily the first covenant had also ordinances of divine service
Hebrews9:2 For there was a tabernacle made; the first
Hebrews9:3 And after the second veil
Hebrews9:4 Which had the golden censer
Hebrews9:5 And over it the cherubims of glory shadowing the mercyseat; of which we cannot now speak particularly.
Hebrews9:6 Now when these things were thus ordained
Hebrews9:7 But into the second went the high priest alone once every year
Hebrews9:8 The Holy Ghost this signifying
Hebrews9:9 Which was a figure for the time then present
Hebrews9:10 Which stood only in meats and drinks
Hebrews9:11 But Christ being come an high priest of good things to come
Hebrews9:12 Neither by the blood of goats and calves
Hebrews9:13 For if the blood of bulls and of goats
Hebrews9:14 How much more shall the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament
Hebrews9:16 For where a testament is
Hebrews9:17 For a testament is of force after men are dead: otherwise it is of no strength at all while the testator liveth.
Hebrews9:18 Whereupon neither the first testament was dedicated without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when Moses had spoken every precept to all the people according to the law
Hebrews9:20 Saying
Hebrews9:21 Moreover he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle
Hebrews9:22 And almost all things are by the law purged with blood; and without shedding of blood is no remission.
Hebrews9:23 It was therefore necessary that the patterns of things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.
Hebrews9:24 For Christ is not entered into the holy places made with hands
Hebrews9:25 Nor yet that he should offer himself often
Hebrews9:26 For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once in the end of the world hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
Hebrews9:27 And as it is appointed unto men once to die
Hebrews9:28 So Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many; and unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time without sin unto salvation.
Hebrews10:1 For the law having a shadow of good things to come
Hebrews10:2 For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once purged should have had no more conscience of sins.
Hebrews10:3 But in those sacrifices there is a remembrance again made of sins every year.
Hebrews10:4 For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.
Hebrews10:5 Wherefore when he cometh into the world
Hebrews10:6 In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin thou hast had no pleasure.
Hebrews10:7 Then said I
Hebrews10:8 Above when he said
Hebrews10:9 Then said he
Hebrews10:10 By the which will we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
Hebrews10:11 And every priest standeth daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices
Hebrews10:12 But this man
Hebrews10:13 From henceforth expecting till his enemies be made his footstool.
Hebrews10:14 For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.
Hebrews10:15 Whereof the Holy Ghost also is a witness to us: for after that he had said before
Hebrews10:16 This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days
Hebrews10:17 And their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.
Hebrews10:18 Now where remission of these is
Hebrews10:19 Having therefore
Hebrews10:20 By a new and living way
Hebrews10:21 And having an high priest over the house of God;
Hebrews10:22 Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith
Hebrews10:23 Let us hold fast the profession of our faith without wavering; (for he is faithful that promised;)
Hebrews10:24 And let us consider one another to provoke unto love and to good works:
Hebrews10:25 Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together
Hebrews10:26 For if we sin wilfully after that we have received the knowledge of the truth
Hebrews10:27 But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery indignation
Hebrews10:28 He that despised Moses' law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:
Hebrews10:29 Of how much sorer punishment
Hebrews10:30 For we know him that hath said
Hebrews10:31 It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
Hebrews10:32 But call to remembrance the former days
Hebrews10:33 Partly
Hebrews10:34 For ye had compassion of me in my bonds
Hebrews10:35 Cast not away therefore your confidence
Hebrews10:36 For ye have need of patience
Hebrews10:37 For yet a little while
Hebrews10:38 Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back
Hebrews10:39 But we are not of them who draw back unto perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.
Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the substance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 For by it the elders obtained a good report.
Hebrews11:3 Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found
Hebrews11:6 But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:9 By faith he sojourned in the land of promise
Hebrews11:10 For he looked for a city which hath foundations
Hebrews11:11 Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed
Hebrews11:12 Therefore sprang there even of one
Hebrews11:13 These all died in faith
Hebrews11:14 For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a country.
Hebrews11:15 And truly
Hebrews11:16 But now they desire a better country
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 Of whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 Accounting that God was able to raise him up
Hebrews11:20 By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God
Hebrews11:26 Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.
Hebrews11:27 By faith he forsook Egypt
Hebrews11:28 Through faith he kept the passover
Hebrews11:29 By faith they passed through the Red sea as by dry land: which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down
Hebrews11:31 By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not
Hebrews11:32 And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon
Hebrews11:33 Who through faith subdued kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 Quenched the violence of fire
Hebrews11:35 Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured
Hebrews11:36 And others had trial of cruel mockings and scourgings
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 (Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts
Hebrews11:39 And these all
Hebrews11:40 God having provided some better thing for us
Hebrews12:1 Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses
Hebrews12:2 Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross
Hebrews12:3 For consider him that endured such contradiction of sinners against himself
Hebrews12:4 Ye have not yet resisted unto blood
Hebrews12:5 And ye have forgotten the exhortation which speaketh unto you as unto children
Hebrews12:6 For whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth
Hebrews12:7 If ye endure chastening
Hebrews12:8 But if ye be without chastisement
Hebrews12:9 Furthermore we have had fathers of our flesh which corrected us
Hebrews12:10 For they verily for a few days chastened us after their own pleasure; but he for our profit
Hebrews12:11 Now no chastening for the present seemeth to be joyous
Hebrews12:12 Wherefore lift up the hands which hang down
Hebrews12:13 And make straight paths for your feet
Hebrews12:14 Follow peace with all men
Hebrews12:15 Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you
Hebrews12:16 Lest there be any fornicator
Hebrews12:17 For ye know how that afterward
Hebrews12:18 For ye are not come unto the mount that might be touched
Hebrews12:19 And the sound of a trumpet
Hebrews12:20 (For they could not endure that which was commanded
Hebrews12:21 And so terrible was the sight
Hebrews12:22 But ye are come unto mount Sion
Hebrews12:23 To the general assembly and church of the firstborn
Hebrews12:24 And to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant
Hebrews12:25 See that ye refuse not him that speaketh. For if they escaped not who refused him that spake on earth
Hebrews12:26 Whose voice then shook the earth: but now he hath promised
Hebrews12:27 And this word
Hebrews12:28 Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved
Hebrews12:29 For our God is a consuming fire.
Hebrews13:1 Let brotherly love continue.
Hebrews13:2 Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.
Hebrews13:3 Remember them that are in bonds
Hebrews13:4 Marriage is honourable in all
Hebrews13:5 Let your conversation be without covetousness; and be content with such things as ye have: for he hath said
Hebrews13:6 So that we may boldly say
Hebrews13:7 Remember them which have the rule over you
Hebrews13:8 Jesus Christ the same yesterday
Hebrews13:9 Be not carried about with divers and strange doctrines. For it is a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats
Hebrews13:10 We have an altar
Hebrews13:11 For the bodies of those beasts
Hebrews13:12 Wherefore Jesus also
Hebrews13:13 Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp
Hebrews13:14 For here have we no continuing city
Hebrews13:15 By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually
Hebrews13:16 But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.
Hebrews13:17 Obey them that have the rule over you
Hebrews13:18 Pray for us: for we trust we have a good conscience
Hebrews13:19 But I beseech you the rather to do this
Hebrews13:20 Now the God of peace
Hebrews13:21 Make you perfect in every good work to do his will
Hebrews13:22 And I beseech you
Hebrews13:23 Know ye that our brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom
Hebrews13:24 Salute all them that have the rule over you
Hebrews13:25 Grace be with you all. Amen.
James1:1 James
James1:2 My brethren
James1:3 Knowing this
James1:4 But let patience have her perfect work
James1:5 If any of you lack wisdom
James1:6 But let him ask in faith
James1:7 For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord.
James1:8 A double minded man is unstable in all his ways.
James1:9 Let the brother of low degree rejoice in that he is exalted:
James1:10 But the rich
James1:11 For the sun is no sooner risen with a burning heat
James1:12 Blessed is the man that endureth temptation: for when he is tried
James1:13 Let no man say when he is tempted
James1:14 But every man is tempted
James1:15 Then when lust hath conceived
James1:16 Do not err
James1:17 Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above
James1:18 Of his own will begat he us with the word of truth
James1:19 Wherefore
James1:20 For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.
James1:21 Wherefore lay apart all filthiness and superfluity of naughtiness
James1:22 But be ye doers of the word
James1:23 For if any be a hearer of the word
James1:24 For he beholdeth himself
James1:25 But whoso looketh into the perfect law of liberty
James1:26 If any man among you seem to be religious
James1:27 Pure religion and undefiled before God and the Father is this
James2:1 My brethren
James2:2 For if there come unto your assembly a man with a gold ring
James2:3 And ye have respect to him that weareth the gay clothing
James2:4 Are ye not then partial in yourselves
James2:5 Hearken
James2:6 But ye have despised the poor. Do not rich men oppress you
James2:7 Do not they blaspheme that worthy name by the which ye are called?
James2:8 If ye fulfil the royal law according to the scripture
James2:9 But if ye have respect to persons
James2:10 For whosoever shall keep the whole law
James2:11 For he that said
James2:12 So speak ye
James2:13 For he shall have judgment without mercy
James2:14 What doth it profit
James2:15 If a brother or sister be naked
James2:16 And one of you say unto them
James2:17 Even so faith
James2:18 Yea
James2:19 Thou believest that there is one God; thou doest well: the devils also believe
James2:20 But wilt thou know
James2:21 Was not Abraham our father justified by works
James2:22 Seest thou how faith wrought with his works
James2:23 And the scripture was fulfilled which saith
James2:24 Ye see then how that by works a man is justified
James2:25 Likewise also was not Rahab the harlot justified by works
James2:26 For as the body without the spirit is dead
James3:1 My brethren
James3:2 For in many things we offend all. If any man offend not in word
James3:3 Behold
James3:4 Behold also the ships
James3:5 Even so the tongue is a little member
James3:6 And the tongue is a fire
James3:7 For every kind of beasts
James3:8 But the tongue can no man tame; it is an unruly evil
James3:9 Therewith bless we God
James3:10 Out of the same mouth proceedeth blessing and cursing. My brethren
James3:11 Doth a fountain send forth at the same place sweet water and bitter?
James3:12 Can the fig tree
James3:13 Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom.
James3:14 But if ye have bitter envying and strife in your hearts
James3:15 This wisdom descendeth not from above
James3:16 For where envying and strife is
James3:17 But the wisdom that is from above is first pure
James3:18 And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace of them that make peace.

James4:2 Ye lust
James4:3 Ye ask
James4:4 Ye adulterers and adulteresses
James4:5 Do ye think that the scripture saith in vain
James4:6 But he giveth more grace. Wherefore he saith
James4:7 Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil
James4:8 Draw nigh to God
James4:9 Be afflicted
James4:10 Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord
James4:11 Speak not evil one of another
James4:12 There is one lawgiver
James4:13 Go to now
James4:14 Whereas ye know not what shall be on the morrow. For what is your life? It is even a vapour
James4:15 For that ye ought to say
James4:16 But now ye rejoice in your boastings: all such rejoicing is evil.
James4:17 Therefore to him that knoweth to do good
James5:1 Go to now
James5:2 Your riches are corrupted
James5:3 Your gold and silver is cankered; and the rust of them shall be a witness against you
James5:4 Behold
James5:5 Ye have lived in pleasure on the earth
James5:6 Ye have condemned and killed the just; and he doth not resist you.
James5:7 Be patient therefore
James5:8 Be ye also patient; stablish your hearts: for the coming of the Lord draweth nigh.
James5:9 Grudge not one against another
James5:10 Take
James5:11 Behold
James5:12 But above all things

James5:15 And the prayer of faith shall save the sick
James5:16 Confess your faults one to another
James5:17 Elias was a man subject to like passions as we are
James5:18 And he prayed again

2Peter1:1 Simon Peter
2Peter1:2 Grace and peace be multiplied unto you through the knowledge of God
2Peter1:3 According as his divine power hath given unto us all things that pertain unto life and godliness
2Peter1:4 Whereby are given unto us exceeding great and precious promises: that by these ye might be partakers of the divine nature
2Peter1:5 And beside this
2Peter1:6 And to knowledge temperance; and to temperance patience; and to patience godliness;
2Peter1:7 And to godliness brotherly kindness; and to brotherly kindness charity.
2Peter1:8 For if these things be in you
2Peter1:9 But he that lacketh these things is blind
2Peter1:10 Wherefore the rather
2Peter1:11 For so an entrance shall be ministered unto you abundantly into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ.
2Peter1:12 Wherefore I will not be negligent to put you always in remembrance of these things
2Peter1:13 Yea
2Peter1:14 Knowing that shortly I must put off this my tabernacle
2Peter1:15 Moreover I will endeavour that ye may be able after my decease to have these things always in remembrance.
2Peter1:16 For we have not followed cunningly devised fables
2Peter1:17 For he received from God the Father honour and glory
2Peter1:18 And this voice which came from heaven we heard
2Peter1:19 We have also a more sure word of prophecy; whereunto ye do well that ye take heed
2Peter1:20 Knowing this first
2Peter1:21 For the prophecy came not in old time by the will of man: but holy men of God spake as they were moved by the Holy Ghost.
2Peter2:1 But there were false prophets also among the people
2Peter2:2 And many shall follow their pernicious ways; by reason of whom the way of truth shall be evil spoken of.
2Peter2:3 And through covetousness shall they with feigned words make merchandise of you: whose judgment now of a long time lingereth not
2Peter2:4 For if God spared not the angels that sinned
2Peter2:5 And spared not the old world
2Peter2:6 And turning the cities of Sodom and Gomorrha into ashes condemned them with an overthrow
2Peter2:7 And delivered just Lot
2Peter2:8 (For that righteous man dwelling among them
2Peter2:9 The Lord knoweth how to deliver the godly out of temptations
2Peter2:10 But chiefly them that walk after the flesh in the lust of uncleanness
2Peter2:11 Whereas angels
2Peter2:12 But these
2Peter2:13 And shall receive the reward of unrighteousness
2Peter2:14 Having eyes full of adultery
2Peter2:15 Which have forsaken the right way
2Peter2:16 But was rebuked for his iniquity: the dumb ass speaking with man's voice forbad the madness of the prophet.
2Peter2:17 These are wells without water
2Peter2:18 For when they speak great swelling words of vanity
2Peter2:19 While they promise them liberty
2Peter2:20 For if after they have escaped the pollutions of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ
2Peter2:21 For it had been better for them not to have known the way of righteousness
2Peter2:22 But it is happened unto them according to the true proverb

Revelation4:3 And he that sat was to look upon like a jasper and a sardine stone: and there was a rainbow round about the throne
Revelation4:4 And round about the throne were four and twenty seats: and upon the seats I saw four and twenty elders sitting
Revelation4:5 And out of the throne proceeded lightnings and thunderings and voices: and there were seven lamps of fire burning before the throne
Revelation4:6 And before the throne there was a sea of glass like unto crystal: and in the midst of the throne
Revelation4:7 And the first beast was like a lion
Revelation4:8 And the four beasts had each of them six wings about him; and they were full of eyes within: and they rest not day and night
Revelation4:9 And when those beasts give glory and honour and thanks to him that sat on the throne
Revelation4:10 The four and twenty elders fall down before him that sat on the throne
Revelation4:11 Thou art worthy
Revelation5:1 And I saw in the right hand of him that sat on the throne a book written within and on the backside
Revelation5:2 And I saw a strong angel proclaiming with a loud voice
Revelation5:3 And no man in heaven
Revelation5:4 And I wept much
Revelation5:5 And one of the elders saith unto me
Revelation5:6 And I beheld
Revelation5:7 And he came and took the book out of the right hand of him that sat upon the throne.
Revelation5:8 And when he had taken the book
Revelation5:9 And they sung a new song
Revelation5:10 And hast made us unto our God kings and priests: and we shall reign on the earth.
Revelation5:11 And I beheld
Revelation5:12 Saying with a loud voice
Revelation5:13 And every creature which is in heaven
Revelation5:14 And the four beasts said

Revelation6:2 And I saw
Revelation6:3 And when he had opened the second seal

Revelation6:5 And when he had opened the third seal
Revelation6:6 And I heard a voice in the midst of the four beasts say
Revelation6:7 And when he had opened the fourth seal
Revelation6:8 And I looked
Revelation6:9 And when he had opened the fifth seal
Revelation6:10 And they cried with a loud voice
Revelation6:11 And white robes were given unto every one of them; and it was said unto them
Revelation6:12 And I beheld when he had opened the sixth seal
Revelation6:13 And the stars of heaven fell unto the earth
Revelation6:14 And the heaven departed as a scroll when it is rolled together; and every mountain and island were moved out of their places.
Revelation6:15 And the kings of the earth
Revelation6:16 And said to the mountains and rocks
Revelation6:17 For the great day of his wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?
Revelation7:1 And after these things I saw four angels standing on the four corners of the earth
Revelation7:2 And I saw another angel ascending from the east
Revelation7:3 Saying
Revelation7:4 And I heard the number of them which were sealed: and there were sealed an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel.
Revelation7:5 Of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Reuben were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Gad were sealed twelve thousand.
Revelation7:6 Of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Nephthalim were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Manasses were sealed twelve thousand.
Revelation7:7 Of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Levi were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Issachar were sealed twelve thousand.
Revelation7:8 Of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Joseph were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand.
Revelation7:9 After this I beheld
Revelation7:10 And cried with a loud voice
Revelation7:11 And all the angels stood round about the throne
Revelation7:12 Saying
Revelation7:13 And one of the elders answered
Revelation7:14 And I said unto him
Revelation7:15 Therefore are they before the throne of God
Revelation7:16 They shall hunger no more
Revelation7:17 For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them
Revelation8:1 And when he had opened the seventh seal
Revelation8:2 And I saw the seven angels which stood before God; and to them were given seven trumpets.
Revelation8:3 And another angel came and stood at the altar
Revelation8:4 And the smoke of the incense
Revelation8:5 And the angel took the censer
Revelation8:6 And the seven angels which had the seven trumpets prepared themselves to sound.
Revelation8:7 The first angel sounded
Revelation8:8 And the second angel sounded

Revelation8:10 And the third angel sounded
Revelation8:11 And the name of the star is called Wormwood: and the third part of the waters became wormwood; and many men died of the waters
Revelation8:12 And the fourth angel sounded

Revelation9:1 And the fifth angel sounded
Revelation9:2 And he opened the bottomless pit; and there arose a smoke out of the pit
Revelation9:3 And there came out of the smoke locusts upon the earth: and unto them was given power
Revelation9:4 And it was commanded them that they should not hurt the grass of the earth
Revelation9:5 And to them it was given that they should not kill them
Revelation9:6 And in those days shall men seek death
Revelation9:7 And the shapes of the locusts were like unto horses prepared unto battle; and on their heads were as it were crowns like gold
Revelation9:8 And they had hair as the hair of women
Revelation9:9 And they had breastplates
Revelation9:10 And they had tails like unto scorpions
Revelation9:11 And they had a king over them
Revelation9:12 One woe is past; and
Revelation9:13 And the sixth angel sounded
Revelation9:14 Saying to the sixth angel which had the trumpet
Revelation9:15 And the four angels were loosed
Revelation9:16 And the number of the army of the horsemen were two hundred thousand thousand: and I heard the number of them.
Revelation9:17 And thus I saw the horses in the vision
Revelation9:18 By these three was the third part of men killed
Revelation9:19 For their power is in their mouth
Revelation9:20 And the rest of the men which were not killed by these plagues yet repented not of the works of their hands
Revelation9:21 Neither repented they of their murders

Revelation12:1 And there appeared a great wonder in heaven; a woman clothed with the sun
Revelation12:2 And she being with child cried
Revelation12:3 And there appeared another wonder in heaven; and behold a great red dragon
Revelation12:4 And his tail drew the third part of the stars of heaven
Revelation12:5 And she brought forth a man child
Revelation12:6 And the woman fled into the wilderness
Revelation12:7 And there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought against the dragon; and the dragon fought and his angels
Revelation12:8 And prevailed not; neither was their place found any more in heaven.
Revelation12:9 And the great dragon was cast out
Revelation12:10 And I heard a loud voice saying in heaven

Revelation12:12 Therefore rejoice

Revelation12:15 And the serpent cast out of his mouth water as a flood after the woman
Revelation12:16 And the earth helped the woman
Revelation12:17 And the dragon was wroth with the woman
Revelation13:1 And I stood upon the sand of the sea
Revelation13:2 And the beast which I saw was like unto a leopard
Revelation13:3 And I saw one of his heads as it were wounded to death; and his deadly wound was healed: and all the world wondered after the beast.
Revelation13:4 And they worshipped the dragon which gave power unto the beast: and they worshipped the beast
Revelation13:5 And there was given unto him a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies; and power was given unto him to continue forty and two months.
Revelation13:6 And he opened his mouth in blasphemy against God
Revelation13:7 And it was given unto him to make war with the saints
Revelation13:8 And all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him
Revelation13:9 If any man have an ear
Revelation13:10 He that leadeth into captivity shall go into captivity: he that killeth with the sword must be killed with the sword. Here is the patience and the faith of the saints.
Revelation13:11 And I beheld another beast coming up out of the earth; and he had two horns like a lamb
Revelation13:12 And he exerciseth all the power of the first beast before him
Revelation13:13 And he doeth great wonders
Revelation13:14 And deceiveth them that dwell on the earth by the means of those miracles which he had power to do in the sight of the beast; saying to them that dwell on the earth
Revelation13:15 And he had power to give life unto the image of the beast
Revelation13:16 And he causeth all
Revelation13:17 And that no man might buy or sell
Revelation13:18 Here is wisdom. Let him that hath understanding count the number of the beast: for it is the number of a man; and his number is Six hundred threescore and six.

Revelation16:6 For they have shed the blood of saints and prophets
Revelation16:7 And I heard another out of the altar say

Revelation16:9 And men were scorched with great heat

Revelation16:11 And blasphemed the God of heaven because of their pains and their sores

Revelation16:14 For they are the spirits of devils
Revelation16:15 Behold

Revelation18:1 And after these things I saw another angel come down from heaven
Revelation18:2 And he cried mightily with a strong voice
Revelation18:3 For all nations have drunk of the wine of the wrath of her fornication
Revelation18:4 And I heard another voice from heaven
Revelation18:5 For her sins have reached unto heaven
Revelation18:6 Reward her even as she rewarded you
Revelation18:7 How much she hath glorified herself
Revelation18:8 Therefore shall her plagues come in one day
Revelation18:9 And the kings of the earth
Revelation18:10 Standing afar off for the fear of her torment
Revelation18:11 And the merchants of the earth shall weep and mourn over her; for no man buyeth their merchandise any more:
Revelation18:12 The merchandise of gold
Revelation18:13 And cinnamon
Revelation18:14 And the fruits that thy soul lusted after are departed from thee
Revelation18:15 The merchants of these things
Revelation18:16 And saying
Revelation18:17 For in one hour so great riches is come to nought. And every shipmaster
Revelation18:18 And cried when they saw the smoke of her burning

Revelation18:20 Rejoice over her

Revelation18:22 And the voice of harpers
Revelation18:23 And the light of a candle shall shine no more at all in thee; and the voice of the bridegroom and of the bride shall be heard no more at all in thee: for thy merchants were the great men of the earth; for by thy sorceries were all nations deceived.
Revelation18:24 And in her was found the blood of prophets

Revelation20:15 And whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire.
Revelation21:1 And I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away; and there was no more sea.
Revelation21:2 And I John saw the holy city
Revelation21:3 And I heard a great voice out of heaven saying
Revelation21:4 And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death
Revelation21:5 And he that sat upon the throne said
Revelation21:6 And he said unto me
Revelation21:7 He that overcometh shall inherit all things; and I will be his God
Revelation21:8 But the fearful
Revelation21:9 And there came unto me one of the seven angels which had the seven vials full of the seven last plagues
Revelation21:10 And he carried me away in the spirit to a great and high mountain
Revelation21:11 Having the glory of God: and her light was like unto a stone most precious
Revelation21:12 And had a wall great and high
Revelation21:13 On the east three gates; on the north three gates; on the south three gates; and on the west three gates.
Revelation21:14 And the wall of the city had twelve foundations
Revelation21:15 And he that talked with me had a golden reed to measure the city
Revelation21:16 And the city lieth foursquare
Revelation21:17 And he measured the wall thereof
Revelation21:18 And the building of the wall of it was of jasper: and the city was pure gold
Revelation21:19 And the foundations of the wall of the city were garnished with all manner of precious stones. The first foundation was jasper; the second
Revelation21:20 The fifth
Revelation21:21 And the twelve gates were twelve pearls: every several gate was of one pearl: and the street of the city was pure gold
Revelation21:22 And I saw no temple therein: for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are the temple of it.
Revelation21:23 And the city had no need of the sun
Revelation21:24 And the nations of them which are saved shall walk in the light of it: and the kings of the earth do bring their glory and honour into it.
Revelation21:25 And the gates of it shall not be shut at all by day: for there shall be no night there.
Revelation21:26 And they shall bring the glory and honour of the nations into it.
Revelation21:27 And there shall in no wise enter into it any thing that defileth
